title: feat(tenancy/orm_filter): support scope-through-join for tables without workspace_id
description: unit, area, property_closure belong to a property via property_id; workspace boundary is enforced via property_workspace. Today these tables are left unregistered because the filter auto-injects workspace_id which doesn't exist on them. Extend orm_filter.py to either (a) walk the query's joins to verify property_workspace is joined with a workspace_id predicate, or (b) raise TenantFilterMissing when a bare SELECT hits an unregistered scope-through-join table. Discovered during cd-i6u.

title: feat(api/stays): iCal feeds, reservations, stay bundles, guest tokens
description:
## Problem / goal
Stays router — registration of iCal feeds, reservation listing
(read-mostly; rows come from the poller), stay bundles (the
generated turnover/welcome/deep-clean groupings), and the
short-lived guest tokens that gate the welcome surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Properties / areas /
  stays" (iCal + reservations + bundles + guest tokens
  subsections).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/CalendarsPage.tsx\` (iCal feeds),
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ReservationsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StayBundleDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/guest/web/src/pages/WelcomePage.tsx\` (consumes guest
  tokens).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/stays.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`stays\`.
- iCal endpoints: \`POST /ical-feeds\` accepts URL +
  property_id; \`POST /ical-feeds/{id}/poll\` triggers a manual
  poll (audited).
- Reservation listing supports the standard filters
  (\`?check_in_gte=\`, \`?status=\`, \`?property_id=\`).
- Stay bundle endpoint exposes regen
  (\`POST /stay-bundles/{id}/regenerate\`) — idempotent.
- Guest token issuance returns a short-lived (24-72h) opaque
  token + the public welcome URL. Issuance is audited; tokens
  cannot be listed (only issued / revoked).
- Welcome read endpoint accepts an anonymous bearer (the guest
  token); responds with curated read-only payload.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] iCal feed CRUD + manual poll
- [ ] Reservation listing with filters + cursor pagination
- [ ] Stay bundle regen idempotent
- [ ] Guest token issued + revocable; never re-listed
- [ ] Anonymous welcome read works only with a valid token
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`stays\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_stays.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_stays_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: sec(auth/signup): rate limits, disposable-email blocklist, CAPTCHA stub, reserved slugs
description:
## Problem / goal
Signup is the only fully open POST surface on the SaaS. It must
absorb scripted abuse without leaking through to billing
(workspace bloat) or to the LLM budget (downstream consequence). This
task hardens `p2.signup` against the §15 self-serve abuse vectors.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Self-serve abuse
  mitigations", §"Per-IP aggregate LLM spend cap".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-serve signup"
  reserved slugs + homoglyph guard.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/signup/SignupStart.tsx` (CAPTCHA
  placeholder).
- Phase 0 capability: `app.config.capabilities.signup_enabled`.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/auth/signup_abuse.py`:
  - `check_rate(ip, email)` — token bucket per IP and per email
    (Redis-backed in prod, in-memory in dev).
  - `is_disposable(email)` — bundled blocklist file under
    `app/auth/disposable_domains.txt` (cite source + refresh date in
    a header comment).
  - `check_captcha(token)` — verifies a Turnstile/HCaptcha token via
    `httpx`; in dev a fixed test token always passes.
  - `check_reserved_slug(slug)` — wraps `app.tenancy.slug.is_reserved`
    + `…is_homoglyph_collision` so callers get one-call coverage.
- Wire into `p2.signup` `POST /signup/start`. Failures return 429
  for rate, 422 for disposable email + bad CAPTCHA, 409 for slug
  rejections (already covered by signup itself — verify wiring).
- Audit: `audit.signup.rate_limited`, `…disposable_email`,
  `…captcha_failed`. Each row records `ip_hash` + `email_hash`,
  never raw values.
- Capabilities entry `captcha_required` (default true on
  `crew.day`, false on self-host) so self-hosters can disable it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Per-IP + per-email rate limits enforced; bypassable in tests via
      a clear fixture
- [ ] Disposable-email blocklist loaded once at startup; reload via
      SIGHUP test passes
- [ ] CAPTCHA stub returns 422 on failure; success path threads
      through cleanly
- [ ] Reserved-slug check matches the existing `app.tenancy.slug`
      single source of truth (no duplicate list)
- [ ] Audit rows carry only hashes, never raw IP/email
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover every refusal path

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_signup_abuse.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_signup_abuse_wired.py -xvs
```
Manual: hammer `/signup/start` with `hey` from a fixture IP; expect
429 once the bucket is empty.

title: feat(billing): organization CRUD (client/vendor/mixed)
description:
## Problem / goal
Organizations are the billing parties: clients (we invoice them),
vendors (they invoice us), or mixed (both). Foundation for rate
cards, work orders, quotes, vendor invoices, and the client portal.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"Design summary",
  §"\`organization\`", §"Starter rows".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/OrganizationsPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/organizations.py\`:
  \`OrganizationService(ctx)\` with \`create / get / list / update /
  archive\`.
- Validate \`kind ∈ {client,vendor,mixed}\`; cannot demote a mixed
  org to client if vendor invoices exist (and vice versa) — return
  422 with the conflict reason.
- API \`app/api/billing/organizations.py\`:
  - \`GET/POST /organizations\`
  - \`GET/PATCH /organizations/{id}\`
  - \`POST /organizations/{id}/archive\`
- Default-currency from workspace settings; tax_id is opaque text
  (per-jurisdiction validation out of scope).
- Audit \`billing.organization.created/updated/archived\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cannot delete an org with dependent rate_cards / work_orders
      / quotes / vendor_invoices — archive instead
- [ ] Kind transitions validated against existing artefacts
- [ ] Listing supports filter by kind + text search on name
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_organizations.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_organizations_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): web push subscription endpoints + VAPID key mgmt
description:
## Problem / goal
Browsers call \`POST /notifications/push/subscribe\` to register a
\`PushSubscription\` (endpoint + p256dh + auth). \`unsubscribe\`
removes it. VAPID public key is served so the SW can subscribe.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Channels"
  (web push), §"In-app messaging" → §"Agent-message delivery".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/components/ChatChannelsMeCard.tsx\` (the
  notification preferences surface).

## Implementation notes
- New router \`app/api/messaging/push.py\`:
  - \`GET /notifications/push/vapid-key\` → returns base64url public.
  - \`POST /notifications/push/subscribe\`
    \`{endpoint, keys:{p256dh,auth}, ua}\` → upsert by
    (\`user_id\`,\`endpoint\`).
  - \`POST /notifications/push/unsubscribe\` \`{endpoint}\`.
- VAPID keypair lives in workspace settings; rotated via CLI (out
  of scope here). Public key cached in-process, refreshed on
  settings change SSE.
- Validate endpoint origin against an allow-list of providers
  (Mozilla, Google, Apple) to dodge SSRF amplification.
- Audit \`messaging.push.subscribed\` / \`unsubscribed\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Subscribe is idempotent on (\`user_id\`, \`endpoint\`)
- [ ] Endpoint URL must be https + allow-listed host
- [ ] Unsubscribe returns 204 even if row was already gone
- [ ] VAPID public key endpoint cached for 5 min
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_push_token.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/messaging/test_push_endpoints.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(chat-gateway): agent_dispatch_sweep APScheduler job (restart safety net)
description:
## Problem / goal
Webhook + dispatcher race against worker restarts: a message can
land, the dispatcher process can die mid-handle, and
\`dispatched_to_agent_at\` stays NULL. Periodic sweep finds those
stragglers and re-publishes \`chat.message.received\` so the
dispatcher catches up.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"Routing" →
  §"Inbound" (restart-safety guarantees).

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/workers/chat_gateway/sweep.py\` scheduled by APScheduler
  every \`30s\`.
- Query: \`chat_message\` rows where \`channel.kind='chat_gateway'\`,
  \`dispatched_to_agent_at IS NULL\`, and \`created_at <
  now() - 30s\`.
- For each row, re-publish \`chat.message.received\` (the dispatcher
  is idempotent — see \`p13.gateway.dispatcher\`).
- Bound the batch (max 200 per tick) so a backlog doesn't pin one
  worker tick.
- Per-row try/except — one bad row mustn't kill the sweep.
- Metrics: emit a counter for \`sweep.requeued\`; gauge of pending
  count.
- Audit \`chat_gateway.sweep.requeued\` per message.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Picks up rows older than 30 s, never younger
- [ ] No double-dispatch when the original handler eventually
      catches up first (covered by dispatcher idempotency test)
- [ ] Per-row failure logged + audited; sweep continues
- [ ] Bounded batch size respected
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy-path + bounded-batch + per-row failure

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_sweep.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/chat_gateway/test_sweep_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(tasks): one-off task creation (no schedule)
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers, managers, and the NL-intake agent need a path to create a
single task that is not attached to a schedule. The quick-add modal
on `/today` and `/schedule`, the manager's "+ New task" button, and
`POST /api/v1/tasks/from_nl/commit` all land here.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Task kinds"
  (kind 1 — One-off), §"Self-created and personal tasks",
  §"Natural-language intake (agent)".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/components/NewTaskModal.tsx` for the
  quick-add shape; `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TodayPage.tsx`
  for the surface.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/oneoff.py`.
- Function: `create_oneoff(ctx: WorkspaceContext, payload:
  TaskCreate) -> TaskView`.
- Payload: `template_id` (optional — when present, copy `title`,
  `description_md`, `priority`, `photo_evidence`,
  `duration_minutes`, `linked_instruction_ids`,
  `inventory_consumption_json`, and seed checklist items via
  `expand_checklist_for_task(..., is_ad_hoc=True)`); explicit
  `title`, `description_md`, `priority`, `property_id`, `area_id`,
  `unit_id`, `expected_role_id`, `assigned_user_id` (optional —
  defer to `p5.assignment` if null), `scheduled_for_local`,
  `duration_minutes`, `photo_evidence`, `is_personal` (default
  `False` per §06 — only the quick-add UI sets `True` explicitly).
- Always sets `created_by = ctx.user_id`, `state = 'pending'` if
  `scheduled_for_local <= now`, else `'scheduled'` (the
  `scheduled → pending` worker handles the future case).
- Permission: caller must hold `tasks.create` (§05); enforced by
  the route layer, but assert it here too for defence-in-depth.
- When `assigned_user_id` is `null` and `expected_role_id` is set,
  delegate to `p5.assignment` to pick an assignee.
- Publish `task.created` (and `task.assigned` when applicable).
- Audit `task.create_oneoff` with the resolved fields.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Template-backed and template-less paths both work
- [ ] `is_personal=True` requires `assigned_user_id == ctx.user_id`
      (per §06 "personal" semantics)
- [ ] Past `scheduled_for_local` lands in `state = 'pending'`
      immediately
- [ ] Checklist seeding uses `is_ad_hoc=True`
- [ ] Audit + events emitted
- [ ] Unit tests cover all branches above

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_oneoff.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): TaskTemplate CRUD service
description:
## Problem / goal
A `task_template` is the reusable definition that schedules and stay
lifecycle rules materialise from. Without a CRUD service there is no
way to author "Weekly kitchen deep clean" or "Standard turnover".

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Task template",
  §"Checklist template shape", §"Evidence policy inheritance";
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"task_template".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx`
  (renders `name`, `role`, `priority`, `photo_evidence`,
  `duration_minutes`, `property_scope`, `checklist`).
- **Boundary** (`docs/specs/01-architecture.md`): service lives in
  `app/domain/tasks/templates.py`; only this service writes
  `task_template` rows.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/templates.py`.
- Pydantic DTOs: `TaskTemplateCreate`, `TaskTemplateUpdate`,
  `TaskTemplateView`. Fields: `name`, `description_md`, `role_id`,
  `duration_minutes`, `property_scope`
  (`any | one | listed`), `listed_property_ids`, `area_scope`,
  `listed_area_ids`, `checklist_template_json` (validated as a list
  of items — see `p5.checklist-template`), `photo_evidence`
  (`disabled | optional | required`), `linked_instruction_ids`,
  `priority` (`low | normal | high | urgent`),
  `inventory_consumption_json`, `llm_hints_md`.
- All operations take `ctx: WorkspaceContext` and resolve
  `workspace_id` from it — never from the request payload.
- Validate `property_scope` consistency: `listed` requires non-empty
  `listed_property_ids`; `one` requires exactly one; `any` requires
  empty.
- Same shape rule for `area_scope` / `listed_area_ids`.
- `delete()` is soft (sets `deleted_at`); refuse delete when any
  active `schedule.template_id` or active `stay_lifecycle_rule
  .template_id` references the template — return a typed
  `TemplateInUseError` with the offending ids.
- Every mutation calls `audit.record(...)` with the actor and the
  before/after diff (`task_template.create | update | delete`).
- No business logic about scheduling here — that's `p5.schedule.crud`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Create / read / list (with `q`, `role_id`, `deleted` filters) /
      update / soft-delete all working
- [ ] Returns 422 with typed errors on scope inconsistency
- [ ] Returns 409 with `TemplateInUseError` when delete is blocked
- [ ] Audit row written on every mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy path + every validation branch
- [ ] WorkspaceContext is the only source of `workspace_id`

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_templates.py -xvs
uv run mypy app/domain/tasks/templates.py
```

title: feat(domain/stays): ical_feed registration + URL validation + provider auto-detect
description:
## Problem / goal
Register an iCal feed against a property/unit. Validate the URL
synchronously (scheme, SSRF host resolution, reachability probe),
detect the provider from the URL host pattern, and persist with the
feed disabled until the first probe succeeds.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"iCal feed"
  (fields, polling, **SSRF guard**, supported providers).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"ical_feed".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Stays/Calendar feeds tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (ICAL_FEEDS).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/stays/ical_service.py\` —
  \`register_feed\`, \`update_feed\`, \`disable_feed\`,
  \`delete_feed\`, \`list_feeds\`, \`probe_feed\`.
- URL validation port: \`app/ports/ical_validator.py\` with an HTTPS
  scheme check, IP-resolution check (reject loopback / RFC 1918 /
  link-local / multicast), 2 MB / 10 s probe with same-origin
  redirects only. Implementation (\`adapters/ical/validator.py\`)
  reuses the SSRF helper used elsewhere.
- Provider auto-detect from host: \`*.airbnb.com\` → airbnb,
  \`*.vrbo.com\` / expedia → vrbo, \`*.booking.com\` → booking,
  \`*.calendar.google.com\` → gcal, else generic.
- URL stored envelope-encrypted (per spec §15) — depend on the
  storage adapter for that.
- \`probe_feed\` re-runs validation on demand and updates
  \`last_polled_at\` / \`last_error\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] HTTPS-only enforced at registration AND at probe
- [ ] Private/loopback IPs rejected at both validation and fetch
- [ ] Provider auto-detected; manual override allowed
- [ ] URL persisted envelope-encrypted (no plaintext at rest)
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Unit tests for each provider + each SSRF rejection path

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_ical_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/adapters/ical/test_validator.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_ical_registration.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(time/shifts): auto-open on occurrence start, auto-close on completion
description:
## Problem / goal
For schedules where the worker's "shift" is implied by the task itself
(housekeeper arrives = shift starts; checklist done = shift ends),
auto-open and auto-close shifts driven by occurrence lifecycle events.
Configurable per template / property.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Bookings"
  on time-sources; `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  occurrence states; `docs/specs/00-event-bus.md`.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx` shows
  shift status driven by occurrence cards.

## Implementation notes
- Subscribe to event-bus topics from Phase 0:
  - `task.occurrence.started` → call `shifts.open_shift(..., source='occurrence')`
    if a config flag (`auto_shift_from_occurrence`) is set on the
    property OR the template.
  - `task.occurrence.completed` → close any matching open shift
    (same user, same property) opened from this occurrence
    (link via new column `shift.source_occurrence_id` nullable).
- Migration `0018_shift_source_occurrence` adds the FK column +
  index.
- Idempotency: re-delivery of the same event must not open a
  duplicate shift (lookup by `source_occurrence_id`).
- A worker who already has an open manual shift on the same property
  is a no-op (audit a `shift.auto_open_skipped` row).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Occurrence start opens a shift when config enabled
- [ ] Occurrence completion closes the auto-opened shift
- [ ] Duplicate event delivery is idempotent
- [ ] Manual shift in flight blocks auto-open (with audit)
- [ ] Config flag respected at both template and property level

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_shift_from_occurrence.py -xvs
```

title: feat(db/stays): reservation + ical_feed + stay_bundle
description:
## Problem / goal
Stays are the bridge between external calendars and internal task
generation. Reservations drive turnover tasks.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"reservation",
  §"ical_feed", §"stay_bundle",
  \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/stays/models.py\`:
  - \`IcalFeed\`: id, workspace_id, property_id, url, provider
    (airbnb|vrbo|booking|custom), last_polled_at, last_etag, enabled.
  - \`Reservation\`: id, workspace_id, property_id, ical_feed_id (nullable),
    external_uid, check_in (aware), check_out (aware), guest_name,
    guest_count, status (\`scheduled|checked_in|completed|cancelled\`),
    source (enum), raw_summary, raw_description, created_at.
  - \`StayBundle\`: id, workspace_id, reservation_id, kind
    (\`turnover|welcome|deep_clean\`), tasks_json (list of task
    template refs).
- Alembic migration \`0007_stays\`.
- Register all tables in tenancy registry.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tables present, indexes on (\`property_id\`, \`check_in\`)
- [ ] Unique index on (\`ical_feed_id\`, \`external_uid\`) so re-poll
      is idempotent
- [ ] Unit tests cover CRUD + upsert behaviour

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_stays.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(cli/codegen): build _surface.json from openapi.json + _exclusions.yaml
description:
## Problem / goal
The CLI command tree is **derived** from the API surface — every public
REST resource gets `crewday <resource> <verb>` for free, with an
exclusion list for things that don't make sense on the CLI (file
uploads via raw multipart, browser-only routes, etc.). A build-time
generator reads `openapi.json`, applies the exclusions, and emits
`_surface.json` (committed to the repo) consumed by `_runtime.py`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Codegen pipeline",
  §"Exclusions", §"Surface JSON shape".
- **Source**: `app/api/openapi.json` (Phase 14 produces this).
- **Convention**: codegen runs at build time, not at CLI startup;
  `_surface.json` is committed so users get a deterministic command
  tree without needing the API on disk.

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_codegen.py` — script + `python -m crewday._codegen`
  entrypoint. Reads `app/api/openapi.json`, walks paths, groups by
  tag → resource, maps HTTP verb → Click subcommand
  (`GET /tasks` → `crewday tasks list`, `GET /tasks/{id}` →
  `crewday tasks get`, `POST` → `create`, `PATCH` → `update`,
  `DELETE` → `delete`, `POST /…/close` → `tasks close`, etc.).
- `cli/crewday/_exclusions.yaml` — list of `operationId`s and path
  patterns the CLI must not generate (e.g. browser-only `/login/*`,
  raw blob uploads — composites cover those).
- Output: `cli/crewday/_surface.json` — array of command specs:
  `{group, name, http: {method, path}, params, body_schema_ref,
   response_schema_ref, summary, idempotent}`. Stable sort.
- CI step: `python -m crewday._codegen --check` re-runs and fails
  if `_surface.json` would change without being regenerated.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `python -m crewday._codegen` regenerates `_surface.json`
- [ ] `_exclusions.yaml` honoured (excluded ops never appear)
- [ ] Stable, deterministic output (idempotent re-run, sorted keys)
- [ ] `--check` mode exits non-zero on drift
- [ ] Unit tests cover verb→command mapping + exclusion logic

## Test plan
```bash
uv run python -m crewday._codegen
uv run python -m crewday._codegen --check
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_codegen.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/manager): approvals queue — task amendments + HITL agent actions
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager decides every approval request the workspace generates: worker self-amendments above threshold, agent actions paused for human-in-the-loop, expense flags, etc. The queue is the manager's compliance trail.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/approvals` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/approvals, POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/approve|reject.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The mock surfaces `risk` tone via APPROVAL_RISK_TONE — keep that mapping; do not recolour by category.
- HITL agent approvals carry an `agent_run_id` link back to the LLM trace (§11) — render it as the mock does.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/approvals`.

title: feat(tasks): generate_task_occurrences worker job
description:
## Problem / goal
The hourly worker that walks every active schedule, expands its
RRULE/RDATE/EXDATE inside the workspace horizon, and inserts a `task`
row per new occurrence. Idempotent (re-runs do not duplicate).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Generation"
  (steps 1–5), §"Pause vs active range";
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"occurrence" / §"task" rows.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx` for the
  workspace timeline; `mocks/app/mock_data.py` for occurrence shape
  (`assignee`, `state`, `starts_at`, `ends_at`, `checklist_items`).
- Boundary: lives in `app/workers/tasks/generator.py`; uses
  `app/domain/tasks/schedules.py` reads only — no direct table writes
  outside its own DAO.

## Implementation notes
- Entry point: `generate_task_occurrences(ctx: WorkspaceContext, *,
  now: datetime | None = None) -> GenerationReport`.
- Horizon: workspace setting `tasks.generation_horizon_days`
  (default 30; §06 says "now + 30 days").
- For each schedule where `paused_at IS NULL`, `deleted_at IS NULL`,
  `active_from <= today`, and (`active_until IS NULL OR active_until
  >= today`):
  1. Compute next occurrences in property timezone via
     `dateutil.rrule.rrulestr(schedule.rrule, dtstart=...)`.
  2. Apply `exdate_local`, then add `rdate_local`.
  3. For each candidate `(schedule_id, scheduled_for_local)` not
     already present in `tasks`, materialise a row with
     `originally_scheduled_for = scheduled_for_local`,
     `state = 'scheduled'`, fields copied from template + schedule
     defaults, and call `expand_checklist_for_task(...)` from
     `p5.checklist-template` to seed `task_checklist_item` rows.
  4. Skip days covered by an active `property_closure` matching the
     task's `(property_id, unit_id)`; record a
     `schedules.skipped_for_closure` audit event (no task row).
  5. Call the assignment service (`p5.assignment`) on each new task.
- **Idempotency**: enforce a partial unique index on `tasks
  (schedule_id, scheduled_for_local) WHERE schedule_id IS NOT NULL`
  in the migration; the worker uses `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` and
  reports skipped duplicates in `GenerationReport.skipped_duplicate`.
- Publish `task.created` (one event per new row) on the bus from
  Phase 0 events.
- Schedule via the APScheduler instance from §16 — hourly tick.
- Audit summary row per run: `schedules.generation_tick` with the
  count of new tasks, skipped duplicates, skipped-for-closure days.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Two consecutive runs over the same window create rows once
- [ ] Paused / past-active-until / deleted schedules are skipped
- [ ] Property closures suppress occurrences and emit the audit
      event
- [ ] `task.created` published per new row
- [ ] Unit tests cover the idempotency invariant and an RRULE that
      crosses a DST boundary in the property tz
- [ ] WorkspaceContext threaded through; never reads tenancy from
      the env

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_generator.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tasks_generator_run.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/manager): asset types catalogue with default action templates
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager curates the asset type taxonomy and default recurring action templates (annual service, weekly check) that propagate to new assets of the type.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetTypesPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/asset_types` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/asset_types, POST/PATCH /api/v1/asset_types.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetTypesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.


## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/asset_types`.

title: feat(ops): structured logs + /metrics endpoint + OpenTelemetry trace hook
description:
## Problem / goal
Operators running crewday past trial size need the standard ops
trifecta: structured logs (already partly via `structlog`),
a Prometheus-compatible `/metrics` endpoint with workspace-tagged
counters, and an OpenTelemetry trace exporter wired to a
configurable collector. None of this is mandatory for self-host;
all of it is mandatory for the managed SaaS.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe D —
  Managed multi-tenant deployment" (where the observability stack
  matters most), §"Recipe A/B" (still benefit from logs + a
  scrape endpoint).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Logging and
  redaction" — the redactor must run before metrics labels (no
  per-user PII in label values).

## Implementation notes
- Logging: extend `app/util/logging.py` to add a request-id
  processor (per-request UUID, propagated to subprocess workers)
  and a workspace-id processor sourced from
  `WorkspaceContext`. Format JSON, one line per record.
- Metrics: new `app/observability/metrics.py` exposes a
  `prometheus_client` registry. Counters / histograms:
  - `crewday_http_requests_total{workspace_id, route, status}`
  - `crewday_http_request_duration_seconds{route}`
  - `crewday_llm_calls_total{workspace_id, capability, status}`
  - `crewday_llm_cost_usd_total{workspace_id, model}`
  - `crewday_worker_jobs_total{job, status}`
  - `crewday_worker_job_duration_seconds{job}`
  Endpoint `GET /metrics` is gated by a deployment setting
  (`settings.metrics_enabled`, default `true` in SaaS, `false`
  in self-host). When on, accessible only from
  `CREWDAY_METRICS_ALLOW_CIDR` (default `127.0.0.0/8` plus
  Tailscale ranges). Workspace label values pass through
  `redact()` to ensure no PII slips in (workspace slug is fine;
  user emails are not).
- Tracing: optional `opentelemetry-sdk` exporter targeting an
  OTLP endpoint configured via `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`.
  Auto-instrument FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, httpx. Disabled when no
  endpoint is configured.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every log line is JSON with `request_id` and (when in scope)
      `workspace_id` keys
- [ ] `GET /metrics` returns Prometheus format with the listed
      counters under load
- [ ] Workspace label values never include PII (asserted by a
      redaction round-trip test)
- [ ] `/metrics` returns 404 when disabled, 403 from a non-allowed
      CIDR
- [ ] Setting `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` produces traces in a
      local Jaeger container (manual verification step documented)
- [ ] No measurable overhead on the hot path (< 2% under load)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/observability/test_metrics.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/observability/test_metrics_endpoint.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/observability/test_tracing.py -xvs
```

title: feat(cli/client): httpx async client — token auth, retries, streaming, idempotency, pagination
description:
## Problem / goal
Single shared HTTP client used by every generated command and every
composite override. Handles bearer/token auth from the active profile,
retries with backoff for transient failures, request streaming
(downloads), idempotency-key plumbing, and cursor pagination helpers.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"HTTP client", §"Retries",
  §"Streaming", §"Pagination".
- **API**: `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Auth", §"Idempotency",
  §"Pagination", §"Errors".

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_client.py`:
  - `class CrewdayClient` wrapping `httpx.AsyncClient` with sync
    facade for Click commands.
  - Base URL + token loaded from `_config.py` active profile.
  - Default headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`,
    `User-Agent: crewday-cli/<version>`,
    `X-Workspace: <slug>` when present.
  - Retries: 3 attempts on connect-error / 502/503/504 with
    exponential backoff + jitter; never retry non-idempotent verbs
    unless an explicit `Idempotency-Key` is set.
  - Streaming: `download(path, dest)` honours `Range` resume.
  - Pagination: `iterate(path, params)` async generator returning
    pages until `next_cursor` is empty.
  - Error mapping: structured `ApiError(status, code, message,
    details)` raised on non-2xx.
- All requests log to the structured logger from Phase 0 with the
  correlation id echoed back from the server.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Token auth uses the active profile's token
- [ ] Retries do NOT clobber non-idempotent calls
- [ ] `iterate()` follows cursors; `download()` resumes via Range
- [ ] `ApiError` carries server `code` + `message` + `details`
- [ ] Unit tests cover retry, pagination, error mapping (use
      `pytest-httpx`)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_client.py -xvs
uv run mypy --strict cli/crewday/_client.py
```

title: feat(billing): client portal data API (org-scoped read: hours, invoices, portfolio)
description:
## Problem / goal
Read-only API the client portal SPA calls. Scoped to the client's
own org: properties they own (portfolio), hours billed
(billable hours), and invoices/quotes addressed to them.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\`
  §"Client surface (client login)", §"Acceptance authority",
  §"Redactions".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/PortfolioPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/BillableHoursPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/InvoicesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New router \`app/api/client/portal.py\` mounted under
  \`/client\`. Auth: client role grant only — workspace + org
  bound to the session.
- Endpoints (all org-scoped via \`role_grant.scope_organization_id\`):
  - \`GET /client/portfolio\` — properties owned by client
  - \`GET /client/billable-hours\` — accrued hours grouped by
    work_order + property + week
  - \`GET /client/invoices\` — issued invoices (status, total, PDF
    link)
  - \`GET /client/quotes\` — pending/sent quotes with accept link
- Apply spec §"Redactions": strip staff names, strip cost
  components, only show client-facing rates.
- Cache-friendly (\`Cache-Control: private, max-age=30\`).
- Audit \`client_portal.viewed\` with the slug accessed (no PII).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Client A cannot see client B's data even with crafted query
      params (tested with two clients in the same workspace)
- [ ] Redaction rules enforced (staff names absent, no internal
      cost columns)
- [ ] All four endpoints return paginated, sorted, deterministic
      data
- [ ] Unit + API + authz boundary tests

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/client/test_portal.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/client/test_portal_authz.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/client/test_portal_endpoints.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(leave): leave-request CRUD with state machine
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers need to request time off; managers need to approve/reject;
either side may cancel before the start date. The state machine is
`pending → approved | rejected | cancelled`, with no back-edges except
explicit reopen.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` (worker self-service),
  `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Leave"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"leave"; `docs/specs/12-api.md` §"/leaves".
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeLeavesPage.tsx`
  (worker view of own leaves), `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/LeavesInboxPage.tsx`
  (manager queue).
- DB tables in `p1.time` (Leave model already exists).

## Implementation notes
- New service `app/services/leave/service.py` taking
  `WorkspaceContext`; methods `create`, `cancel_own`, `update_dates`
  (only while pending), `list_for_user`, `list_for_workspace`.
- `create` defaults `status = pending`, `requester_user_id = ctx.user_id`.
- `cancel_own` valid in `pending` or `approved` (latter requires
  the start date to be in the future); flips to `cancelled`.
- Audit every transition (old → new status, actor, reason).
- Validation: `starts_at < ends_at`; both align to whole days in the
  worker's timezone (use the tz utility from `p0.util`).
- Approval / rejection logic stays in `p4.leave.approval` — this task
  ships the CRUD, the cancel-own path, and the state-machine guards.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Workers can `POST /me/leaves` and see their own leaves only.
- [ ] Managers can `GET /workspaces/{ws}/leaves?status=pending`.
- [ ] State transitions enforced: `pending → cancelled` allowed by
      requester or manager; non-pending transitions in this task
      reject with 409.
- [ ] Updating dates while `pending` rewrites starts_at/ends_at and
      audits old/new values.
- [ ] Audit rows for create / update / cancel.
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy path + each illegal transition.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_service_leave.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_leave.py -xvs
```

(open / invite / closed), rate limits, passkey attestation policy,
default workspace budget cap. The capability registry is also
read out here.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is

and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves

mock at every supported breakpoint.

- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →

title: feat(auth/signup): self-serve signup (email → magic → passkey → slug)
description:
## Problem / goal
Open self-service signup creates a workspace + first user atomically
when `settings.signup_enabled` is true. The flow is the public face of
the SaaS; correctness here is load-bearing for everything downstream.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-serve signup"
  (full step list, reserved slugs, homoglyph guard, grace period).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Self-serve abuse
  mitigations" (covered separately by `p2.signup.abuse`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/signup/*` (full storyboard already
  exists).
- Phase 1 tables: `workspace`, `user`, `user_workspace`,
  `permission_group`, `permission_group_member`, `role_grant`.

## Implementation notes
- Endpoints (all bare-host, `tenant_agnostic()`):
  - `POST /signup/start` `{email, desired_slug}` → magic link sent;
    `signup_attempt` row keyed by `(email, desired_slug)`,
    15-minute TTL.
  - `GET /signup/verify?token=…` → consumes the magic link, atomically
    inserts the new `workspace`, `user`, four system permission groups,
    `permission_group_member` row in `owners@<ws>`, and a
    `role_grant(scope_kind=workspace, grant_role=manager)`. Issues
    a signup-scoped session cookie (no `__Host-crewday_session` yet).
  - `POST /signup/passkey/start` + `/finish` reuse `p2.passkey.register`
    via the bootstrap path; on success the session is upgraded to a
    full `__Host-crewday_session`.
- Gate every endpoint with `app.config.capabilities.signup_enabled`
  — when false, return 404 (not 403) so the surface is invisible.
- Validate `desired_slug` against the §02 regex + the reserved-slug
  blocklist constant in `app.tenancy.slug` (do **not** duplicate the
  list — import the single source of truth).
- 409 codes: `slug_taken`, `slug_reserved`, `slug_in_grace_period`,
  `slug_homoglyph_collision` (homoglyph guard logic in
  `app.tenancy.slug.normalise_for_collision`).
- All inserts wrapped in one transaction; rollback on any failure.
- Audit on every step: `audit.signup.requested`, `…verified`,
  `…completed`, `…aborted`. `actor_kind = "system"` until the
  passkey ceremony succeeds.
- `signup_gc` background job (APScheduler) prunes 1-hour-old
  workspaces with no user.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All inserts in one transaction; partial failures roll back fully
- [ ] `signup_enabled = false` → 404 on every signup route
- [ ] Reserved + homoglyph + grace-period rejections covered by tests
- [ ] First user becomes a member of `owners@<ws>` and holds a
      `manager` role grant on the workspace scope
- [ ] Quotas seeded at 10 % of free-tier (verification_state=
      `email_verified`)
- [ ] `signup_gc` removes orphaned workspaces after 1h
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DB backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_signup_full_flow.py -xvs
```
Manual: dev container with `signup_enabled=true`, walk
`/signup` end-to-end with Playwright; confirm `bd /w/<slug>/today`
loads after the ceremony.

title: test(api): schemathesis contract tests against /openapi.json
description:
## Problem / goal
Schemathesis property-tests every endpoint declared in
`openapi.json`: status codes match the spec, response schemas
validate, idempotency keys are honoured, ETag round-trips behave.
This catches drift between the OpenAPI document and the actual
handler.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"API contract"
  (`schemathesis run --checks all ./openapi.json` against a live
  dev server seeded with fixture data; custom hooks enforce
  Authorization, idempotency, ETag), §"Quality gates"
  (`schemathesis` is a PR gate).

## Implementation notes
- Add `schemathesis` to dev deps.
- New `scripts/schemathesis_run.sh`:
  1. Boots the app under `pytest`'s `app_client` fixture (same
     factories as the integration suite seed).
  2. Runs `schemathesis run --checks all
     http://localhost:<port>/openapi.json --data-generation-method=
     positive,negative --hypothesis-max-examples=20`.
  3. Custom hooks (`tests/contract/hooks.py`):
     - assert `Authorization: Bearer …` is present on every
       non-public path
     - assert the `Idempotency-Key` header round-trips: a second
       call returns the cached response
     - assert `ETag` round-trip on GET → If-None-Match → 304
- CI runs schemathesis once per PR (the §17 < 5 min budget).
- Findings are surfaced via JUnit XML for the GitHub Action
  summary.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `make schemathesis` runs end-to-end and reports zero unhandled
      failures on the seeded fixture set
- [ ] Authorization, idempotency, ETag custom checks fire
- [ ] CI step `schemathesis` blocks merge on failure
- [ ] A deliberately broken handler (wrong status code) is caught
- [ ] Generated cases include negative inputs (bad enums, missing
      required fields)

## Test plan
```bash
make openapi
bash scripts/schemathesis_run.sh
```

title: feat(web/public): GuestPage — tokenized guest welcome page
description:
## Problem / goal
Guest-facing welcome page reached via tokenised URL (§03 guest tokens; §07 guest welcome). Surfaces wifi, house rules, checkout instructions, and the per-stay guest checklist + assets the manager has chosen to expose. Read-only and printable; date formatting matches the legacy Jinja template (`%a %d %b`).

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/public/GuestPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/public/GuestPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/public/GuestPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `Loading` (`@/components/common`), `useQuery` over `/api/v1/guest/{token}`. Types: `Asset`, `ChecklistItem`, `Property`, `Stay`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/guest/{token}` returning `{ stay, property, guest_checklist, guest_assets }`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/public/GuestPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/public/GuestPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Token validation server-side — invalid/expired token must render a clean 'link expired' state (mock has the helper `fmtDayMonth` for the expiry footer).
- Preserve the print-friendly layout exactly — guests print this page.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-public/GuestPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-public/GuestPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-public/GuestPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/public/GuestPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: chore(deploy): Dockerfile single + docker-compose dev/prod profiles
description:
## Problem / goal
Make the app runnable as a single SQLite container and as a compose
stack (Postgres + Caddy + MinIO) from day 1. No code depends on
deployment mode — same image everywhere.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md\`
  §"Single-container", §"Compose", \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\`
  §"Environments".
- Mocks compose at \`mocks/docker-compose.yml\` is the reference for
  port layout / Caddy config.

## Implementation notes
- \`deploy/single/Dockerfile\`: multi-stage — stage 1 builds
  \`app/web/dist\` (Node 22); stage 2 \`python:3.12-slim\` copying
  \`app/\`, \`cli/\`, \`dist/\`, \`migrations/\`. Entrypoint runs
  \`alembic upgrade head\` then \`uvicorn app.main:create_app
  --factory\`.
- \`deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml\`: services \`web\`, \`worker\`
  (external scheduler), \`postgres\`, \`caddy\`, \`minio\`, \`mailhog\`
  (dev-only profile).
- \`deploy/compose/Caddyfile\` terminates TLS, proxies to web,
  loopback-binds the admin surface.
- Healthcheck: curl \`/healthz\`.
- Web only binds on \`127.0.0.1\` by default; compose exposes via
  Caddy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`docker build -f deploy/single/Dockerfile -t crewday:test .\`
      succeeds
- [ ] \`docker run crewday:test\` starts and \`curl localhost:8000/healthz\`
      returns 200
- [ ] \`docker compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml up\`
      brings the stack up
- [ ] Caddy serves over HTTPS in the staging profile

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
docker build -f deploy/single/Dockerfile -t crewday:test .
docker run --rm -p 8000:8000 crewday:test &
sleep 5
curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8000/healthz
docker compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml up -d
\`\`\`

title: fix(tests): test-pollution between integration DB tests and tenancy ORM-filter unit tests
description:
After cd-od3 (capabilities) landed, running the full `uv run pytest` suite began exposing a latent test-pollution bug in `tests/unit/test_tenancy_orm_filter.py`.

Symptom: one of the tenancy join/update tests fails with `DID NOT RAISE TenantFilterMissing` or `assert 'foo.workspace_id = ?' in flattened` when the integration capabilities tests run first. The tenancy hook verifiably fires (verified by instrumenting `_do_orm_execute` with print); `_collect_scoped_targets` returns the scoped tables; `ctx` is set; and the rewritten statement with the WHERE clause is assigned back to `orm_execute_state.statement`. Yet the SQL that reaches `sql_capture` lacks the predicate.

Heisenbug characteristic: adding `print()` statements inside `_do_orm_execute` makes the failure disappear — strong indicator of a timing / race / order-of-events issue inside SQLAlchemy's internal execution pipeline rather than a straightforward cache-hit.

Diagnosis attempts that did NOT fix it:
- `engine._compiled_cache.clear()` at module teardown.
- `engine.dispose()` at module teardown.
- Forcing `.execution_options(compiled_cache=None)` on the rewritten statement (broke 10 unrelated tests).
- Rearranging test order inside `TestRefreshSettings`.
- Clearing `sys.modules` caches.

Repro:
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_capabilities.py tests/unit/test_tenancy_orm_filter.py
# => 1-2 failed (test_join_two_scoped_tables_filters_both, test_self_join_with_alias_filters_both_sides,
#    test_update_scoped_without_ctx_raises, or a sibling join/update/alias test)
```

Narrowing: requires 8+ cumulative `Session` operations against the integration engine's DB connection before the tenancy tests run. Running any 7-or-fewer subset first is fine.

Follow-up candidates:
1. Hoist the tenancy test's `engine` fixture to session scope (avoids per-test engine rebuild, which is the only piece of state the integration conftest and the unit file share via SQLAlchemy internals).
2. Switch the tenancy test to a fresh process via `pytest-forked` for the affected tests.
3. Investigate SQLAlchemy's `_compile_state_attrs` or `_has_events` internal caches — the heisenbug characteristic matches an execution-time assertion that's racing with a cache.
4. Long-term fix: move tenant filter injection into a `before_compile` / `SchemaEvents` hook rather than `do_orm_execute`, so the predicate is part of the original compile step (and therefore cannot be silently bypassed).

Capabilities (cd-od3) unit + integration tests themselves are stable in isolation and with the full suite — they simply act as a reliable reproducer for this latent regression.
close_reason: Fixed: WeakSet idempotency for install_tenant_filter + defensive rewrite of _collect_scoped_targets

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Additional selfreview investigation (cd-njs autofix, 2026-04-19):

Narrowed the failure: _collect_scoped_targets() returns only ['foo_with_bar'] for the join when the flake triggers, instead of ['foo', 'foo_with_bar']. The _walk_from recursion mis-handles the _ORMJoin's left side somehow — when we add ANY side-effect (print, sys.stderr.write) inside _walk_from or after _collect_scoped_targets, the correct two-table result is returned and the test passes.

Reconfirmed tried-and-failed fixes:
- .list() wrapping around _collect_scoped_targets — still fails with missing foo filter
- Using and_(*predicates) single .where() call instead of chained .where() — still fails (because scoped only has one target)
- @pytest.fixture(scope='session') on engine — causes 3 different test failures
- Explicit eng.dispose() in engine teardown — no effect
- new_stmt._generate_cache_key() / new_stmt.compile() before assignment — 1-2 unrelated tests break

Root cause sketch: get_final_froms() on the _ORMJoin returns a Join object whose left AnnotatedTable is not getting walked on a cold path. Adding a side-effect warms some internal state. Strong candidate: SQLAlchemy's ORM annotation deserialization lazily materializes the Join's .left property only after a compile / visit, which the print triggers via getattr.

Workarounds worth trying next:
- Use stmt.columns_clause_froms (returns implied FROMs from columns) instead of get_final_froms, then walk joins manually from stmt._from_obj.
- Use sqlalchemy.inspect() or visitors.traverse() to walk the FROM tree instead of get_final_froms (which triggers a full ORM compile that interferes).
- Call stmt.froms first (deprecated, but may not trigger the same compile path).

Leaving cd-3yhd OPEN because the fix is non-trivial — probably needs a rewrite of _collect_scoped_targets to avoid get_final_froms entirely. The capabilities (cd-od3) tests ship as the reliable reproducer.

title: feat(web/admin): DashboardPage — deployment-wide stats landing
description:
## Problem / goal
The deployment admin (operator) lands on `/admin/dashboard` after
log-in and needs a single-pane view of deployment health: 30-day LLM
spend, workspace count, paused / stressed workspaces, and the most
recent deployment-scope audit entries. This is the `AdminLayout` home
page — without it, every admin nav target is an island.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin (bare host,
  deployment-admin grant only)" → `/admin/dashboard` — operator
  landing page: deployment health, recent audit, usage.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/DashboardPage.tsx`.
- **Components used**: `DeskPage`, `StatCard`, `ProgressBar`, `Chip`,
  `Loading` from `@/components/common`; `Link` from
  `react-router-dom`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /admin/api/v1/usage/summary`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/workspaces`, `GET /admin/api/v1/audit`.
- **Types** (from `@/types/api`): `AdminUsageSummary`,
  `AdminWorkspaceRow`, `AuditEntry`.
- **Layout**: mounted under `AdminLayout` (no workspace prefix) per
  §14 "Admin" route block.

## Implementation notes
- Copy the mock TSX verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/admin/DashboardPage.tsx`.
- Replace the mock fixture handlers with real API calls via the
  shared TanStack Query client + `fetchJson` (Phase 16
  `p16.api-client`). Query keys come from `qk.adminUsageSummary()`,
  `qk.adminWorkspaces()`, `qk.adminAudit()`.
- Wire SSE invalidation: subscribe to
  `EventSource('/admin/api/v1/events')` in `AdminLayout` and
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: qk.adminUsageSummary() })`
  on `usage.summary.updated`, `qk.adminWorkspaces()` on
  `admin.workspace.changed`, `qk.adminAudit()` on `audit.appended`
  (filter to `scope_kind == 'deployment'`).
- Permission gate: route returns 404 from the API for non-admins
  (§14); render the "you don't have access" card defined in §14
  "Admin surface" when `GET /admin/api/v1/me` 404s instead of
  bouncing to /login.
- Preserve every CSS class, `grid--stats`, `Chip` tone, breakpoint,
  and copy string from the mock — visual parity is the test.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page mounts at `/admin/dashboard`; `/admin` redirects there.
- [ ] All three queries load and render with mock-equivalent layout.
- [ ] Loading and error states use `<DeskPage>` shell with `<Loading />`
      / "Failed to load." identical to the mock.
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes summary, workspaces, audit on the
      relevant events (verified with a manual emitter).
- [ ] Non-admin user sees the "ask your operator" card, never `/login`
      bounce.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock at desktop breakpoint passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_dashboard.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-dashboard.spec.ts
# Manual: visual parity against `http://127.0.0.1:8100/admin/dashboard`.
```

title: feat(adapters/mail): SMTP mailer impl
description:
## Problem / goal
Magic links, digests, invoices go over email. Adapter is SMTP for v1
(MailHog in dev; real provider in prod).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\`,
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters/mail".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/mail/smtp.py\`: \`SMTPMailer\` implementing \`Mailer\`.
  Uses \`aiosmtplib\` or \`smtplib\` in a thread executor (pick one and
  justify in a short docstring).
- Envelope sender from \`settings.smtp_*\`; return-path matching
  \`bounce+<token>@\` so future bounce webhooks can correlate.
- Retries: 3 attempts w/ exponential backoff.
- Dev mode uses MailHog on \`localhost:1025\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Unit tests with MailHog (via testcontainer or its Docker service)
      send a message and assert headers
- [ ] Retry path triggers on transient SMTP errors
- [ ] Unit tests cover success + retry + permanent failure

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
docker compose -f tests/docker-compose.test.yml up -d mailhog
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: chore(ci): GitHub Actions \u2014 lint, type, test, import-linter
description:
## Problem / goal
Every PR must be blocked from merging until lint, type, unit tests, and
the import-linter boundary gate pass. Wire this up now so downstream
tasks cannot regress it.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md\` §"CI gates",
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Enforcement".
- Matrix: Python 3.12 + 3.13; SQLite + Postgres 15 (via
  \`services: postgres\`).
- Cache \`~/.cache/uv\` keyed on \`uv.lock\`.

## Implementation notes
- Create \`.github/workflows/ci.yml\` with jobs:
  - \`lint\`  — \`uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .\`
  - \`type\`  — \`uv run mypy app cli\`
  - \`boundaries\` — \`uv run lint-imports\` (config from the
    import-linter task; can start as a placeholder that always passes
    and be made strict by the import-linter task)
  - \`unit\`  — \`uv run pytest tests/unit\` on SQLite
  - \`integration\` — \`uv run pytest tests/integration\` on both
    SQLite and Postgres via service container
- Use \`actions/setup-python@v5\`, \`astral-sh/setup-uv@v3\`.
- Fail the workflow on any red job.
- Gate merges in branch protection (repo admin action; call it out in
  the task comment).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] PR against main runs all five jobs and they all pass on a clean
      tree
- [ ] A deliberately broken \`app/util/_bad.py\` makes \`lint\` fail
- [ ] A type violation makes \`type\` fail
- [ ] The workflow uses the matrix and completes in under 10 min on
      the empty tree

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
# Run locally via act or just push a PR and watch the checks.
act -j lint
act -j type
act -j unit
\`\`\`

title: feat(inventory): movement service (receive/issue/adjust/consume) + on_hand cache
description:
## Problem / goal
The inventory ledger is append-only `inventory_movement` rows; the
denormalised `on_hand` on `inventory_item` must always equal the
sum of deltas. The movement service is the only writer to that
column.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"`inventory_movement`",
  §"Adjustments", §"Transfers between properties"; the on-hand
  cache invariant is implicit (recomputed from movements).
- DB tables from `p1.inventory`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/inventory/movement_service.py` with
  `receive`, `issue`, `adjust_to_observed`, `consume`,
  `transfer` (one call → two rows in a single transaction with
  shared `transfer_correlation_id` in `note`).
- Each call: insert movement, update `inventory_item.on_hand`
  via `UPDATE ... SET on_hand = on_hand + :delta` to avoid lost
  updates under contention; row-locks the item for the
  transaction.
- `adjust_to_observed(observed_qty)` computes
  `delta = observed - on_hand`, writes a single
  `audit_correction` movement (per spec §"Adjustments").
- `consume` accepts an optional `source_task_id` and `actor_kind`
  defaulting to caller's actor. Negative `on_hand` is permitted
  (per spec §"Consumption on task completion": staff > model).
- Emit `inventory.item_changed` event (used by `p7.reorder` and
  the SSE invalidation bus).
- Audit row per movement (the movement table itself doubles as
  the audit trail per §15 — but the cross-cutting `audit` row
  still goes for consistency with other services).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each method writes exactly one movement row and updates
      `on_hand` atomically.
- [ ] Concurrent issues against the same item never lose updates
      (test with parallel sessions if Postgres adapter, serialised
      under SQLite).
- [ ] `transfer` writes two movements in a single tx; rollback on
      either side rolls both back.
- [ ] `consume` with `source_task_id` carries it through.
- [ ] `inventory.item_changed` published exactly once per
      committed movement.
- [ ] Unit tests + an integration test for transfer rollback.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_movements.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_inventory_transfer_rollback.py -xvs
```

title: feat(domain/stays): turnover task auto-generation from reservation events
description:
## Problem / goal
Subscribe to \`reservation.upserted\` events and auto-generate one-off
turnover \`occurrence\` rows in the gap between check-out and the next
check-in on the same unit, respecting \`property_closure\` (no
turnover on closed dates) and per-unit lifecycle rules.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Stay task
  bundles" (after_checkout / before_checkin / during_stay), §"Airbnb-
  style edge cases" (same-day turnovers, owner stays).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"reservation",
  §"occurrence", §"property_closure".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx\` (turnover
  badges), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (TURNOVER_BUNDLES).

## Implementation notes
- **Cross-context coupling**: Tasks-context service lands in Phase 5
  (\`p5.tasks.*\`). For now expose a thin port
  \`app/ports/tasks_create_occurrence.py\` that the stays job calls;
  ship a no-op test double until Phase 5, but make the contract
  precise.
- \`app/domain/stays/turnover_generator.py\` subscribes to
  \`reservation.upserted\` via the Phase 0 event bus.
- Resolve next stay on same \`unit_id\` ordered by \`check_in_at\`;
  compute the gap window.
- Skip if the gap intersects any \`property_closure\` (call into
  \`closure_service.list_closures\` over the gap).
- Skip if \`guest_kind\` is filtered out by the rule's
  \`guest_kind_filter\` (default rules exclude \`owner\`).
- Same-day turnover handling per spec: time-box the bundle to the gap.
- Idempotency key: \`(reservation_id, rule_id)\` so re-fired events
  don't create duplicates; use the spec's edit-in-place rule when the
  shift is < 4h.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One occurrence per (reservation, rule); re-firing is a no-op
- [ ] Closures suppress turnover generation
- [ ] Owner-kind stays don't fire default rules
- [ ] < 4h reschedule patches in place; >= 4h regenerates
- [ ] Cross-context coupling documented and gated by the port
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover happy + closure + owner + reschedule paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_turnover_generator.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_turnover_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/admin): ChatGatewayPage — provider config + inbound test + dispatch health
description:
## Problem / goal
Configure the deployment-default WhatsApp / SMS provider, see
template approval state, send a test inbound message, and observe
dispatch health (last-error, queue depth). Per-workspace overrides
appear here too.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/chat-gateway`; §23 chat-gateway spec — webhook,
  templates, overrides.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `StatCard`, `Chip`, `Loading`;
  `Copy`, `Check`, `MessageSquare` from `lucide-react`; status /
  template tone maps.
- **API endpoints**:
  `GET /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/providers`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/templates`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/overrides`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/test_inbound`.
- **Types**: `AdminChatProvider`, `AdminChatProviderTemplate`,
  `AdminChatOverrideRow`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx`.
- Copy-button (clipboard) preserves the mock's animation +
  `Check` swap on success.
- Test-inbound submits a fake webhook payload and renders the
  dispatcher's response (correlation id, agent invoked, latency).
- SSE `chat_gateway.provider.changed` and
  `chat_gateway.template.changed` invalidate the relevant queries.
- Preserve every status tone mapping (connected→moss,
  error→rust, not_configured→ghost; approved→moss,
  pending→sand, rejected→rust, paused→ghost) and copy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Provider, template, override sections render identical to
      mock with correct chips.
- [ ] Test-inbound flows end-to-end through the dispatcher and
      shows the result inline.
- [ ] Webhook URL clipboard-copy works with the success animation.
- [ ] Permission gate: deployment-admin only.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_chat_gateway.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-chat-gateway.spec.ts
```

title: feat(web/public): LoginPage — passkey email prompt + magic-link fallback
description:
## Problem / goal
First impression for every visitor. Passkey is the only credential (§03 Principles); the page prompts for the user's email then invokes the WebAuthn ceremony, with a 'Lost your device?' link to /recover for magic-link recovery.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `KeyRound` icon (lucide), `<button class='btn btn--moss btn--lg login__primary'>`, `.surface--login`, `.login__card` semantic classes.
- **API endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/begin`, `POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/finish`, navigation to `/recover`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Wire the Use passkey button to the WebAuthn ceremony from `p2.passkey.login`.
- The email prompt is the rendezvous identifier — passkeys are looked up by it.
- On success, redirect to the role-appropriate landing page (worker → `/today`, manager → `/desk`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-public/LoginPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash

cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-public/LoginPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.

# capture .playwright-mcp/app-public/LoginPage.tsx.png

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/public/LoginPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(auth/magic-link): request + single-use consume + nonce store
description:
## Problem / goal
Magic links are the second factor for self-service recovery and the
sole credential during signup's email-verification step. They must be
single-use, time-bounded, and rate-limited.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Magic link format",
  §"Self-serve signup" (step 1 → 2), §"Recovery paths".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Rate limiting and
  abuse controls".
- **Phase 0** dependency: `app.mail` transport (`p0.mail`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/recover/RecoverRequest.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/recover/RecoverConsume.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Routes: `POST /auth/magic/request` and `POST /auth/magic/consume`
  (both bare-host, tenant-agnostic — `tenant_agnostic()` context).
- Token format per §03: `itsdangerous`-signed blob `{purpose,
  subject_id, jti, exp}` — purposes are `signup_verify`,
  `recover_passkey`, `email_change_confirm`.
- `magic_link_nonce` table (new) keyed by `jti`; insert on consume
  inside a `SERIALIZABLE`/`UPGRADE` lookup so a parallel consume
  loses the race and gets `409 already_consumed`.
- 10-minute window (`exp` capped server-side regardless of caller
  preference); expired tokens return 410.
- Rate limits: per-IP and per-email per §15 (lean on the rate-limit
  primitive from Phase 0; add the keys).
- Mail send uses `app.mail.send(to, template, ctx)` — never raw
  email bodies in code; templates live under `app/mail/templates/`.
- Audit: `audit.magic_link.sent`, `…consumed`, `…rejected` (no
  email or token in payload).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Token purpose enforced — a `signup_verify` token cannot be
      consumed by `/auth/magic/consume?purpose=recover_passkey`
- [ ] Single use guaranteed under concurrent consume
- [ ] 10-minute hard ceiling on `exp`
- [ ] Per-IP + per-email rate limits enforced
- [ ] Email never logged; only sha256(email) for correlation
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on both backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_magic_link.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_magic_link_concurrent.py -xvs
```
Manual: trigger recovery from `/recover`, confirm the dev-mail
log captures the URL, and replay it twice — second consume must 409.

title: feat(tenancy): SQLAlchemy before_compile filter + tenant_agnostic escape
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-inject \`AND workspace_id = :current_workspace_id\` on every query
touching a workspace-scoped table. Developers never thread the filter
by hand. Queries without a context **and** outside
\`tenant_agnostic()\` raise \`TenantFilterMissing\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Tenant filter
  enforcement".
- **Sibling spec**: \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Tenant
  isolation".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/tenancy/orm_filter.py\`: register a SQLAlchemy
  \`do_orm_execute\` / \`before_compile\` event on \`Session\` that:
  1. Walks the compiled \`Select\` / \`Update\` / \`Delete\` targets.
  2. If any target table is in
     \`tenancy.registry.WORKSPACE_SCOPED_TABLES\`:
     - If \`tenant_agnostic\` is set, skip.
     - Else, if \`get_current()\` is None, raise
       \`TenantFilterMissing(table=...)\`.
     - Else, inject \`AND workspace_id = :ctx_workspace_id\`.
- Wire the event hook in the engine factory (Phase 0 db task).
- Grep CI: a \`scripts/check_tenant_agnostic.py\` greps for
  \`tenant_agnostic(\` calls lacking a \`# justification:\` comment
  within 3 lines; fails the build. Plumb it into the \`lint\` CI job.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Query against a scoped table without a context raises
      \`TenantFilterMissing\`
- [ ] Same query inside a \`WorkspaceContext\` compiles with the
      workspace_id filter in the SQL
- [ ] Same query inside a \`tenant_agnostic()\` block compiles without
      the filter
- [ ] The grep script fails on an unjustified \`tenant_agnostic()\`
      call
- [ ] Unit tests cover every path, on both SQLite and Postgres

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tenancy_orm_filter.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tenancy_e2e.py -xvs
uv run python scripts/check_tenant_agnostic.py
\`\`\`
