title: feat(assets): asset CRUD + QR code gen/regen + property/area placement
description:
## Problem / goal
Concrete asset instances (this villa's boiler, this kitchen's
fridge) attached to a property and optionally an area. Each asset
gets a workspace-unique QR token used for the scan endpoint.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/21-assets.md` §"Assets", §"QR codes"
  §"Token format", §"URL pattern", §"Printing".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/assets/models.py`
  (`Asset`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetsPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetDetailPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/assets/assets.py`:
  - `create_asset(ctx, *, asset_type_id, property_id, area_id?,
    label, purchased_at?, warranty_ends_at?, metadata?)`.
  - `update_asset(ctx, asset_id, **fields)`.
  - `move_asset(ctx, asset_id, *, property_id, area_id?)` — separate
    method so the move event is distinct in audit.
  - `regenerate_qr(ctx, asset_id)` — issues a new token and
    invalidates the old one (audited; warns the user via SSE).
- QR token via `app/util/tokens.py::short_token(workspace_id, asset_id)`
  using HMAC of `(workspace_id, asset_id, nonce)` — base32 12-char.
  Unique constraint per workspace already in DB.
- PNG renderer in `app/adapters/qr/`:
  - `render_qr(token, *, size=512, label=None) -> bytes`
    using `segno`.
  - `GET /api/assets/{id}/qr.png` (cap `assets.view`) returns the
    PNG; cached via etag.
- Capability `assets.manage`.
- REST under `app/api/assets/assets.py`.
- Audit every mutation; emit `asset.changed` SSE.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD endpoints + audit
- [ ] QR token unique per workspace; collision retried
- [ ] Regenerate invalidates old token (404 on old scan)
- [ ] Move records both old and new placement in audit
- [ ] PNG endpoint returns valid PNG decodable by a QR reader

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_assets_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_assets_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/manager): organizations — clients, vendors, rate cards, work orders, invoices
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager (especially in agency / property-management mode) manages the third parties they bill or pay: clients, vendors, their rate cards, the work orders issued to them, and the invoices generated.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/OrganizationsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/organizations` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, RateCardEditor, WorkOrderTable, InvoiceTable.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/organizations, GET /api/v1/work_roles, GET /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/organizations/{oid} (detail).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/OrganizationsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The page nests four sub-tables (org list / detail / rate cards / work orders / invoices) — keep the master-detail layout from the mock; do not split into multiple routes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/organizations`.

title: feat(assets): action log (service/repair/replace/inspect/read) + document uploads
description:
## Problem / goal
Every interaction with an asset (serviced, repaired, replaced,
inspected, meter-read) is an `AssetAction` row carrying who, when,
notes, evidence, optional meter reading. Documents (warranty,
manual, invoice, photo, other) are uploaded via Storage and listed
on the asset page.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/21-assets.md` §"Asset actions", §"Asset
  documents", §"Asset-action to task integration".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/assets/models.py`
  (`AssetAction`, `AssetDocument`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetDetailPage.tsx`
  (action timeline + docs tab),
  `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DocumentsPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx` (worker
  records a read / quick service from the field).

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/assets/actions.py`:
  - `record_action(ctx, asset_id, *, kind, performed_at?, notes_md?,
    meter_reading?, evidence_blob_hash?)` — `performed_at` defaults
    to `now()`; emits `asset.action.recorded`.
  - `list_actions(ctx, asset_id, *, kind=None, since=None,
    until=None)` ordered by `performed_at desc`.
  - `next_due(ctx, asset_id)` — computes from default-actions
    `interval_days` per spec §"Next due computation".
- Service `app/domain/assets/documents.py`:
  - `attach_document(ctx, asset_id, *, blob_hash, filename,
    category)`.
  - `delete_document(ctx, document_id)` — soft delete (`deleted_at`).
  - `list_documents(ctx, asset_id, *, category=None)`.
- Capabilities `assets.record_action` (workers OK),
  `assets.manage_documents` (managers).
- Audit + SSE on every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Action CRUD; meter readings stored numeric; evidence FK valid
- [ ] `next_due` returns the soonest action across the catalog
- [ ] Document upload validates mime + 25 MB cap
- [ ] Workers can record actions on assets at their assigned
      properties; cannot delete documents
- [ ] All mutations audited

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_asset_actions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_asset_actions_api.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_asset_documents_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/admin): WorkspacesPage — list + trust + archive + quota
description:
## Problem / goal
Operators need to see every workspace on the deployment, trust /
archive them, and tune their quota / verification state. This page
is the directory for everything else admin.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/workspaces` — list, trust, archive; per-workspace summary
  card.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/WorkspacesPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`,
  verification-tone map; uses `formatMoney` for spend.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /admin/api/v1/workspaces`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/workspaces/{id}/trust`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/workspaces/{id}/archive` (and unarchive),
  `PATCH /admin/api/v1/workspaces/{id}` (quota cap edits).
- **Types**: `AdminWorkspaceRow`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy the mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/admin/WorkspacesPage.tsx`; swap fixture
  handlers for `useMutation` against the real endpoints with
  `qc.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: qk.adminWorkspaces() })` on
  success.
- Trust transitions emit
  `admin.workspace.trust_changed` audit (write at server). Front
  end relies on SSE `admin.workspace.changed` to refresh.
- Permission gate: enforced server-side (§4 deployment-grant
  check); render the standard not-authorised card on 404.
- Preserve verification-tone mapping
  (`trusted/human_verified/email_verified/unverified` →
  moss/sky/sand/ghost) and every label, copy string, breakpoint.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] List renders with verification chip + spend / cap progress
      identical to mock.
- [ ] Trust button toggles state with optimistic update + rollback
      on failure.
- [ ] Archive (and unarchive) updates `archived_at` and grays the
      row per the mock.
- [ ] Quota edit submits `PATCH` and refreshes via SSE.
- [ ] Non-deployment-admin gets 404 + the "ask your operator" card.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock at desktop breakpoint passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_workspaces.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-workspaces.spec.ts
```

title: feat(marketplace): vendor directory + service requests + match flow scaffolding (deferred-feature seam)
description:
## Problem / goal
Marketplace is **deferred** in v1 (§25 explicitly: "Not in v1");
the design exists so v1 doesn't quietly foreclose the feature.
This task lands the **scaffolding only** — the entity tables,
the capability gate (`settings.marketplace_enabled`, default
`false`), the API skeleton with all routes returning 404 unless
enabled, and the placeholder web pages — so a future enablement
is a translation + UX polish job rather than a months-long build.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/25-marketplace.md` §"Design summary" (one
  marketplace per deployment, auth-required, discovery-only,
  `platform_fee_event` ledger, capability-gated default off),
  §"Entities (shape)" (`marketplace_listing`, `service_request`,
  `marketplace_match`, `platform_fee_event`),
  §"How it plugs into §22" (auto-creates a
  `property_workspace_invite` on accept; `accrue_platform_fees`
  worker), §"Privacy and cross-workspace boundary" (listings
  publishable, requests semi-public, match acceptance triggers
  §15 cross-workspace redactions).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe D"
  (managed SaaS is the intended host).

## Implementation notes
- New migration `marketplace_v1`: creates the four tables exactly
  as §25 describes (`marketplace_listing`, `service_request`,
  `marketplace_match` with `source_match_id` FK back into
  `property_workspace_invite`, `platform_fee_event` append-only
  ledger). Tables are deployment-scope (no `workspace_id`); they
  reference workspaces by FK.
- New domain module `app/domain/marketplace/`:
  - `listings.py`, `requests.py`, `matches.py`, `fees.py` services.
  - All actions gated by `caps.has("settings.marketplace_enabled")`;
    when off, the service raises `FeatureDisabled` and the API
    returns 404.
- API routes under `app/api/v1/marketplace/`:
  - `GET/POST /marketplace/listings`, `GET/POST
    /marketplace/requests`, `POST /marketplace/matches/propose`,
    `POST /marketplace/matches/{id}/accept` (unconditionally
    approval-gated — `x-agent-confirm` annotation required),
    `POST /marketplace/matches/{id}/cancel`.
  - Every route 404s when `settings.marketplace_enabled = false`.
- Web pages (placeholders): `mocks/web/src/pages/marketplace/
  ListingDirectoryPage.tsx`, `RequestBoardPage.tsx`, `MatchPage.tsx`.
  Pages render an "enable marketplace in deployment settings"
  empty state when capability off.
- Worker `accrue_platform_fees`: subscribes to
  `booking_billing.resolved` + `vendor_invoice.approved`; writes
  one `platform_fee_event` row per matching pair; idempotent on
  the (match_id, source_kind, source_id) tuple. Disabled when
  capability off.
- Capability seam `features.postgis` registered (default off);
  GeoJSON columns stored as JSONB on PG, TEXT on SQLite — point-
  in-polygon done in application code via Shapely until PostGIS
  is enabled.
- All entity types added to the LLM tool surface only **when**
  `settings.marketplace_enabled = true`; default schema does not
  expose them to the agent.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Migration applies on SQLite + PG; `features.postgis` capability
      is registered (default off)
- [ ] Every API route returns 404 when capability off; documented
      operator setting flips it on
- [ ] When on, `marketplace_listing` CRUD + `service_request` CRUD
      + `marketplace_match.propose / accept` end-to-end works
- [ ] Match acceptance auto-creates a `property_workspace_invite`
      with the correct `source_match_id` back-pointer
- [ ] `accrue_platform_fees` writes one ledger row per qualifying
      `booking_billing` / `vendor_invoice` event; never updates
- [ ] Cross-workspace privacy redactions kick in on accept
      (guest identity hidden unless `share_guest_identity=true`)
- [ ] Web placeholder pages render correctly under both states
- [ ] OpenAPI document reflects the routes only when enabled
      (spec build flips the include flag)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/marketplace/test_capability_gate.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/marketplace/test_match_invite.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/marketplace/test_fee_accrual.py -xvs
cd mocks/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/marketplace
```

title: feat(web/manager): task templates with checklist editor + photo / timer steps
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager builds the task templates that materialised tasks instantiate from — ordered checklist steps, required photos, timed steps, priority defaults.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/templates` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, lucide-react Camera/Timer icons.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/task_templates, POST/PATCH /api/v1/task_templates.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The PRIORITY_TONE map in the mock ties priorities to tone tokens; reuse, do not re-derive.
- Drag-to-reorder steps must keep server-side ordering stable — debounce the PATCH.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/templates`.

title: feat(capabilities): probe webauthn_configured via make_relying_party at boot
description:
## Context
cd-gox scaffolded the WebAuthn RP config module (app/auth/webauthn.py). The task prompt mentioned an optional v1 nicety — adding a `features.webauthn_configured: bool` field to `Features` in app/capabilities.py, set by `_probe_features(settings)` after `make_relying_party(settings)` succeeds — deferred to keep cd-gox tightly scoped.

## What to add
- New bool field `webauthn_configured` on `app.capabilities.Features`.
- In `_probe_features`, wrap `make_relying_party(settings)` in try/except `RelyingPartyMisconfigured` to set the flag. A mismatched rp_id shouldn't crash capability boot — the flag going false will feed the health endpoint (§16) and the admin UI (§14).
- Update existing capability tests in tests/unit/test_capabilities.py with a case asserting the flag is true for a valid public_url / dev fallback, false when misconfigured.

## Out of scope
- Any bind-guard integration (that lives separately in cd-… — consult /resume-work).
- Flipping the flag at runtime (mutations to `Features` are forbidden; re-probe happens on process restart).

title: feat(api/llm): agents, approvals, preferences, model assignments, usage, budget, outbound webhooks
description:
## Problem / goal
LLM router — everything the agent runtime, the per-user agent
preferences, and the deployment LLM-graph admin need: agent
runtime invocation, human-in-the-loop approval flow, per-user
preferences, model assignments (capability → model chain),
usage feed, budget ledger, and outbound webhook subscriptions.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"LLM and approvals",
  §"Agent preferences", §"Admin surface" (LLM graph + chat
  gateway + usage aggregates).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LLMGraphPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ProvidersPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ModelsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AssignmentsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/PromptLibraryPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AgentDocsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/CallFeedPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/UsagePage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/BudgetPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/AgentPreferencesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/llm.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`llm\`.
- Agent invocation: \`POST /agents/{slug}/invoke\` — accepts
  prompt + page-context; returns either a result or an
  \`approval_request_id\` (409 \`approval_required\`).
- Approvals: \`POST /approvals/{id}/decision\` with
  \`approve|reject|amend\`; audited; SSE
  \`approval_request.decided\` fires.
- Preferences: \`/me/agent-preferences\` (per-user toggles).
- Model graph endpoints (providers / models / pricing /
  assignments / prompt library / agent docs) are
  deployment-scoped and live under \`/admin/api/v1/...\`,
  schema-defined here.
- Outbound webhooks: \`POST /webhooks\` registers a URL +
  HMAC secret; signed deliveries per spec §12 §"Webhook
  signatures".
- Budget endpoint exposes per-period spent_cents + cap_cents.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Agent invoke returns either result or 409
      \`approval_required\` with \`approval_request_id\`
- [ ] Approval decision audits + emits SSE
- [ ] Preferences PATCH per-user
- [ ] Provider / model / assignment CRUD on admin surface
- [ ] Outbound webhook delivery signs payload with HMAC
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`llm\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_llm.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_llm_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(llm/tasks-intake): parse free text → template + schedule + assignee
description:
## Problem / goal
`POST /api/v1/tasks/from_nl` turns a free-text instruction ("Have
Maria deep-clean the guest bath every Tuesday 9am at Villa Sud")
into a resolved `{property, assignee, template, schedule}` plus
an explicit `assumptions` and `ambiguities` list. Always
dry-run-first; the caller confirms via `POST /from_nl/commit`.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Natural-language
  task intake".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`task_template`,
  §`task_schedule`.
- Cross-ref: §05 "Schedule CRUD" — consumed by
  `p5.schedule.crud` (the commit endpoint).
- Mocks: NL intake composer in the manager web.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/capabilities/tasks_intake.py`
  with `draft(ctx, text) -> NlPreview` and
  `commit(ctx, preview_id, edits) -> ScheduledTask`.
- LLM resolves property name, assignee name, RRULE, evidence
  flags. Resolver also performs a deterministic post-pass: every
  named entity (property, user, evidence kind) must resolve to a
  concrete row or land in `ambiguities` — no free-text
  fabrication into the resolved object.
- Preview persisted to a short-lived table `nl_task_preview`
  (TTL 24 h) keyed by `preview_id`.
- Commit honours `Idempotency-Key` per §12.
- Budget + audit via the standard wrapper. Reads of the workspace
  graph (properties / users) flow through tenant filters.
- Failure modes: ambiguity raised when more than one match for a
  named entity; `assumptions` filled when exactly one match for
  an unspecified field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sample text in the spec produces the expected `resolved`
  shape and `assumptions` list.
- [ ] Two properties whose names both match raise an ambiguity.
- [ ] `commit` with an unmodified preview creates the
  scheduled task; `commit` after edits applies the edits.
- [ ] Idempotency-Key dedup works on commit.
- [ ] Expired preview returns `410 preview_expired`.
- [ ] Audit on commit attributes to the calling user with token
  + agent_label when delegated.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/capabilities/test_tasks_intake.py`.
- Integration test: end-to-end from NL to a row in `task_template`
  + `task_schedule`.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

description:
## Problem / goal
Manager audits one employee's leave history (requested, approved, declined, taken) and remaining balance for the year.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeLeavesPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/user/<eid>/leaves` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/employees/{eid}/leaves.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeLeavesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.


## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/user/<eid>/leaves`.

title: feat(payroll/pay-period): CRUD + draft → locked → paid state machine
description:
## Problem / goal
Pay periods are the windows over which payslips are computed. They
walk a strict state machine: `draft → locked → paid`. Locking
freezes the inputs; paid means money was disbursed.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Pay period",
  §"Period close", §"Transition to paid".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`PayPeriod`).

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/payroll/periods.py`:
  - `create_period(ctx, *, starts_at, ends_at)` — refuses overlap
    with another non-draft period.
  - `lock_period(ctx, period_id)` — only from `draft`. Captures
    `locked_at`, `locked_by`. Triggers payslip recompute (Phase 8
    task `p8.payslip.compute`) for every active engagement.
  - `mark_paid(ctx, period_id)` — only from `locked`. All payslips
    must have a `paid_at` timestamp recorded externally (or be
    cancelled).
  - `reopen_period(ctx, period_id)` — locked → draft only; rejected
    if any payslip already paid.
- Capability `payroll.manage_periods`.
- Emit events `pay_period.locked`, `pay_period.paid` for digests.
- All transitions audit.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Overlap detection works
- [ ] State transitions enforced; bad transitions return 409
- [ ] Locking triggers payslip recompute (smoke)
- [ ] Paid state irreversible without reopen
- [ ] Audit + events emitted

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_pay_periods.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_pay_periods_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(api/places): properties, units, areas, closures + multi-belonging
description:
## Problem / goal
Places router covering properties, their units and areas,
closures, and the multi-belonging surface (a property can be
shared across workspaces via \`property_workspace\`).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Properties / areas /
  stays" (properties, units, areas, closures, multi-belonging
  share endpoints).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertiesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Units / Areas / Closures tabs + Sharing tab).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/places.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`places\`.
- Pydantic schemas split per resource. \`PropertyCreate\` accepts
  the canonical \`address_json\`; the back-fill of
  \`property.country\` happens in the service, not the router.
- Multi-belonging endpoints (\`POST /properties/{id}/share\`,
  \`DELETE /properties/{id}/share/{workspace_slug}\`) live here
  but are gated by an explicit \`places.share\` capability.
- Closure endpoints emit SSE \`property_closure.upserted\` so the
  scheduler view invalidates.
- All listings cursor-paginated; every mutation idempotent.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD routes for property, unit, area, closure, share
- [ ] \`address_json\` validated against canonical shape
- [ ] Cross-workspace operations denied unless caller has
      \`places.share\` cap
- [ ] Sharing endpoint creates one \`property_workspace\` row,
      audits, fires SSE
- [ ] Closure endpoint refuses overlap with confirmed-stay
      window unless \`force=true\`
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`places\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_places.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_places_routes.py -xvs
uv run schemathesis run --checks all http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json --tag places
\`\`\`

title: feat(authz/role-grants): grant CRUD service + owner-authority policy
description:
## Problem / goal
Role grants are the per-user authority model: who is a manager / worker
/ client / guest, and on which property scope. The service must enforce
the owner-authority policy (only owners can mint manager grants) and
validate scope shape.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Role grants",
  §"Surface grants at a glance", §"Permissions: surface, groups,
  and action catalog".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"role_grant".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/team/RoleGrantsAdmin.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `role_grant`.

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/identity/role_grants.py`:
  - `list(ctx, *, user_id=None, scope_property_id=None)`
  - `grant(ctx, *, user_id, grant_role, scope_property_id=None)`
  - `revoke(ctx, *, grant_id)`
- Policy:
  - Only members of `owners@<ws>` may grant `manager`.
  - Only owners + managers may grant `worker` / `client` / `guest`.
  - `scope_property_id` must reference a `property_workspace` row in
    the calling workspace; cross-workspace property → 422.
  - Cannot remove the last owner grant — 409 `last_owner_grant`.
- Routes:
  - `GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/role-grants`
  - `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/role-grants`
  - `DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/role-grants/{id}`
- Audit `audit.role_grant.granted` / `…revoked` in the same tx as
  the row mutation.
- Capability check via the action catalog (`users.grant_role` /
  `users.revoke_grant`, §05).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Owner-only authority enforced for manager grants
- [ ] Last-owner protection prevents lockout
- [ ] Cross-workspace scope_property_id rejected
- [ ] Audit row written for every mutation
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_role_grants.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_role_grants_policy.py -xvs
```
Manual: drive `/team` admin in dev, attempt to remove the only owner,
confirm 409.

title: feat(tasks): completion service (state machine + checklist + skip)
description:
## Problem / goal
"Mark done" is the highest-traffic worker action. The service must
gate-check the state machine, enforce evidence + checklist policy,
write the completion tombstone, fire `task.completed`, and handle
the concurrent-completion race per §06.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  §"Task row" → "State machine", §"Completing a task",
  §"Skipping and cancellation", §"Concurrent completion",
  §"Evidence policy inheritance".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`
  for the worker UX (Mark done, Skip with reason, Cancel).

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/completion.py`.
- All transitions are explicit `transition_to_<state>` functions
  on the service, each enforcing the §06 graph:
  - `start(ctx, task_id)` — `pending → in_progress`
  - `complete(ctx, task_id, *, note_md, evidence: list[EvidenceRef]
    | None = None)` — `pending | in_progress → completed`. Enforces:
    - Resolved `evidence.policy` from §06 cascade (forbid first,
      else most-specific) — reject if `policy == 'require'` and no
      photo, reject any photo when `policy == 'forbid'`.
    - When `tasks.checklist_required` is true, every required
      `task_checklist_item` must have `completed_at` set.
    - Apply `inventory_consumption_json` via `inventory_movement`
      rows unless `inventory.consume_on_task = false`.
    - When `task.asset_action_id` is set, update
      `asset_action.last_performed_at|last_performed_task_id`.
    - Insert a `task_completion` tombstone row (history survives
      later edits).
  - `skip(ctx, task_id, *, reason)` — only when
    `tasks.allow_skip_with_reason = true` resolves to true at the
    task scope.
  - `cancel(ctx, task_id, *, reason)` — owners/managers only.
  - `revert_overdue(ctx, task_id, target_state)` — clears
    `overdue_since`.
- All transitions are `enum -> enum` functions with a single
  `_assert_transition(current, target)` validator; raise typed
  `InvalidStateTransition` carrying both states.
- **Concurrent completion** (§06): the second writer wins —
  `UPDATE ... SET completed_at = ..., completed_by_user_id = ...`
  unconditionally. The loser receives a 200 with the final row;
  audit `task.complete_superseded` against the displaced row.
- Emit `task.completed`, `task.skipped`, `task.cancelled` on the
  bus; one audit row per mutation; `WorkspaceContext` everywhere.
- Permission checks (§06 "State transitions and who may trigger
  them"): worker may drive their own assigned tasks through
  `pending ↔ in_progress ↔ completed`; owners/managers may move
  any task to `cancelled`; only the scheduler worker may drive
  the auto `scheduled → pending` flip.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every illegal transition raises `InvalidStateTransition`
- [ ] Required-photo policy enforced (and forbid is absolute)
- [ ] Required checklist items enforced when the resolved setting is on
- [ ] Inventory movements written by default, suppressed when
      `inventory.consume_on_task = false`
- [ ] Asset-action `last_performed_*` updated when applicable
- [ ] `task_completion` tombstone written
- [ ] Concurrent-completion race produces the audit
      `task.complete_superseded` row
- [ ] Three terminal events emitted; audit on every mutation
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover all branches

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_completion.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tasks_completion_race.py -xvs
```

title: feat(llm/digest-gen): friendly prose around structured digest stats
description:
## Problem / goal
The daily digest job (§10, `p10.digest.daily`) assembles
structured facts (overdue tasks, upcoming stays, anomalies)
deterministically; this capability writes the friendly prose
wrapper. The structured data is authoritative — a post-generation
check rejects + rewrites prose that contradicts the numbers.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Daily digest /
  anomaly detection".
- Cross-ref: §10 "Daily digest" — consumed by
  `p10.digest.daily` (the cron orchestrator).
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`anomaly_suppression`.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/capabilities/digest_gen.py` with
  `compose(ctx, recipient_user_id, structured_payload) ->
  DigestProse`.
- Prompt template under capability `digest.compose` (self-
  seeded per §11). System prompt pins "do not invent numbers
  or names; rephrase only".
- Post-generation validator: extract every number, currency
  amount, and proper noun from the prose; assert each one
  appears in the structured payload (or is one of a small
  whitelist of stop-words). On mismatch, regenerate up to twice;
  on third failure, fall back to a deterministic template.
- Recipient locale honoured (§18) — the prompt loads the
  recipient's preferred language and tone from
  `p12.preferences`.
- Anomaly ranking + one-line explanations per anomaly per the
  spec; ignored anomalies are filtered out by the orchestrator
  via `anomaly_suppression` (this capability does not read
  the table itself).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A digest with no anomalies and one overdue task produces
  prose that names the task and the assignee verbatim.
- [ ] Prose that introduces a hallucinated number is regenerated;
  third failure falls back to template.
- [ ] Recipient with French locale receives French prose.
- [ ] At-cap workspace triggers the deterministic-template
  fallback (no LLM call attempted).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/capabilities/test_digest_gen.py`
  with golden structured payloads.
- Integration test wired in alongside `p10.digest.daily`.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(messaging): chat_channel CRUD (staff / manager / chat_gateway)
description:
## Problem / goal
Channels are the container for chat threads. Three kinds: \`staff\`
(everyone in workspace), \`manager\` (managers only), and
\`chat_gateway\` (auto-created per inbound contact).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"In-app
  messaging" (channels & roles); kind=\`chat_gateway\` integrates
  with \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"\`chat_thread\`".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ChatChannelsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/components/ChatChannelsMeCard.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/messaging/channels.py\`:
  \`ChatChannelService(ctx)\` with \`create / list / get / archive /
  add_member / remove_member\`.
- Visibility: \`staff\` returned to every workspace member; \`manager\`
  filtered by capability \`messaging.manager_channel\`;
  \`chat_gateway\` returned only to staff with the
  \`chat_gateway.read\` capability.
- API \`app/api/messaging/channels.py\`:
  - \`GET /chat/channels\` (paginated)
  - \`POST /chat/channels\` (manager-only for \`staff/manager\`;
    auto-create only for \`chat_gateway\`)
  - \`PATCH /chat/channels/{id}\` (rename, archive)
- Audit \`messaging.channel.created/archived\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visibility per kind enforced — manager-only never leaks to
      worker
- [ ] Archive is soft (\`archived_at\`); list defaults to active only
- [ ] Workers can post to \`staff\`; not to \`manager\`
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_chat_channel.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/messaging/test_chat_channel_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: sec(auth): brute-force throttles, disposable-domain blocklist, suspicious-activity alerts
description:
## Problem / goal
Open self-serve signup, magic-link recovery, and passkey login are
the highest-blast-radius public surfaces. This task lands the
shared throttle + alert path: per-IP and per-email rate limits on
magic-link send and passkey login attempts, the disposable-domain
blocklist (already partly in §15), and `/admin/signups`
suspicious-activity surfacing.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Rate limiting and
  abuse controls" (10/min/IP login begin, 5/min/IP magic-link send,
  3 fails → 10 min IP lockout), §"Self-serve abuse mitigations"
  (the per-IP/per-email/global signup caps, disposable-domain list
  with weekly refresh + 30-day staleness CI gate, magic-link 15-min
  TTL one-use, abuse signals to audit + `/admin/signups`,
  `signup_gc` worker), §"Per-IP aggregate LLM spend cap" (3×
  per-WS cap default, `402 payment_required`, verify-ownership
  flow).
- **Phase 2**: `p2.signup.abuse`, `p2.magic-link`, `p2.passkey.login`
  already cover the per-feature pieces; this task unifies them
  through a shared throttle path so adding a new endpoint is one
  decorator, not three.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/abuse/throttle.py`: a `@throttle(scope, key_fn,
  budget_per_window, window_s)` decorator backed by a sliding-window
  counter (sqlite table or Postgres `bigserial` table — pick
  whichever the storage layer prefers; per-row TTL via the existing
  GC worker).
- Wire `@throttle` into magic-link send, passkey
  begin/finish, signup start, recovery start. Each instantiation
  cites the §15 budget value.
- Disposable-domain list under `app/abuse/data/disposable_domains.txt`
  with the leading-comment freshness pin (`# generated YYYY-MM-DD`);
  a CI step `refresh-disposable-domains.yml` regenerates weekly.
  CI build asserts the in-repo file is < 30 days old.
- Suspicious-activity rollups (burst-rate trips, same IP across
  distinct emails, repeat provisioning) write to
  `audit.signup.suspicious` and surface on `/admin/signups`.
- Per-IP LLM spend cap landed here too: rolling 30-day sum across
  every workspace whose `signup_ip` matches; default 3×; `402`
  + verify-ownership banner.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Magic-link send: 6th attempt in a minute returns 429
- [ ] Passkey login: 11th begin attempt in a minute returns 429
- [ ] 3 failed magic-link consumptions trigger a 10-minute IP
      lockout
- [ ] Signup with a disposable domain returns
      `400 disposable_email`
- [ ] CI fails when the disposable-domain file is > 30 days old
- [ ] Per-IP LLM spend cap returns `402 payment_required` with the
      spec banner copy when exceeded
- [ ] Verify-ownership flow promotes the workspace and removes it
      from the per-IP cap pool
- [ ] `/admin/signups` shows burst-rate + duplicate-email signals

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/abuse/test_throttle.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_abuse_guards.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_signups_dashboard.py -xvs
```

title: feat(expenses): claim CRUD (draft / submit) with Storage attachments
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers create expense claims (vendor, amount, currency, category,
date, optional property, description) in `draft`, attach receipt
images via Storage, then `submit` for manager approval.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Expense
  claims" §"Submission flow (worker)", §"Model".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/expenses/models.py`
  (`Expense`, `Receipt`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/expenses/claims.py`:
  - `create_claim(ctx, **fields)` → `draft`.
  - `update_claim(ctx, expense_id, **fields)` — only while `draft`.
  - `attach_receipt(ctx, expense_id, blob_hash)` — references an
    already-uploaded blob; up to 10 receipts per claim.
  - `submit_claim(ctx, expense_id)` → `submitted`; emits
    `expense.submitted` event.
  - `cancel_claim(ctx, expense_id)` — owner only, only while `draft`
    or `submitted` (not after `approved`).
- Currency in ISO 4217; amount stored as integer cents.
- REST under `app/api/expenses/claims.py`.
- Capabilities `expense.claim_self`.
- Audit on every transition.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Worker can CRUD their own draft claims; not other users'
- [ ] Submitted claims become read-only
- [ ] Receipt attachments validated (image/* mime, < 10 MB)
- [ ] Event emitted on submit
- [ ] OpenAPI regenerated

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_expense_claims.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_expense_claims_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tenancy): FastAPI middleware resolving /w/<slug>/\u2026 \u2192 WorkspaceContext
description:
## Problem / goal
Every authenticated HTTP surface lives under \`/w/<slug>/\u2026\`. The
middleware:
1. Parses the slug from the path.
2. Validates against the slug regex + blocklist.
3. Loads the workspace row (via identity context, once it exists —
   Phase 2 wires the real call; stub initially).
4. Verifies \`user_workspace\` membership for the authenticated actor.
5. Returns \`404\` if not a member (never \`403\`, per §01).
6. Binds a \`WorkspaceContext\` via \`tenancy.set_current\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Workspace addressing",
  §"WorkspaceContext".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/tenancy/middleware.py\`: \`WorkspaceContextMiddleware\`
  (Starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware). Skip-paths (bare-host routes) are
  hard-coded per §01.
- Correlation id: if the request carries \`X-Request-Id\`, use it;
  else generate a ULID. Log it, put it on \`WorkspaceContext\`.
- For Phase 0 the membership check can be a stub that always passes
  when \`X-Test-Workspace-Id\` is present. Phase 2 (identity) replaces
  the stub.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Request to \`/w/villa-sud/api/v1/ping\` sets the context
- [ ] Request to \`/w/<unknown>/api/v1/ping\` returns 404
- [ ] Skip-paths (\`/healthz\`, \`/signup\`, \`/login\`, \`/w\`,
      \`/docs\`, \`/openapi.json\`, etc.) don't attempt slug parsing
- [ ] Unit tests cover every branch

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/employee): AssetScanPage — camera/QR scan -> AssetDetail
description:
## Problem / goal
Camera-first scan surface (§21 assets) for workers to find an asset's record by pointing at the QR sticker. On detect, navigates to `/asset/{id}`. The mock includes a 'Demo' link that opens a sample asset directly.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `Link` (react-router), `.scan-overlay`, `.scan-frame`, `.scan-text` semantic classes.
- **API endpoints**: Client-side QR decoding (no server endpoint); resolves to `/asset/{qr_code}` which calls `GET /api/v1/assets/{qr_code}`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Use a maintained QR library (e.g. `@zxing/browser` or `qr-scanner`) — declare the choice in the PR.
- Request camera permission with a clear in-page prompt; degrade gracefully if denied (show the manual code-entry fallback).
- Preserve the demo link in dev builds only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/AssetScanPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/AssetScanPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/AssetScanPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(tasks): assignment service (primary + backups + candidate pool)
description:
## Problem / goal
Assigning a task is more than `task.assigned_user_id = ?`. §06
defines a five-step algorithm: walk the schedule's primary +
backups, then a candidate pool filtered by work-role + property
rota + availability stack, then load-balance with rotation
tiebreakers. Every path emits an event the manager can act on.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  §"Availability precedence stack", §"Assignment algorithm",
  §"Pull-back logic for before_checkin tasks".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx` shows the
  end result on a workspace timeline; `mocks/app/mock_data.py`
  for the `assignee` shape on occurrences.
- Boundary: `app/domain/tasks/assignment.py`. Reads from
  identity, places, and the availability tables; writes only to
  `tasks` (and an audit row).

## Implementation notes
- Entry: `assign_task(ctx, task_id, *, override_user_id: UUID |
  None = None) -> AssignmentResult`.
  - When `override_user_id` is set (manager UI), skip the algorithm
    and assign directly; emit `task.assigned` with
    `assignment_source = 'manual'`.
  - Else run the §06 algorithm:
    1. Walk `[default_assignee, *backup_assignee_user_ids]`; for
       each, run the availability precedence stack
       (`leave → override → public_holiday → weekly_pattern`) and
       the rota filter (`schedule_ruleset` covering weekday + local
       window, or generalist fallback). First available wins;
       record `assignment_source = 'primary' | 'backup[N]'`.
    2. Else build a candidate pool: users with the right
       `user_work_role`, scoped via
       `property_work_role_assignment` (or generalist), passing
       the same availability + rota filters; exclude users already
       tried in step 1.
    3. Tiebreaker: fewest tasks at this property in the 7-day
       window around `scheduled_for_local`; then rotation by oldest
       last-task-here.
  - When zero candidates: leave `assigned_user_id = NULL`, emit
    `task.primary_unavailable` (step-1 attempted) **or**
    `task.unassigned` (pool was empty from the start).
- `reassign_task(ctx, task_id, new_user_id)` — explicit move; same
  validation; emits `task.reassigned` with the previous user.
- `unassign_task(ctx, task_id, *, reason)` — emits `task.unassigned`.
- `WorkspaceContext` everywhere; one audit row per mutation
  carrying the `assignment_source` and the candidate pool size.
- All event payloads carry `task_id`, `workspace_id`,
  `assigned_user_id`, `assignment_source`, `candidate_count`.
- Helper `availability_for(user, date_local, property_id)` reused
  by the scheduler UI (kept here so there is one source of truth).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Primary-then-backup ordering verified by unit tests
- [ ] Candidate pool excludes users already tried via primary/backup
- [ ] Rota filter respected (and generalist fallback works)
- [ ] Tiebreakers (fewest-tasks, rotation) deterministic in tests
- [ ] Three event variants emitted in the right cases
- [ ] Audit row carries `assignment_source` and pool size
- [ ] Unit tests for each branch (primary, each backup, pool,
      empty)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_assignment.py -xvs
```

title: feat(auth/passkey): registration challenge + verify endpoints
description:
## Problem / goal
Implement the passkey registration ceremony so an authenticated user
(or a freshly verified signup session) can enrol a credential. Two
endpoints — challenge issue + assertion verify — produce a
`passkey_credential` row.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"WebAuthn specifics",
  §"Self-serve signup" step 3, §"Additional passkeys", §"Privacy"
  (whitelist of stored fields).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/onboarding/PasskeyEnroll.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/profile/Passkeys.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `app/adapters/db/identity/models.py::PasskeyCredential`.
- Depends on the RP module from `p2.webauthn-rp`.

## Implementation notes
- Routes mounted at `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/passkey/register/start`
  and `.../register/finish`. A bare-host equivalent under
  `/api/v1/auth/passkey/register/*` exists for the signup path
  (tenant-agnostic, scoped to the in-progress signup session).
- `start`: generates challenge via `app.auth.webauthn`, stashes
  `(user_id, challenge, exclude_credentials)` in a short-lived
  `webauthn_challenge` row (Redis-style row in the identity DB,
  10-min TTL).
- `finish`: verifies attestation, inserts `passkey_credential`
  (credential_id, public_key, sign_count, transports, AAGUID, label).
  Writes `audit.passkey.registered` in the same transaction
  (`app.audit.write_audit`).
- Per-user cap: 5 passkeys (§03). Returns 422
  `error = "too_many_passkeys"` on the 6th.
- Service signature `register_finish(ctx: WorkspaceContext, …)`
  for the in-workspace path; `register_finish_signup(signup_ctx, …)`
  uses `tenant_agnostic()` for the bootstrap path.
- No PII in audit beyond the user id; AAGUID and label are fine.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `start` returns options matching `PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions`
- [ ] `finish` rejects mismatched challenge / origin / rp_id with 400
- [ ] Successful finish writes one `passkey_credential` row + one
      audit row in the same transaction
- [ ] 6th passkey for a user returns 422 `too_many_passkeys`
- [ ] Replay of the same `finish` request returns 409
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on SQLite and Postgres

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_register.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_passkey_register_pg.py -xvs
```
Manual: in the dev container, drive `/onboarding/passkey` with
Playwright virtual authenticator and confirm a row lands.

title: feat(web/manager): property detail — units, areas, closures, staff, sharing
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager edits one property's facts (units, areas, default checklist, settings) and shares or revokes access to peer workspaces (cross-workspace property sharing per §02).

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/property/<pid>` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, EntitySettingsPanel.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/properties/{pid}, GET /api/v1/employees, GET /api/v1/properties/{pid}/settings, GET /api/v1/settings/catalog, POST /api/v1/property_workspaces/share, POST /api/v1/property_workspaces/revoke.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**,
  but the mock is ~550 lines — **split it** during promotion into a
  tree under `app/web/src/pages/manager/property/`, keeping
  `PropertyDetailPage.tsx` itself under ~250 lines. Suggested split:
  - `SettingsOverridePanel.tsx` — the catalog-driven override panel
    sub-component already defined in the mock.
  - `SharingPanel.tsx` — the share / revoke cross-workspace panel.
  - `AreasPanel.tsx`, `UnitsPanel.tsx`, `StaffPanel.tsx`,
    `ClosuresPanel.tsx` — one per top-level section visible on the
    page.
  - `lib/propertyFormatters.ts` — `fmtDayMon`, `fmtDayMonTime`,
    `formatValue`.
  - `PropertyDetailPage.tsx` — orchestrator: fetches detail, wires
    mutations + SSE, composes the panels.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons —
  rendered DOM and class names must be byte-equivalent to the mock.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The share/revoke mutations operate on the cross-workspace `property_workspace` join — verify the Phase 14 router exposes them with the same body shape the mock posts.
- The settings panel relies on the catalog-driven `EntitySettingsPanel` shared component; do not duplicate its logic locally.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~250 lines.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/property/<pid>`.

title: feat(leave): manager approval flow + conflict detection + leave.decided event
description:
## Problem / goal
A manager working the leaves inbox must see, per pending request, any
shifts or scheduled task occurrences that overlap the requested range
so they can make an informed call. On decision, an event fans out so
downstream services (assignment generator, notifications) can react.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Worker settings"
  (note worker view), `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Leave",
  `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Conflict detection",
  `docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md` §"Events" for the event
  shape, `docs/specs/12-api.md` §"/leaves/{id}/decision".
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/LeavesInboxPage.tsx`.
- Event bus from `p0.events` — publish `leave.decided` (payload:
  `leave_id`, `decision`, `decided_by`, `decided_at`,
  `conflicting_shift_ids`, `conflicting_occurrence_ids`).

## Implementation notes
- New `LeaveDecisionService.decide(leave_id, decision, rationale)`
  takes `WorkspaceContext`; permission-gates on (currently
  hard-coded — wire to `permission_rule` resolver once `p4.permission.enforce`
  lands) `manager` grant_role; stamps `decided_by`/`decided_at`.
- Conflict detection helper queries `Shift` + `Occurrence` for the
  requesting user where `(starts_at, ends_at)` overlaps the leave
  window; returns ids only (UI fetches detail).
- Atomic: decision + audit + event publish in the same transaction
  (use the outbox pattern from `p0.events`).
- Conflicts do NOT block the decision — they're advisory. The
  manager can still approve a leave that overlaps a shift; the
  downstream assignment service is expected to react to the event
  and unassign / reassign.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/leaves/{id}/decision` with
      `{decision: approved|rejected, rationale_md}` flips status,
      stamps decision metadata.
- [ ] `GET /workspaces/{ws}/leaves/{id}/conflicts` returns
      overlapping shift_ids + occurrence_ids.
- [ ] `leave.decided` event published exactly once per terminal
      transition; replay-safe (decision is immutable post-decide).
- [ ] Audit row records prior status + new status + rationale.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: no conflicts, with conflicts, idempotent
      replay, unauthorized caller.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_leave_approval.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_leave_approval_event.py -xvs
```

title: feat(domain/places): property service — create, update, soft-delete, list
description:
## Problem / goal
First-class CRUD for properties with workspace-scoped listing,
soft-delete, and the multi-belonging bootstrap that creates the initial
\`property_workspace\` row (with \`membership_role = owner_workspace\`
and a label) so the new property is visible to its creating workspace
from the first commit.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Property",
  §"`address_json` canonical shape", §"Multi-belonging
  (sharing across workspaces)".
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"property",
  §"property_workspace".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertiesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (PROPERTIES list).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/property_service.py\` — pure use cases
  (\`create_property\`, \`update_property\`, \`soft_delete_property\`,
  \`list_properties\`, \`get_property\`).
- Public surface \`app/services/places/__init__.py\` re-exports the use
  cases as the only allowed import path from other contexts.
- Every method takes a \`WorkspaceContext\`; reads filter through the
  Phase 0 tenancy filter on the \`property_workspace\` join.
- On create: insert \`Property\` then \`PropertyWorkspace\` row with
  \`membership_role = "owner_workspace"\` + caller-supplied label
  (defaults to \`property.name\`); both writes inside one transaction.
- On write: copy \`address_json.country\` → \`property.country\`
  back-fill rule per spec.
- Audit every mutation via the Phase 0 audit port.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Create yields one \`property\` + one \`property_workspace\` row
- [ ] Update is workspace-scoped; cross-workspace update is denied
- [ ] Soft-delete sets \`deleted_at\`, hides from default list, audited
- [ ] List filters by workspace via \`property_workspace\` join
- [ ] Country back-fill works in both directions
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes; no \`Any\`
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy + cross-tenant denial paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_property_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_property_crud.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(expenses/ocr): receipt OCR via LLM (default model) → autofill claim
description:
## Problem / goal
On receipt upload, run an OCR / extraction pass via the LLM port
(default model — full router lands in Phase 12) and autofill the
draft claim's `vendor_name`, `amount_cents`, `currency`, `spent_at`,
suggested `category`. Store raw output + confidence on the receipt
row.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Submission
  flow (worker)" (OCR autofill), §"LLM accuracy & guardrails";
  `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` (LLM port shape).
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/expenses/models.py`
  (`Receipt.ocr_json`, `Receipt.ocr_confidence`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`
  shows the OCR loading state + accept/reject.

## Implementation notes
- Worker job `extract_receipt(ctx, receipt_id)`:
  - Loads blob from Storage.
  - Calls `llm.extract(prompt='receipt-extract', image=...)` using
    the **default extraction model** registered in Phase 0
    (`p0.llm`). The capability→model `ModelAssignment` lookup is
    Phase 12 (`p12.model-router`) — for now, read a fallback model
    id from config (`LLM_OCR_MODEL`).
  - Parses to a pydantic schema (`ReceiptExtraction`):
    `vendor`, `amount_cents`, `currency`, `spent_at`, `category`,
    `confidence` (0-1 per field).
  - Writes `receipt.ocr_json`, `receipt.ocr_confidence`.
  - If overall confidence > threshold AND claim is still in `draft`
    AND fields are empty → autofill claim fields. Otherwise leave
    fields untouched and emit a UI hint.
- Job is enqueued from `attach_receipt` (Phase 8 task
  `p8.expense.crud`) without blocking the HTTP request.
- Audit `receipt.ocr_completed` with token / cost / latency
  (records LLM usage row per `p1.llm`).
- PII safety: only the receipt image goes to the model — no other
  workspace data; respect `LLM_REDACT` workspace setting.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Job runs end-to-end against a stubbed LLM port
- [ ] Pydantic schema rejects malformed model output
- [ ] Autofill only happens on draft + empty + high confidence
- [ ] LLM usage row recorded
- [ ] No PII beyond the image leaves the process
- [ ] Failure modes (LLM timeout, parse error) leave the claim
      untouched and audit the failure

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_receipt_extraction.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_receipt_ocr_job.py -xvs
```

title: feat(agent/approval): HITL approval pipeline (workspace + per-user)
description:
## Problem / goal
Two layers of gating intercept agent writes: the workspace policy
(committee, owner/manager-curated) and the per-user agent approval
mode (self, `bypass | auto | strict`). On gate, the runtime
returns 202 with an approval id; deciders approve/reject from
`/approvals` (desk) or inline in chat (web sidebar). On approve,
the recorded payload is replayed under the original idempotency
key. **Decisions travel through the human session, not the agent
token.**

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent action
  approval", §"Approval decisions travel through the human
  session, not the agent token", §"Action confirmation
  annotation", §"Per-user agent approval mode",
  §"Workspace policy: which actions", §"Flow", §"Model",
  §"Bypass", §"Inline approval UX", §"TTL".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`approval_request`
  (a.k.a. `agent_action`) — fields enumerated in §11 "Model".
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx` (desk),
  `mocks/web/src/components/AgentApprovalModePanel.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/components/AgentSidebar.tsx` (inline cards).

## Implementation notes
- Middleware on every mutating route reads `x-agent-confirm` /
  `x-agent-forbidden` / `x-interactive-only` annotations + the
  workspace always-gated and configurable lists + the calling
  user's mode → decides execute / gate / forbid.
- On gate: write `agent_action` (resolved payload, idempotency
  key, `pre_approval_source`, `inline_channel` from
  `X-Agent-Channel`, `for_user_id`, `resolved_user_mode`,
  rendered `card_*` fields). Return `202 { approval_id, status:
  pending, expires_at }`.
- Notifications: SSE `agent.action.pending` to the delegating
  user's tabs (inline channels) **and** email + `approval.pending`
  webhook to managers (desk).
- Decision endpoints `POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/{approve,deny}`
  reject delegated tokens with `403 approval_requires_session`;
  PATs accepted only when carrying `approvals:act` (off by
  default, never on agent tokens).
- On approve: replay the original handler with the recorded
  payload under the recorded idempotency key; persist
  `result_json`, flip state to `executed`, emit
  `approval.decided`. On reject: emit
  `approval.decided{decision=rejected}`, no replay. On TTL
  expiry: worker flips to `expired`,
  `decision_note_md='auto-expired'`.
- Audit rows: `audit.approval.granted`, `audit.approval.denied`,
  `audit.approval.credential_rejected` (the bypass-attempt
  surface) per the spec.
- TTL default 7 days; per-action overrides allowed via the route
  annotation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A delegated-token write to a route with `x-agent-confirm`
  + caller mode `auto` returns 202 with an `approval_id`.
- [ ] A workspace-always-gated route gates regardless of caller
  mode (including `bypass`).
- [ ] Approve replays the recorded payload exactly once under the
  recorded idempotency key (a duplicate replay is a no-op).
- [ ] Decision endpoints reject delegated-token submissions with
  `403 approval_requires_session` and write
  `audit.approval.credential_rejected`.
- [ ] PAT with `approvals:act` accepted; PAT without is
  rejected.
- [ ] TTL expiry flips state and emits the right event.
- [ ] Inline channel set in `X-Agent-Channel` is persisted on
  the row and used by the SSE payload.
- [ ] Per-user `bypass` does not unlock workspace always-gated
  actions.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/agent/test_approval.py`.
- Integration test: end-to-end agent → 202 → manager approves
  from `/approvals` → row executed → audit chain joinable on
  correlation_id.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(expenses): manager approval inbox + diff view + reimbursement state
description:
## Problem / goal
Managers triage submitted claims: approve, reject (with reason), or
edit-then-approve (diff captured for audit). Approved claims walk
through `approved → reimbursed`; reimbursement records the channel
(cash, bank, card, other) and timestamp.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Approval
  (owner or manager)", §"Reimbursement".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/expenses/models.py`
  (`Expense.status`, `decided_*`, `reimbursed_via`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/expenses/approval.py`:
  - `approve_claim(ctx, expense_id, *, edits=None)` — applies edits
    inline; stores diff in audit row (`expense.approved` with
    `before/after` JSON); status → `approved`.
  - `reject_claim(ctx, expense_id, *, reason_md)` → `rejected`.
  - `mark_reimbursed(ctx, expense_id, *, via, paid_at=None)` →
    `reimbursed`; emits `expense.reimbursed`.
- Inbox query `list_pending(ctx, *, claimant?, property?,
  category?)` ordered by `submitted_at desc`.
- Capability `expense.approve` (Phase 4 enforcement).
- Notification on decision (Phase 10) — emit a domain event
  `expense.decided`; the messaging context turns it into a notif.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] State machine enforced (no skipping states)
- [ ] Edit-on-approve preserves before/after diff in audit
- [ ] Reject without reason rejected with 422
- [ ] Reimbursement updates ledger filters in CSV export
- [ ] Permission gate enforced
- [ ] Events emitted on every decision

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_expense_approval.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tenancy): replace Phase-0 stub with real slug→workspace + membership check
description:
## Problem / goal
Phase 0 shipped a tenancy middleware stub that synthesised a
`WorkspaceContext` from the slug alone. With identity now real,
the middleware must resolve the slug → `workspace`, look up the
authenticated user (session or token), verify a `user_workspace`
membership exists, and bind the full `WorkspaceContext` (including
`is_owner`). Non-members get 404, not 403, so workspace existence is
not leaked.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Multi-tenancy runtime",
  §"Workspace addressing".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Sessions" + §"API
  tokens" auth wiring.
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Constant-time cross-tenant responses".
- **Phase 0**: `app/tenancy/middleware.py` (stub),
  `app/tenancy/context.py::WorkspaceContext`.

## Implementation notes
- Replace `resolve_workspace_stub()` with `resolve_workspace()`:
  - Look up `workspace` by slug; **constant-time** miss handling —
    same response shape + timing as a non-member-of-known-workspace.
  - Resolve actor: session cookie → `user_id`; bearer token →
    `user_id` (api_token) or `delegate_for_user_id` (agent_token).
  - Look up `user_workspace(user_id, workspace_id)`; missing or
    non-`active` → 404 `workspace_not_found`.
  - Compute `is_owner` via `permission_group_member` in `owners@<ws>`.
  - Build `WorkspaceContext(workspace_id, slug, user_id,
    is_owner, actor_kind, agent_label, session_id, token_id)` and
    bind to the request scope.
- Bare-host routes (signup, magic-link, recovery, login) keep using
  `tenant_agnostic()`; the middleware skips them by route prefix.
- Reject mismatched session/token + slug: a session for user X used
  against `/w/<other-slug>/` returns 404 (constant-time).
- Update existing route handlers to assert
  `ctx.user_id is not None` for any non-public route.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Slug miss + non-member responses indistinguishable in body and
      timing (cross-tenant constant-time test from §15 passes)
- [ ] `WorkspaceContext.is_owner` populated correctly for both
      sessions and delegated tokens
- [ ] Bare-host signup / magic-link routes unaffected
- [ ] Phase-0 stub removed; only callers go through the real resolver
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/tenancy/test_middleware_real.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/tenancy/test_membership_404_constant_time.py -xvs
```
Manual: in dev, log in as a member of `wsA`, hit `/w/wsB/today`,
confirm 404 with the same body shape as a slug that doesn't exist.
