title: feat(web/manager): employee detail — profile, schedule, pay, leaves, settings
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager opens one employee's record to manage assignments, pay rules, leave balance, and per-employee settings (notifications, weekend defaults, etc.).

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/user/<eid>` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, EntitySettingsPanel, Chip, Avatar, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/employees/{eid}, GET /api/v1/properties, GET /api/v1/employees/{eid}/settings, GET /api/v1/settings/catalog.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeDetailPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- Tab anchors (`#schedule`, `#pay`, `#leaves`, `#settings`) must remain stable URLs — the mock's deep links are reused by the agent and email digests.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/user/<eid>`.

title: feat(adapters/storage): LocalFsStorage content-addressed blobs
description:
## Problem / goal
Receipts, evidence photos, asset docs, payslip PDFs need a storage
adapter. Default is \`LocalFsStorage\` writing under
\`\$CREWDAY_DATA_DIR/uploads/<first-2-of-hash>/<hash>\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters/storage",
  \`docs/specs/21-assets.md\`.
- Content-addressed by SHA-256 of the bytes.
- \`sign_url\` returns a short-lived \`itsdangerous\`-signed URL routed
  through the API (no direct FS exposure).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/storage/localfs.py\`: \`LocalFsStorage\` implementing
  \`Storage\`. Atomic write: stream to \`<hash>.tmp\`, rename on
  finalize. Dedupe when the hash file already exists.
- \`sign_url\` returns \`/w/<slug>/api/v1/files/<sig>\` paths.
- S3 implementation is specified but out of scope here — leave
  \`app/adapters/storage/s3.py\` as a stub docstring pointing to the
  Phase "deploy" S3 task.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`put\` writes under \`\$DATA/uploads/ab/abcdef\u2026\`
- [ ] Re-putting identical bytes is a no-op (same hash)
- [ ] \`sign_url\` round-trips through a test verifier
- [ ] Unit tests cover put / get / exists / sign + expired-signature
      rejection

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_storage_localfs.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/shared): SchedulerPage — standalone weekly rota grid (read or edit per role)
description:
## Problem / goal
The standalone scheduler is the weekly rota tool: who is on, where,
when. It is mounted in the manager surface for editing and in the
client portal as a read-only view scoped to the client's properties
(first-name + role only per §15 cross-workspace visibility).



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Scheduler" — used in
  workspace setup (manager) and in `ClientLayout` as read-only;
  §15 cross-workspace visibility (`first name + work role only`
  for clients).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx` (scheduler-specific
  selectors live in `mocks/web/src/styles/globals.css` — no
  dedicated `scheduler*.css` file exists; selectors move across with
  the `globals.css` copy in `cd-pjus`).
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `PageHeader`, `Loading`, `useRole`
  (from `@/context/RoleContext`).
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/scheduler/calendar?from=&to=`
  returning `SchedulerCalendarPayload`.
- **Types**: `SchedulerCalendarPayload`, `ScheduleAssignment`,
  `ScheduleRulesetSlot`, `SchedulerTaskView`, `SchedulerUserView`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx`.
- Reuse the existing weekday helpers (`startOfIsoWeek`, `addDays`)
  and the `WEEKDAYS` constant from the mock.
- Role gating: `useRole()` decides edit vs read-only. In the
  client portal the server pre-redacts user names to first-name +
  role only; the client never sees a last name or contact handle.
- SSE invalidation: subscribe to `schedule.assignment.changed`,
  `schedule.ruleset.changed`, `occurrence.changed` to refresh the
  calendar query.
- Mounts under both `/w/:slug/scheduler` (manager) and
  `/w/:slug/scheduler` (client portal) — same component, different
  layout shell.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Weekly grid renders with assignments + rule-set slots
      identical to mock at desktop and tablet breakpoints.
- [ ] Manager role can drag / edit; client role cannot.
- [ ] For clients, no last names or contact info appear in any cell
      (verified by integration test).
- [ ] SSE updates the visible week without a full re-render.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes for both
      manager-mode and client-mode renders.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_scheduler_page.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/scheduler.spec.ts
```

title: feat(domain/places): area service — CRUD scoped to a property with ordering
description:
## Problem / goal
Area CRUD with stable display ordering, a one-level-deep nesting
invariant, and the auto-seed flow for \`kind = str\` properties (Entry,
Kitchen, Living, Bedroom 1, Bathroom 1, Outdoor, Trash & Laundry per
unit).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Area",
  §"Auto-seeded areas".
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"area".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Areas tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (AREAS list).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/area_service.py\` — \`create_area\`,
  \`update_area\`, \`delete_area\`, \`list_areas\`, \`reorder_areas\`,
  \`move_area\`.
- Reorder is bulk: takes \`(area_id, order_hint)\` pairs and writes in
  one transaction; emits a single audit row "reordered N areas".
- Reject moves that would create depth > 1 with \`AreaNestingTooDeep\`.
- Hook into \`property_service.create_property\` and
  \`unit_service.create_unit\` so newly-created \`str\` units get the
  seeded set; respect the table in §"`kind` semantics".
- Workspace-scoped reads via the property join.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Reorder is atomic and emits one audit row
- [ ] Nesting > 1 level is rejected
- [ ] STR property creation auto-seeds the spec area set per unit
- [ ] Cross-workspace access denied
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover happy, depth limit, seed, and denial paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_area_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_area_seeding.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: sec(privacy): PII redactor for outbound LLM payloads, logs, exports
description:
## Problem / goal
PII (legal names, emails, phones, IBAN/PAN, payout account
numbers, door codes, wifi passwords) must never reach an upstream
LLM provider without explicit consent, must never appear in logs,
and must be scrubbed from user-facing exports the same way. A
single redactor module is the seam.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Logging and
  redaction" (regex-match-redact email/phone/IBAN/PAN; redact
  `password|token|secret|cookie|account_number|...` keys at any
  depth), §"Privacy and data rights" (export + erasure paths),
  §"Audit log" (`before_json`/`after_json` pass through redaction).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` consent gating —
  redactor runs on every outbound LLM call; consent flag set per
  workspace lets through specific fields the user opted in to.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/util/redact.py`:
  `redact(payload: Any, *, scope: Literal["log","llm","export"],
  consents: ConsentSet) -> Any`. Walks dict/list, applies key-name
  rules and free-text regexes (email, phone E.164, IBAN, PAN with
  Luhn). Each redacted value becomes `<redacted:reason>` so logs
  remain debuggable.
- Wire into:
  - `structlog` root processor (scope=log)
  - LLM client (scope=llm) — runs after the §11 prompt builder,
    before serialisation; consents read from
    `agent_preferences.upstream_pii_consent` (workspace-scoped)
  - export serialiser (scope=export) — `POST /me/export` and
    CSV exports
  - `audit_log` writer (scope=log) on `before_json`/`after_json`
- Property-based tests with `hypothesis` over realistic payloads
  to catch any unredacted leak across module boundaries.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Email, phone, IBAN, PAN regexes redact in free-text payloads
- [ ] `password`, `token`, `secret`, `cookie`, `account_number`,
      `pan`, `iban` keys redact at any nesting depth
- [ ] LLM payload with consent OFF redacts; with consent set for
      `legal_name` lets that one field through
- [ ] Audit log `before_json`/`after_json` pass through redactor
      before persistence (asserted on a sample row)
- [ ] Export bundle for a user contains no other user's PII
- [ ] Hypothesis fuzzer cannot find a leak after 1000 trials

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/util/test_redact.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/llm/test_outbound_redaction.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/property/test_redact_fuzz.py -xvs
```

title: feat(app): FastAPI factory wiring middleware + routers + worker
description:
## Problem / goal
Central \`create_app()\` composes the FastAPI app: CORS, security
headers, bind-guard, tenancy middleware, SSE handler, SPA catch-all.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"High-level picture",
  §"Repo layout", §"web.*".
- Worker (APScheduler) starts in-process by default; external when
  \`settings.worker = external\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/main.py\`:
  - \`create_app()\` returns \`FastAPI\`.
  - Wires middleware in order: logging/correlation, security headers,
    session, bind-guard, tenancy.
  - Mounts API routers (empty for now; context routers land later).
  - Mounts SSE endpoint at \`/events\` (placeholder).
  - Mounts SPA catch-all serving \`mocks/web/dist\` (temporary; the
    web-foundation task later repoints to \`app/web/dist\`).
  - \`/healthz\`, \`/readyz\`, \`/version\` endpoints.
- Uvicorn entrypoint: \`uv run uvicorn app.main:create_app --factory
  --host \${CREWDAY_BIND_HOST:-127.0.0.1} --port
  \${CREWDAY_BIND_PORT:-8000}\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`GET /healthz\` returns 200
- [ ] \`GET /readyz\` returns 200 when DB up, 503 when down
- [ ] \`GET /version\` returns \`{"version": "..."}\` with the pyproject
      version
- [ ] Visiting \`/\` returns the SPA \`index.html\`
- [ ] Unit tests via \`TestClient\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_main.py -xvs
uv run uvicorn app.main:create_app --factory --port 8000 &
curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8000/healthz
kill %1
\`\`\`

title: feat(i18n): translation store + t(key, params) helper + locale negotiation
description:
## Problem / goal
v1 ships English-only, but every user-facing string must already
flow through a translation seam so that adding fr/es/pt-BR/tl-PH
later is a translation job, not a code refactor. This task lands
the seam: a Babel-backed catalog on the server, a typed `t(key,
params)` helper on the SPA, and a single locale-resolution chain
(per-user pref → `Accept-Language` → workspace default → `en-US`)
shared by both.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/18-i18n.md` §"Library choices" (Babel +
  `lingua` + Jinja2 i18n + ICU MessageFormat), §"Rules followed
  in v1" (every string via `_()`/`t()`, no concat, plural via
  `ngettext`), §"Locale resolution chains" (UI / document /
  notification chains), §"Currency formatting" (Babel
  `format_currency` server, `Intl.NumberFormat` browser).
- **Bounded contexts**: every domain context owns its own message
  keys; the `messaging` and `notifications` contexts (Phase 10)
  consume the server-side renderer for emails.

## Implementation notes
- Server: add `babel` + Jinja2 `i18n` extension. New
  `app/i18n/__init__.py` with `get_locale(request) -> str`,
  `t(key, **params)`, `format_date / format_currency / format_number`
  helpers. Catalog under `app/i18n/locales/<bcp47>/LC_MESSAGES/`.
- Email templates use `{% trans %}` blocks; render path takes a
  `locale` parameter (notification chain) frozen at queue time.
- Web: new `mocks/web/src/i18n/` module with `t(key, params)` hook
  + provider that reads the JSON bundle for the negotiated locale.
  Resolves locale from `users.preferred_locale` (when wired) →
  `navigator.languages` → workspace default → `en-US`.
- Pseudolocale `qps-ploc` (English with accents + 30% inflation)
  enabled by `?locale=qps-ploc` for layout stress-tests.
- `LOCALIZED_ENUMS` catalog so model enum labels live in one place.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `t("login.title")` resolves to the en-US value; missing key
      raises in dev, falls back to the key in prod
- [ ] Locale negotiation chain returns the expected value for each
      precedence step (unit-tested truth table)
- [ ] Email template render produces identical output today as
      before the seam (regression baseline)
- [ ] `?locale=qps-ploc` round-trips through every shell
- [ ] No raw user-facing string survives in any new code added in
      this task (lint pass)
- [ ] Unit tests: `format_currency` matches §"Currency formatting"
      examples for fr-FR vs en-US

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/i18n/test_locale_resolution.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/i18n/test_format_helpers.py -xvs
cd mocks/web && pnpm vitest run src/i18n
```

title: feat(auth/recovery): passkey self-service recovery via magic link
description:
## Problem / goal
A user who lost their passkey-bearing device must be able to recover
without a manager. Email + magic link → enrol a new passkey → revoke
old credentials. Audit must show the chain end-to-end.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-service lost-
  device recovery", §"Recovery paths".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Self-service lost-
  device & email-change abuse mitigations".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/recover/*`.
- Reuses `p2.magic-link` and `p2.passkey.register`.

## Implementation notes
- Endpoints (all bare host, `tenant_agnostic()`):
  - `POST /recover/passkey/request` `{email}` → issues a magic link
    of `purpose=recover_passkey`. Per §15 the email is sent even if
    the address is unknown (no enumeration); audit records what
    actually happened.
  - `GET /recover/passkey/verify?token=…` → consumes the link and
    returns a recovery-scoped session sufficient to enrol a new
    passkey, **not** to make any other workspace mutation.
  - `POST /recover/passkey/finish` → registers a new passkey via the
    bootstrap path, then revokes **all** existing
    `passkey_credential` rows for the user and revokes every
    `sessions` row except the current recovery session, in one tx.
- Throttle by `email_hash` and `ip_hash` (§15 abuse mitigations);
  reuse `app.auth.signup_abuse.check_rate` (rename to neutral name
  if needed).
- Audit: `audit.recovery.requested`, `…verified`, `…completed`,
  with `actor_kind="user"` (or `"system"` for the request step when
  the email is unknown).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Unknown-email request still returns 202; audit row distinguishes
      hit vs miss
- [ ] Successful recovery revokes all old passkeys + sessions for the
      user (single tx)
- [ ] Recovery-scoped session cannot perform any non-recovery action
- [ ] Rate limits enforced (per IP + per email)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_recovery.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py -xvs
```
Manual: simulate device loss in dev — drive `/recover` end to end with
Playwright + a new virtual authenticator.

title: feat(tasks): checklist-template editor + RRULE-filtered items
description:
## Problem / goal
A template's `checklist_template_json` is a JSON array of items, each
with an optional RFC 5545 RRULE that controls whether the item appears
on a given materialised task. The template service needs a typed
editor for these items so the manager UI and the generator both speak
the same shape.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Checklist
  template shape", §"Checklist items" (seeding rules);
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"checklist_item".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` renders
  `tpl.checklist[]` with `label` + optional `guest_visible`;
  `mocks/web/src/components/NewTaskModal.tsx` previews items.

## Implementation notes
- Pydantic model `ChecklistTemplateItem` with fields:
  - `key: str` — stable per-template slug, unique within the template.
    Validate `^[a-z0-9_]{1,64}$` and uniqueness across the array.
  - `text: str`
  - `required: bool` (default `False`)
  - `guest_visible: bool` (default `False`)
  - `rrule: str | None` — optional RFC 5545; validated by parsing
    with `dateutil.rrule.rrulestr` (round-tripped for normalisation).
  - `dtstart_local: date | None` — anchor for the RRULE. Resolution
    rule lives in §06; the template editor accepts an optional date
    only.
- Service helpers in `app/domain/tasks/templates.py`:
  - `validate_checklist_template(items)` — runs at every
    `TaskTemplate{Create,Update}`.
  - `expand_checklist_for_task(items, *, scheduled_for_local,
    is_ad_hoc)` — returns the subset of items to insert as
    `task_checklist_item` rows (used by the generator and stay
    bundle worker; ad-hoc tasks always include every item).
  - `reorder_checklist(items, ordered_keys)` — preserves item
    identity by `key` while updating `ordinal`.
- The `key` is what `asset_action.checklist_item_key` (§21) and
  reports use to track an item across template revisions.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Invalid RRULEs rejected at write time (typed
      `ChecklistRRULEError`, payload includes the bad string)
- [ ] Duplicate or empty `key` rejected
- [ ] `expand_checklist_for_task` filters by RRULE when
      `is_ad_hoc=False`, includes all items when `True`
- [ ] Reorder preserves keys + completion state on the existing rows
- [ ] Unit tests cover RRULE filter on a representative date set
      (monthly, every-Nth-week, every-6-months)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_checklist_template.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/manager): audit log query with actor / action / entity filters
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager queries the workspace's audit log to investigate what happened, when, and by whom — owner-only or scoped by capability.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AuditPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/audit` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, AuditFilterBar.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/audit (with actor / action / entity / since / until query params).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AuditPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The audit query is paginated server-side; reuse the shared infinite-scroll hook used on /scheduler.
- Filter state is URL-driven so audit links from incident reports survive a refresh.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/audit`.

title: feat(web/employee): MePage — profile + avatar + shift clock + personal tokens
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's account hub (§14 worker me). Profile editing, avatar upload, language selection, shift clock-in/clock-out widget, agent approval-mode panel, agent preferences, appearance, chat-channels card, and personal API tokens.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `AgentApprovalModePanel`, `AgentPreferencesPanel`, `AppearancePanel`, `AvatarEditor`, `ChatChannelsMeCard`, `PersonalTokensPanel`, `Chip`, `Loading`, `Pencil` icon. Types: `Me`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`; `PATCH /api/v1/me` (profile updates); `POST /api/v1/me/avatar` (multipart); `POST /api/v1/me/shifts/clock-in` + `/clock-out`; `GET|POST|DELETE /api/v1/me/tokens`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- The page composes many sub-panels; copy each as it stands.
- Personal tokens panel must show prefix only after creation (secret displayed once).
- Subscribe to SSE `shift.updated` to keep the clock widget honest.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/MePage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/MePage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/MePage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/MePage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(logging): structured JSON logger + secret redaction filter
description:
## Problem / goal
Every mutation is audited (§15) and every secret must stay out of logs.
A central structured-logger setup with a secret-redaction filter is the
foundation.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Secrets never logged",
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants" #7.
- Redactor masks: \`token\`, \`cookie\`, \`Authorization\`, \`passkey
  credential id\`, anything matching \`SecretStr\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/util/logging.py\` with \`setup_logging(level: str)\`:
  - Root logger \u2192 JSON formatter (structlog-compatible shape with
    \`time\`, \`level\`, \`logger\`, \`msg\`, plus any kwargs).
  - Attach \`RedactionFilter\` that scans every record's \`args\`,
    \`kwargs\`, and serialized \`msg\` for the masked patterns and
    replaces with \`***REDACTED***\`.
  - Bind the correlation id (\`audit_correlation_id\`) into the record
    if a context var is set.
- \`app/main.py\` calls \`setup_logging\` at startup.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`logger.info("auth ok", token="s3cr3t")\` logs \`***REDACTED***\`
- [ ] JSON output parses as valid JSON per line
- [ ] Correlation id threaded via context var appears in records
- [ ] Unit tests cover every redaction pattern

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_logging.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(payroll/payslip): PDF generation (weasyprint) + Storage put
description:
## Problem / goal
After a payslip is computed, render a PDF (legal payslip layout) and
store it via the Storage port. The blob hash is recorded on the
payslip row for retrieval.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"PDF",
  §"Distribution".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`Payslip.pdf_blob_hash`).
- **Storage port**: from Phase 0 (`p0.storage`).

## Implementation notes
- Add `weasyprint` to runtime deps (use `uv add`); document the
  system fonts/cairo dependency in the Dockerfile (already
  installed for the FastAPI image — verify).
- Template `app/adapters/pdf/templates/payslip.html` (Jinja2);
  workspace-tunable header (logo, registered name, address) read
  from workspace settings.
- Service `app/domain/payroll/pdf.py::render_payslip(ctx, payslip_id)`:
  - Loads payslip + components + workspace + user.
  - Renders PDF bytes.
  - `storage.put(...)` returns blob hash → updates
    `payslip.pdf_blob_hash`.
  - Idempotent: if hash already set, return without re-rendering
    unless `force=True`.
- Capability check: only managers + the recipient may fetch the PDF.
- Audit `payslip.pdf_rendered`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] PDF renders for the §09 canonical test fixture
- [ ] Hash stored on payslip row; storage object retrievable
- [ ] Idempotent regeneration
- [ ] Permission denied for unrelated users
- [ ] Worker can fetch own payslip PDF

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_payslip_pdf.py -xvs
# manual smoke: open the PDF and check layout
```

title: feat(adapters/llm): OpenRouter client
description:
## Problem / goal
OCR + NL task intake + digest + staff chat all run via OpenRouter.
Adapter handles API key auth, model routing, streaming, usage accounting.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md\` §"Providers",
  §"Model router", \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters/llm".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/llm/openrouter.py\`: \`OpenRouterClient\` impl using
  \`httpx.AsyncClient\`. Methods: \`complete\`, \`chat\`, \`ocr\`,
  \`stream_chat\`. Return a \`LlmResponse\` with \`tokens_in\`,
  \`tokens_out\`, \`cost_cents\`, \`model_id\`, \`latency_ms\`.
- Secrets via \`settings.openrouter_api_key\`.
- Rate-limit handling: retry w/ backoff on 429, surface
  \`LlmRateLimited\` to the caller after exhaustion.
- Capability router (budget-aware model pick) is in
  \`app/domain/llm/\` — that's a later Phase 12 task; the adapter just
  dispatches.
- Record-replay: pytest fixture that records real calls into
  \`tests/fixtures/llm/*.json\` and replays without a network in CI.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Recorded fixture test runs in CI without network access
- [ ] 429 triggers retry + eventual \`LlmRateLimited\`
- [ ] Usage stats are populated on every response
- [ ] Unit tests cover complete / chat / ocr + streaming + error paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_llm_openrouter.py -xvs
\`\`\`

- NITPICKS (skipped, non-load-bearing): no .python-version pin (spec only requires >=3.12); Makefile lacks help/all target; pytest addopts not set.

Notes verified:

- uv sync (0.11.6) includes dev group by default; no --group dev needed.
- mypy app traverses app/__init__.py correctly (verified with mypy -v).
- Ruff default src=['./src','.'] picks up app/ as first-party; explicit src= not required.

No code changes applied; skeleton ships as-committed under cd-cea.

title: feat(events): typed event registry + in-process synchronous bus
description:
## Problem / goal
Cross-context writes flow through events. v1 is synchronous and
in-process, but the contract is transport-agnostic.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Boundary rules" #3.
- Every typed event has: \`name\` (dotted), \`workspace_id\`,
  \`actor_id\`, \`correlation_id\`, \`payload\` (pydantic model).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/events/registry.py\`: \`@event class TaskCompleted(BaseModel):
  ...\` decorator that registers event types under a stable name and
  exposes a schema for serialization.
- \`app/events/bus.py\`: \`EventBus\` with \`subscribe(event_type,
  handler)\` (decorator) and \`publish(event)\`. Synchronous dispatch
  within the current UoW. If a handler raises, the UoW rolls back.
- \`app/events/__init__.py\` re-exports the decorator + bus instance.
- Initial registered events (placeholders; real payloads per spec):
  \`task.created\`, \`task.completed\`, \`task.overdue\`,
  \`stay.upcoming\`, \`expense.approved\`, \`shift.ended\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Registering an event type twice raises at import time
- [ ] Publishing an event invokes every subscriber synchronously, in
      deterministic order
- [ ] A subscriber exception bubbles up (rolls back the UoW)
- [ ] Unit tests cover register / subscribe / publish / ordering

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_events.py -xvs
\`\`\`

Workflow YAML itself parses cleanly with 5 jobs (lint/type/boundaries/unit/integration), Python 3.12+3.13, SQLite+PG matrix, boundaries continue-on-error until cd-ev0. All local gates pass (ruff, format, mypy, pytest 633, tenant_agnostic).

title: feat(auth/tokens): API token mint / list / revoke (sha256 hash + prefix)
description:
## Problem / goal
Workspace-scoped API tokens for managers and headless integrations.
Mint shows the plaintext exactly once; list shows only the prefix;
revoke is an audit-tracked, irreversible flag.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"API tokens" /
  §"Creation", §"Scopes", §"Usage", §"Revocation and rotation",
  §"Observability fields on `api_token`", §"Guardrails".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/tokens/*`.
- Phase 1 table: `api_token` (id, user_id, workspace_id, label,
  scope_json, prefix, hash, expires_at, last_used_at, revoked_at).

## Implementation notes
- Routes:
  - `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens` `{name, scopes,
    expires_at, note}` → returns `{token, key_id, prefix,
    expires_at}` once.
  - `GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens` → list (no secret).
  - `DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens/{id}` → sets `revoked_at`
    (no hard delete; preserves audit links).
- Token format per §03: `mip_<key_id>_<secret>`; `key_id` ULID,
  `secret` 256 bits base32. Store argon2id hash of the secret per
  §15 §"Token hashing"; `prefix` = first 8 chars of `secret` for
  list display. (Fix the spec/Phase-1 mismatch on hash function in
  the PR — argon2id, not sha256.)
- Permission gate: `api_tokens.manage` action (default: owners +
  managers, §05).
- `expires_at`: required; default 90 days; workspace cap
  enforced (§03 Guardrails). "never" requires the noisy warning
  flag.
- Service signature `mint(ctx: WorkspaceContext, …) -> MintedToken`;
  audit row `audit.api_token.minted` written in the same tx.
- Auth middleware: parse `Authorization: Bearer mip_<key>_<secret>`;
  O(1) lookup by `key_id`; verify `argon2id`; bump `last_used_at`
  every N minutes (debounced, not every request).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Plaintext returned exactly once; never logged or stored
- [ ] List endpoint returns prefix + label, never the secret
- [ ] Revoke is idempotent and immediate (cached parses invalidate)
- [ ] Token auth path is constant-time on bad secret (no early return)
- [ ] Permission gate enforced; non-managers get 403
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_api_token.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_api_token_auth.py -xvs
```
Manual: mint a token in dev, hit `/w/<slug>/api/v1/healthz`
authenticated, then revoke and confirm 401 on next call.

title: chore(repo): bootstrap pyproject.toml + uv + ruff + mypy + pytest
description:
## Problem / goal
Stand up the Python project skeleton so every subsequent Beads task has
a runnable, lintable, typeable, testable tree. There is no real code
yet — this is the foundation.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime dependencies",
  §"Repo layout", §"Environments".
- **Stack**: Python 3.12+, \`uv\` package manager, \`ruff\` (lint +
  format), \`mypy --strict\`, \`pytest\` + \`pytest-asyncio\`.
- **Existing mocks pyproject** at \`mocks/app/pyproject.toml\` — pin
  families should match; do not duplicate mocks deps.

## Implementation notes
- Create \`pyproject.toml\` at the repo root with:
  - \`project\` table (name \`crewday\`, python \`>=3.12\`, Apache-2.0).
  - Runtime deps pinned per §01: \`fastapi>=0.115\`, \`uvicorn[standard]\`,
    \`sqlalchemy>=2.0\`, \`alembic\`, \`pydantic>=2.6\`,
    \`pydantic-settings\`, \`python-multipart\`, \`webauthn\`,
    \`python-ulid\`, \`apscheduler\`, \`httpx\`, \`icalendar\`,
    \`python-dateutil\`, \`itsdangerous\`, \`weasyprint\`, \`click\`.
  - Dev deps: \`pytest\`, \`pytest-asyncio\`, \`pytest-cov\`,
    \`schemathesis\`, \`playwright\`, \`ruff\`, \`mypy\`,
    \`import-linter\`, \`locust\`.
  - \`[tool.ruff]\` with \`line-length=88\`, target \`py312\`; enable E,
    F, I, UP, B, SIM, RUF rulesets.
  - \`[tool.mypy]\` with \`strict = true\`, \`plugins = ["pydantic.mypy"]\`.
  - \`[tool.pytest.ini_options]\` with \`testpaths = ["tests"]\`,
    \`asyncio_mode = "auto"\`.
- Generate \`uv.lock\` via \`uv lock\`.
- Ship a top-level \`Makefile\` with \`lint\`, \`type\`, \`test\`,
  \`fmt\` targets invoking \`uv run ruff\`, \`uv run mypy app\`,
  \`uv run pytest\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`uv sync\` succeeds in a clean venv
- [ ] \`uv run ruff check .\` passes on the empty tree
- [ ] \`uv run mypy app\` passes (no app code yet, but the invocation works)
- [ ] \`uv run pytest\` reports 0 tests, 0 failures
- [ ] \`uv.lock\` is committed

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv sync
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy app || true   # no modules yet
uv run pytest
\`\`\`

title: feat(tasks): task-scoped comments + agent thread
description:
## Problem / goal
Per-task notes are the agent inbox: every message is a row in
`task_comment` ordered by `created_at`, kind `user | agent | system`,
with `@mentions` resolving to workspace members. This service backs
the `Notes (chat)` panel on the worker task-detail page and the
manager's task drawer.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  §"Task notes are the agent inbox", §"Comments";
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"comment".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`
  uses `ChatLog` + `ChatComposer` at `/api/v1/tasks/{id}/chat/log`.
- Email fallback for offline `@mentions` lives in §10
  (out-of-scope here — emit an event the messaging service
  consumes).

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/comments.py`.
- DTOs: `CommentCreate` (markdown body, optional attachments as
  evidence-style `file_id`s), `CommentView` (kind, author, body,
  created_at, attachments, mentions).
- Functions:
  - `post_comment(ctx, task_id, payload, *, kind='user') ->
    CommentView`. `kind='agent'` requires that the caller is the
    embedded workspace agent (token-checked at the route layer);
    `kind='system'` is internal-only (called by the completion /
    assignment services to render state-change markers inline).
  - `list_comments(ctx, task_id, *, after: datetime | None)` —
    paginated, oldest-first.
  - `edit_comment(ctx, comment_id, body)` — only the author within
    a small grace window; never edits agent / system rows.
  - `delete_comment(ctx, comment_id)` — soft; author or
    workspace owner.
- `@mention` resolution: parse `@<slug>` patterns to user ids in
  the workspace; refuse mentions of users not in the workspace.
  Persist resolved ids in `mentioned_user_ids` for the messaging
  fanout.
- Attachments reuse the file storage from `p5.evidence` — keep
  the upload path identical (one file pipeline, two consumers).
- Emit `task.comment_added` on the bus (consumed by §10 for
  offline-mention email fallback). Audit `task_comment.create |
  edit | delete`.
- WorkspaceContext required; personal-task visibility (§06
  §"Self-created and personal tasks") gates read access — only
  the creator and workspace owners see comments on a personal task.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `kind='agent' | 'system'` paths gated by the right caller
- [ ] `@mentions` rejected for non-members
- [ ] Edit window enforced; agent/system rows never editable
- [ ] Attachments share the storage path with `p5.evidence`
- [ ] Personal-task visibility honoured on list/post
- [ ] Event + audit per mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover each path

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_comments.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/admin): AgentDocsPage — agent virtual-file browser + OpenAPI docs
description:
## Problem / goal
The chat agents read system-side virtual files on demand (§11 Agent
knowledge tools). Operators need to inspect what the agents see and
to browse the live OpenAPI spec — this page is the read-side.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/agent-docs`; §11 LLM and agents §"Agent knowledge tools";
  §02 `agent_doc` table.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AgentDocsPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`.
- **API endpoints**:
  `GET /admin/api/v1/agent_docs` (summary list),
  `GET /admin/api/v1/agent_docs/{slug}` (single doc),
  `GET /openapi.json` (OpenAPI spec, browser-rendered).
- **Types**: `AgentDoc`, `AgentDocSummary`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/admin/AgentDocsPage.tsx`.
- Two-pane layout: list of docs on the left, selected doc body on
  the right. Lazy-load the doc body via the `enabled` query option
  when `activeSlug != null` (matches mock).
- Render markdown via the shared `MarkdownView` component (Phase 16)
  for parity with worker-app instructions.
- Embed an iframe (or a minimal client-side renderer) for
  `/openapi.json` so operators can browse endpoints without
  leaving the admin shell.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] List + detail view layout matches mock.
- [ ] Selecting a doc fetches and renders the markdown body.
- [ ] OpenAPI browser loads `/openapi.json` and is searchable.
- [ ] Permission gate: deployment-admin only.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_agent_docs.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-agent-docs.spec.ts
```

title: feat(authz/owners): owners-group bootstrap + governance + WorkspaceContext flag
description:
## Problem / goal
The `owners` group is the workspace's root authority. It must be
created on every workspace bootstrap, protected from deletion, and the
"is owner" bit must be carried on `WorkspaceContext` so downstream
authority checks are O(1).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md`
  §"Permissions: surface, groups, and action catalog"
  (system groups), §"Root-only actions (governance)".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-serve signup"
  step 2 (places the first user in `owners@<ws>`).
- **Phase 0**: `app.tenancy.context.WorkspaceContext`.
- Reuses `p2.permission-groups` for the protected-group machinery.

## Implementation notes
- Hook `bootstrap_owners_group(ws_id, first_user_id)` invoked from:
  - `bootstrap_workspace` fixture (test path).
  - `p2.signup` `GET /signup/verify` transaction.
  - First-owner self-hosted bootstrap (§03 §"First owner").
- The hook seeds `owners@<ws>` (system=True, slug=`owners`,
  capabilities = full action catalog) + the four other system groups
  (`managers`, `workers`, `clients`, `chat-gateway-agents`). Slugs
  are workspace-namespaced via the `workspace_id` foreign key — no
  "@" substrings in storage.
- Last-owner protection: removing the only member of `owners@<ws>`
  via `p2.permission-groups` returns 409 `last_owner_member`. This
  is a separate guard from `p2.role-grants`'s last-owner-grant rule
  — both must hold.
- `WorkspaceContext.is_owner` populated at request time by checking
  `permission_group_member` membership in `owners@<ws>`. Cache for
  the lifetime of the request.
- Audit: `audit.workspace.owners_bootstrapped` written once per
  workspace, in the same tx as the seed inserts.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every new workspace has all five system groups seeded
- [ ] First user is a member of `owners@<ws>`
- [ ] Removing the last owner member → 409
- [ ] `WorkspaceContext.is_owner` is True for owners, False otherwise
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_owners_bootstrap.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_owners_governance.py -xvs
```
Manual: in the dev container, complete signup, then attempt to leave
the owners group via the UI — confirm refusal.

title: feat(cli/config): ~/.config/crewday/profiles.toml multi-environment + default switching
description:
## Problem / goal
Users juggle multiple environments (dev / staging / prod / their own
demo workspace). `crewday auth login` writes a profile entry; `crewday
config use <name>` switches the default. The active profile drives
base URL, token, default workspace, and output preference.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Profiles", §"Config file".
- **XDG**: `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/crewday/profiles.toml` (fallback
  `~/.config/crewday/profiles.toml`); permissions `0600`.

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_config.py`:
  - Dataclass `Profile(name, base_url, token, default_workspace,
    output, ca_bundle)`.
  - `load() -> Config` reads TOML; `save(cfg)` rewrites with `0600`
    perms (atomic via tempfile + rename).
  - `active(cli_override) -> Profile` honours `--profile`,
    `CREWDAY_PROFILE` env, then `default = "<name>"` in the file.
  - Subcommands wired into `_main.py`:
    - `crewday config show` — print active profile (token redacted)
    - `crewday config list` — list all profiles
    - `crewday config use <name>` — set default
    - `crewday config rm <name>` — remove
  - Token storage: prefer OS keyring (`keyring` package) when
    available; fall back to TOML with file perms enforced.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] TOML round-trip preserves field order
- [ ] File perms forced to `0600` on every write
- [ ] `--profile` > `CREWDAY_PROFILE` > `default` precedence
- [ ] `config show` redacts the token (last 4 chars only)
- [ ] Unit tests cover precedence, redaction, atomic write

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_config.py -xvs
crewday config list
crewday config use dev
```

title: feat(transport/sse): /w/<slug>/events stream with role + kind allowlist
description:
## Problem / goal
A single `EventSource('/w/<slug>/events')` per browser tab
carries every cross-client coherence signal: TanStack Query
invalidations, agent turn indicators, pending approvals, LLM
budget refused notices. Filters by the connecting actor's role
and an event-kind allowlist so a worker does not see manager-only
streams.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent turn
  lifecycle" (`agent.turn.started/finished`),
  §"Inline approval UX" (`agent.action.pending`),
  §"Workspace usage budget" (`llm.budget_exceeded` log /
  client banner), §"Embedded agents" (`agent.message.appended`).
- Frontend spec: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (TanStack
  Query invalidation feed).
- Cross-ref: `p0.events` (in-process pub/sub) is the source;
  this task wires the HTTP transport.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/api/transport/sse.py` with
  `@router.get('/w/{slug}/events')` returning a
  `text/event-stream` response.
- Resolves the calling user → membership in workspace `slug` →
  effective role; subscribes to `p0.events` and forwards only
  events matching `{role}` + the per-event `kind` allowlist
  (declared on the event class).
- Scoped events (e.g. `agent.turn.*`,
  `agent.action.pending`, `agent.message.appended`) carry an
  `actor_user_id` and are forwarded only to the matching
  user's tabs.
- Heartbeat comment every 15 s; reconnection via standard
  `Last-Event-ID` (events buffered in-memory for 60 s).
- Resilience: backpressure drops the slowest consumer with a
  closing `event: dropped` frame; client reconnects.
- Companion endpoint `/admin/events` for the deployment-admin
  surface; same code, different scope (no workspace slug).
- TanStack Query integration shipped as part of the web bundle:
  on each event, call `queryClient.invalidateQueries` keyed by
  the event's `invalidates` field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Two tabs of the same user receive identical event
  streams.
- [ ] A worker tab does not receive a manager-only event.
- [ ] An `agent.action.pending` event scoped to user A is not
  delivered to user B.
- [ ] Heartbeat keeps an idle connection alive past common
  proxy timeouts (60+ s).
- [ ] `Last-Event-ID` reconnect replays the missed events
  within the 60 s buffer.
- [ ] Slow consumer drops cleanly; the rest of the fanout is
  not blocked.
- [ ] No PII in event payloads beyond what the user could
  already read.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/api/transport/test_sse.py`.
- Integration test: two browsers (Playwright) on the same
  workspace see a TanStack invalidation fire when one creates a
  task.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(db/llm): model_assignment + agent_token + approval_request + llm_usage + budget_ledger
description:
## Problem / goal
Tables supporting the LLM/agent layer — capability→model map, agent
tokens (delegated), human-in-the-loop approvals, per-call usage, and
budget ledger.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"model_assignment",
  §"agent_token", §"approval_request", §"llm_usage",
  §"budget_ledger", \`docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md\`.

title: feat(web/public): AcceptPage — invite acceptance via token
description:
## Problem / goal
Click-to-accept invitation surface (§03 'Additional users — invite -> click-to-accept'). One URL, two rendered states: 'new' chains straight into passkey registration; 'existing' shows an Acceptance card itemising grants and signs the user in with their existing passkey. Pending grants only activate on the explicit Accept.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `KeyRound` (lucide), `useParams` for the token, `useSearchParams` for the demo `state` query param.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/invites/{token}` (returns invite + state new|existing), `POST /api/v1/invites/{token}/accept`, `POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/finish` (registration ceremony for new users).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- The real app reads the invite record server-side and decides which branch to render — the `state` query param in the mock is a demo convenience only.
- On accept, grants are activated and the user is redirected to their workspace landing page.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-public/AcceptPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-public/AcceptPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-public/AcceptPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/public/AcceptPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(auth/session): cookie lifecycle + sliding refresh + idle/absolute timeouts
description:
## Problem / goal
Issue, refresh, and revoke web sessions. The session cookie is the
substrate every authenticated request rides on; sliding refresh keeps
working users logged in, idle/absolute timeouts cap the blast radius.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Sessions", §"Login",
  §"Recovery paths" (logout-everywhere semantics).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Cookies",
  §"HTTP security headers".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/lib/session.ts` (placeholder).
- Phase 1 table: `Session` (id, user_id, workspace_id, expires_at,
  last_seen_at, ua_hash, ip_hash).

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/auth/session.py`:
  - `issue(user_id, *, workspace_id=None, has_owner_grant: bool)`
    creates the row + sets cookie. Lifetime: 7d when
    `has_owner_grant`, otherwise 30d (configurable via Settings).
  - `refresh(session_id)` extends `expires_at` only after half the
    lifetime has elapsed; updates `last_seen_at` on every hit.
  - `revoke(session_id)` and `revoke_all_for_user(user_id)`.
- Cookie name `__Host-crewday_session`; flags `Secure; HttpOnly;
  SameSite=Lax; Path=/`; **no** `Domain`. Value is a 192-bit random
  token; row stores its sha256.
- CSRF double-submit: emit `crewday_csrf` cookie + require `X-CSRF`
  header on every non-GET (middleware in `app/auth/csrf.py`).
- `ua_hash` = sha256(User-Agent), `ip_hash` = sha256(client_ip + salt)
  — never the raw values (§15 redaction).
- Audit: `audit.session.created`, `…revoked`, `…expired` via
  `app.audit.write_audit`.
- Logging: structured event on issue/refresh/revoke
  (`p0.logging` redaction layer).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Owner / non-owner lifetimes match spec (7d / 30d)
- [ ] Sliding refresh only fires past the half-life mark
- [ ] Cookie flags exactly per spec; rejected if `Domain` is set
- [ ] CSRF double-submit enforced for non-GET routes
- [ ] `revoke_all_for_user` invalidates concurrent sessions
- [ ] No raw IP / UA persisted; only hashes
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DB backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_session.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_session_csrf.py -xvs
```
Manual: `curl -c jar -b jar` round-trip through `/login` (test
fixture) and confirm cookie flags via `curl -v`.

title: feat(domain/stays): poll_ical APScheduler job — fetch, parse, dedupe, upsert
description:
## Problem / goal
Recurring background job that polls every enabled \`ical_feed\`,
parses VEVENTs with \`icalendar\`, dedupes by \`(feed_id, external_uid)\`,
upserts \`reservation\` rows, and converts "Blocked"/"Not available"
VEVENTs to \`property_closure\` rows. Publishes
\`reservation.upserted\` per change for downstream turnover gen.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"iCal feed" →
  "Polling behavior" (covers \`If-None-Match\`/\`If-Modified-Since\`,
  Blocked detection, rate limiting, parse-error issues).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"reservation",
  §"ical_feed", §"property_closure".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx\` (rendered
  feed-driven stays).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/jobs/poll_ical.py\` — APScheduler job; honours
  \`ical_feed.poll_cadence\` (default \`*/15 * * * *\`).
- Fetch via the validated SSRF-guarded HTTP client (re-using the
  validator from \`p3.ical.registration\`); pin resolved IP between
  resolve and connect.
- Parse with \`icalendar\`. Dedupe key in DB:
  \`UNIQUE(ical_feed_id, external_uid)\`; use \`INSERT … ON CONFLICT
  DO UPDATE\` (Postgres) / \`INSERT OR REPLACE\` (SQLite, portable
  via SQLAlchemy upsert).
- Convert VEVENTs whose \`SUMMARY\` matches the spec patterns
  ("Not available", "Blocked", "Airbnb (Not available)") to
  \`property_closure\` with \`reason = ical_unavailable\` instead.
- Publish \`reservation.upserted\` with \`{reservation_id,
  feed_id, change_kind: created|updated|cancelled}\`; publish
  \`property.closure.created\` when a Blocked VEVENT yields a closure.
- Surface parse failures as an \`issue\` row against the feed; record
  \`last_error\` on the feed.
- Per-host rate limit; respect \`Retry-After\` on 429.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Re-poll is idempotent (same VEVENT → no duplicate row, no event)
- [ ] Cancelled VEVENT marks \`reservation.status = cancelled\`
- [ ] Blocked VEVENTs become closures, not reservations
- [ ] Events published per change with the documented payload shape
- [ ] Parse error increments \`last_error\`, doesn't poison the loop
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests against captured Airbnb/VRBO/Booking/Google fixtures

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/jobs/test_poll_ical.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_ical_poll_idempotent.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(chat-gateway): event-bus subscriber that dispatches inbound chat to agent runtime
description:
## Problem / goal
Decouple the webhook from agent execution. A subscriber on
\`chat.message.received\` enqueues an agent dispatch job and stamps
the message with \`dispatched_to_agent_at\` for the audit trail.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"Routing" →
  §"Inbound", §"Events".
- Recent commit \`f869841 spec(chat-gateway): decouple agent
  dispatch from webhook request\` codified this split — preserve
  it.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/chat_gateway/dispatcher.py\` registered against
  the event bus on \`chat.message.received\`.
- Loads the message + binding context (workspace, channel, source
  provider, language hint) and enqueues an
  \`AgentDispatchJob(message_id, capability='inbound_chat')\` for
  the agent runtime worker (Phase 12).
- On enqueue success, stamps \`chat_message.dispatched_to_agent_at\`
  (UTC).
- On exception: logs + audits \`chat_gateway.dispatch.failed\` and
  leaves \`dispatched_to_agent_at\` NULL so the sweep job retries
  (see \`p13.gateway.sweep\`).
- Idempotent on \`message_id\` (don't double-stamp; don't double-
  enqueue when the row is already dispatched).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One enqueue per inbound message under steady state
- [ ] \`dispatched_to_agent_at\` set iff enqueue succeeded
- [ ] Failed enqueue leaves the field NULL + emits failure audit
- [ ] Re-firing the event for an already-dispatched message is a
      no-op
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy-path, failure-path, and idempotency

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_dispatcher.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/chat_gateway/test_dispatcher_event.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(agent/preferences): per-workspace tone, approval mode, blocked actions
description:
## Problem / goal
Workspace owners and managers tune their embedded agent: tone
(plain / formal / playful), default approval mode for new users,
and a denylist of capabilities/actions the agent will refuse even
if the calling user could in principle perform them. Layered with
property and per-user preferences (§11 "Layers").

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent preferences"
  (Layers, Workspace preferences, Property preferences, Your
  preferences, Which capabilities receive preferences,
  Authoring and visibility, PII posture, Size budget, Storage
  versioning audit, Not a substitute for structured rules).
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`workspace.settings`
  (extends with `agent_prefs_md`, `agent_blocked_actions[]`,
  `default_approval_mode`).
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/components/AgentPreferencesPanel.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/components/AgentApprovalModePanel.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Add columns / settings keys: `agent_prefs_md` (markdown,
  capped at the per-spec size budget), `agent_blocked_actions`
  (jsonb array of operationId or capability keys),
  `default_approval_mode` ('bypass'|'auto'|'strict', default
  'auto').
- Resolver `resolve_preferences(ctx, capability) ->
  PreferenceBundle` walks workspace → property (when in scope)
  → user, concatenating prefs in the documented order.
- Runtime injects `PreferenceBundle.text` into the system
  prompt under a stable header so prompt caching works; blocked
  actions short-circuit at tool-dispatch time with
  `403 action_blocked_by_preferences`.
- CRUD via `PUT /api/v1/workspace/agent_prefs` (manager+),
  `PUT /api/v1/users/me/agent_prefs` (self). Mutations write
  `audit.workspace.agent_prefs_updated` with the diff.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Preferences round-trip via API; the new default approval
  mode applies to **new** users (existing users keep their mode).
- [ ] Blocked-action list refuses the matching tool dispatch with
  the documented 403.
- [ ] Resolver concatenates layers in the spec order; size budget
  enforced.
- [ ] Audit row written on every change.
- [ ] Empty preferences ship a stable empty header (so prompt
  cache key is stable).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/agent/test_preferences.py`.
- Integration test: changing tone in the panel reflects in the
  next agent reply (LLM stub).

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: test(util/hashids): replace flaky random-byte flip with deterministic tamper
description: tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid can pass even on a flipped byte because the random flip may re-sign a valid signature. Replace the random.randint byte flip with a deterministic mutation of the signature portion so the test is 100% reliable. Discovered during cd-n0l selfreview.

title: refactor(authz/groups): replace direct adapter import with PermissionGroupRepository Protocol
description:
## Problem

v1 ships `app.domain.identity.permission_groups` with a direct SQLAlchemy model import from `app.adapters.db.authz.models`. This violates import-linter contract 1 (`app.domain -> app.adapters`); the violation is currently masked by a narrow `ignore_imports` exception in `pyproject.toml` (cd-zkr stopgap).

## Goal

Introduce the spec-sanctioned Protocol seam so the domain module stops touching adapters directly:

1. Declare `PermissionGroupRepository` (+ member sub-protocol) under `app/adapters/db/authz/ports.py`.
2. Implement `SqlAlchemyPermissionGroupRepository` under `app/adapters/db/authz/repositories.py`.
3. Migrate `app/domain/identity/permission_groups.py` to accept `repo: PermissionGroupRepository` instead of an SA session; pipe audit through a similar port if needed.
4. Update `pyproject.toml` — drop the `app.domain.identity.permission_groups -> app.adapters.db.authz.models` ignore_imports entry.
5. Every call site (integration tests, future routers) wires the concrete SA-backed repo.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `permission_groups.py` has no `app.adapters` imports (only `app.adapters.db.authz.ports` indirectly via the Protocol).
- [ ] The ignore_imports stopgap is gone; `uv run lint-imports` passes without it.
- [ ] Unit tests inject a stub repo; integration tests wire the real SA repo.
- [ ] No behaviour change on the public surface (signatures + error shapes + audit semantics match).

## Context
- Spec: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` sec. "Adapters" and "Shared kernel" (Protocol seam rule).
- Related: cd-zkr (v1 implementation that introduced the stopgap).

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Scope extended by cd-79r work (role-grants service). The same stopgap now covers:
- app.domain.identity.role_grants -> app.adapters.db.authz.models (RoleGrant + PermissionGroup + PermissionGroupMember reads for the owner-authority policy)
- app.domain.identity.role_grants -> app.adapters.db.places.models (PropertyWorkspace scope check)

When the PermissionGroupRepository Protocol seam lands, please also introduce a RoleGrantRepository (and reuse the same member-lookup surface for the owner-authority policy) so both ignore_imports entries drop in one go. See pyproject.toml lines under the '[tool.importlinter.contracts]' block.

title: feat(employees): user CRUD + invite + work-engagement + avatar upload
description:
## Problem / goal
Owners and managers need to add people to the workspace, edit their
profile, deactivate (archive) them, and let each person upload their
own avatar. v1 collapses "employee" into `users` + per-workspace
`work_engagement` (see §05 "User (as worker)"); the service must keep
those rows consistent across the lifecycle.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"User (as worker)",
  §"Work engagement", §"Archive / reinstate"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"users", §"user_workspace", §"role_grants"; `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md`
  §"Invite + magic link" for the invite token flow; `docs/specs/12-api.md`
  §"/users", §"/me/avatar".
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeesPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Blob storage**: `app/adapters/storage` from `p0.storage` (avatars
  hashed + content-addressed, served via signed URL).

## Implementation notes
- New service `app/services/employees/service.py` taking
  `WorkspaceContext`; expose `invite`, `update_profile`,
  `archive`, `reinstate`, `set_avatar`, `clear_avatar`.
- Invite flow issues a magic-link token (re-use the auth helper from
  Phase 2 once landed; until then, stub a deterministic token + TODO
  marker — do NOT inline a duplicate token mint).
- Archive is soft (`users.archived_at`) AND archives the
  `work_engagement` for this workspace; revoking sessions /
  passkeys is identity-side and out of scope here (covered by
  §05 "Archive a `users` row" deployment-wide path — reference but
  defer).
- Avatar upload: `POST /me/avatar` is self-only (§05 worker surface
  rule). Manager update of someone else's profile (display name,
  email, timezone) does NOT include avatar replacement.
- Every mutation writes an `audit` row via `app/adapters/audit`.
- All reads/writes pass through the tenancy filter; no raw
  `select(User)` without `WorkspaceContext`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/users/invite` creates a `users` row
      (or reuses existing one), `user_workspace` row, and a pending
      `work_engagement` for that workspace.
- [ ] `PATCH /workspaces/{ws}/users/{id}` updates only fields the
      caller is permitted to (`users.edit_profile_other` for
      managers; identity-scoped self-edit otherwise).
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/users/{id}/archive` archives engagement
      + every `user_work_role` in this workspace; idempotent.
- [ ] `POST /me/avatar` accepts an image (≤ 2 MB), hashes it, stores
      the blob, updates `users.avatar_blob_hash`. Returns the signed
      URL. `DELETE /me/avatar` clears it.
- [ ] Audit rows created for every mutation, including reinstate.
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy path + idempotent archive + avatar
      replacement (old blob orphaned, not deleted — GC is separate).

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_service_employees.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_employees.py -xvs
```

title: feat(authz): permission enforcement dependency for routers
description:
## Problem / goal
Every protected endpoint needs the same authorization decision: does
the caller hold a `role_grants` row + appropriate `permission_rule` /
`owners` membership for this action on this scope? Today each router
would re-implement the check; we need one canonical FastAPI
dependency / decorator that takes an `action_key` and refuses
calls that fail.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"Permission resolution",
  `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Action catalog"
  (canonical action keys), §"How a rule narrows or widens a default";
  `docs/specs/15-audit-and-rls.md` for audit row format.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PermissionsPage.tsx`
  (manager UI inspecting effective rules — this task only ships the
  enforcement engine the page eventually targets).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/services/authz/enforce.py`:
  - `Permission(action_key)` FastAPI dependency.
  - `require(ctx: WorkspaceContext, action_key: str, *, scope_kind, scope_id)`
    callable for service-layer use (not every check is router-bound).
- Resolution order per spec §02:
  1. Reject if `action_key` not in catalog → 422 internal misuse
     (caller bug).
  2. If `root_only` and caller not in `owners` group on the scope
     (or any containing scope for property targets) → 403.
  3. Walk `permission_rule` rows scope-by-scope (most-specific
     first): explicit `deny` wins unless `root_protected_deny=True`
     and caller is in `owners`. First `allow` ends the walk.
  4. Fall back to `default_allow` system groups.
- Wire to existing `WorkspaceContext` (`p0.tenancy.ctx`); never
  trust a body field for the calling subject.
- Emit one structured log line per denied check (action_key,
  scope_kind, scope_id, subject) — useful for "who can do this?"
  preview later.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `Permission("expenses.approve")` dependency rejects callers
      without the action; allows owners; allows users matched by an
      `allow` rule.
- [ ] `root_only` actions deny non-owners even with an explicit
      allow rule.
- [ ] `root_protected_deny` actions ignore `deny` rules targeting
      owners.
- [ ] Catalog membership validated at app boot (startup hook fails
      fast on unknown keys in `permission_rule` rows).
- [ ] Unit tests cover every quadrant: allowed, denied, root-only,
      root-protected, unknown-action, no-rule fallback.
- [ ] Integration test wires the dependency into one sample router
      and confirms 403 for an under-permissioned user.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_authz_enforce.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_authz_router_dep.py -xvs
```

title: feat(time/geofence): radius enforcement on clock-in (per-property toggle)
description:
## Problem / goal
When a property has geofencing enabled, clock-in must verify the
worker's reported lat/lon is within `radius_m` of the property's
fence centre. Out-of-fence attempts are rejected (or warned, per
config) and audited.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Bookings"
  geofence references; `docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md`
  geofence settings.
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/time/models.py`
  (`GeofenceSetting`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`
  (geofence rejection toast).

## Implementation notes
- `app/domain/time/geofence.py`:
  - `check(ctx, property_id, lat, lon) -> GeofenceVerdict`
    (`ok | outside | disabled | no_fix`).
  - Haversine in `app/util/geo.py`; reuse if present.
- Wire into `open_shift` (Phase 8 task `p8.shift.crud`):
  - Accept optional `client_lat`, `client_lon`, `gps_accuracy_m`.
  - If property has enabled `GeofenceSetting`, require fix; reject if
    distance > `radius_m + gps_accuracy_m`.
  - Tolerance / mode (`enforce` vs `warn`) is feature-flag per
    property — extend `GeofenceSetting` with `mode` enum
    (`enforce|warn|off`).
- Audit row on every rejection (`shift.geofence_rejected` with
  distance + accuracy).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] In-fence open succeeds
- [ ] Out-of-fence with `mode=enforce` returns 422 + audit row
- [ ] Out-of-fence with `mode=warn` succeeds, audit + SSE warning
- [ ] `mode=off` (or feature absent) bypasses checks entirely
- [ ] Missing GPS fix on enforce → 422; on warn → audit only

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_geofence.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_shifts_geofence.py -xvs
```
