title: feat(llm/receipt-ocr): vendor / amount / date / category / currency parsing
description:
## Problem / goal
The `expenses.autofill` (a.k.a. receipt OCR) capability lifts
structured fields from a receipt image. Used by the receipt
upload flow to pre-fill the expense form; the user always
confirms before save.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Capability
  catalog" (`expenses.autofill`), §"Capability defaults
  (seeds)" (Gemma 3 27B IT with vision tag).
- Cross-ref: §08 "Expenses & receipt OCR" — consumed by
  `p8.receipt.ocr` (the upload pipeline).
- Mocks: existing receipt upload UI under `mocks/web/src/pages/`
  expenses.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/capabilities/receipt_ocr.py` with
  `extract(ctx, image_bytes) -> ReceiptDraft`.
- Prompt template registered under capability
  `expenses.autofill` in `llm_prompt_template` (self-seeded
  per §11).
- Structured output via JSON-mode (Gemma supports it); pydantic
  schema enforces `vendor`, `amount_cents`, `currency_iso4217`,
  `occurred_on`, `category` (free text), `confidence_pct`.
- Calls the LLM via `p12.usage-recorder` (so budget + audit
  apply); image is redacted from prompt logs (PII posture per
  §11 "Redaction / PII").
- On parse failure (model returned non-JSON or schema mismatch),
  falls through the assignment chain; if the chain is exhausted,
  raises `ReceiptParseError` (the upload caller falls back to
  manual entry, no partial fill).
- Currency defaults to the workspace currency (§09) when the
  model can't determine it; `occurred_on` defaults to "today"
  in the property's local tz.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A canonical-shape receipt parses to a `ReceiptDraft` with
  every field populated.
- [ ] A receipt missing the vendor name returns
  `vendor=None` (no fabrication) and a low confidence score.
- [ ] A non-receipt image (e.g. a selfie) raises
  `ReceiptParseError`.
- [ ] Workspace at-cap blocks the call with
  `budget_exceeded` and the upload caller surfaces the
  message verbatim.
- [ ] An admin disabling the assignment for
  `expenses.autofill` makes the capability return
  `capability_unassigned` (the upload UI hides autofill).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/capabilities/test_receipt_ocr.py`
  with stubbed LLM responses.
- Integration test: a fixture receipt image flows through and the
  resulting `Expense` row matches the draft.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(domain/places): property_closure CRUD + clash detection + event
description:
## Problem / goal
Property closures (planned downtime, owner blocks, renovations) with
clash detection against existing schedules and stay-driven occurrences
so managers see what they're stepping on. Publishes
\`property.closure.created\` for downstream subscribers (turnover
generator suppresses on closed dates).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"iCal feed"
  ("Polling behavior" → closures from \`Blocked\` VEVENTs),
  \`docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md\` (\`property_closure\` is
  the surface that stay/lifecycle rules consult).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"property_closure".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyClosuresPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (CLOSURES).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/closure_service.py\` —
  \`create_closure\`, \`update_closure\`, \`delete_closure\`,
  \`list_closures\`, \`detect_clashes\`.
- \`detect_clashes\` returns existing schedules + stays whose date
  range overlaps the proposed closure; UI shows them as warnings,
  user confirms anyway.
- On create/update: publish \`property.closure.created\` (or
  \`.updated\`) via the Phase 0 events port with payload
  \`{property_id, starts_at, ends_at, reason, source_ical_feed_id?}\`.
- Honour the \`source_ical_feed_id\` lineage from iCal upserts; manual
  delete is allowed and not recreated unless the upstream VEVENT
  reappears.
- Workspace-scoped via property join; audit every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Clash detection returns overlapping stays + schedules
- [ ] Event published exactly once per mutation, payload validated
- [ ] iCal-sourced closures carry \`source_ical_feed_id\`
- [ ] Manual delete is sticky until upstream re-asserts
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover clash + event publishing + lineage

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_closure_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_closure_clash.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(payroll/pay-rules): CRUD + effective windows + per-user currency
description:
## Problem / goal
A `PayRule` defines a worker's hourly base + premium multipliers
(overtime, night, weekend) over an effective window. Multiple rules
per user are allowed; selection by date is left to the payslip
compute step.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Pay rules",
  §"Pay-rule selection when multiple rules overlap".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`PayRule`).

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/payroll/rules.py`:
  - `create_rule(ctx, user_id, *, currency, base_cents_per_hour,
    multipliers, effective_from, effective_to=None)`.
  - `list_rules(ctx, *, user_id)` ordered by `effective_from desc`.
  - `update_rule(ctx, rule_id, **fields)` — refuses changes after
    a payslip in `paid` state has consumed the rule.
  - `delete_rule(ctx, rule_id)` — soft delete (set `effective_to`
    to now).
- Validation:
  - Multipliers in `[1.0, 5.0]`.
  - `effective_to > effective_from` when set.
  - Currency in ISO 4217.
- REST under `app/api/payroll/rules.py`.
- Capability `payroll.manage_rules` (manager / owner only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD endpoints + audit rows
- [ ] Validators reject invalid multipliers / currency / window
- [ ] Locked-period rules immutable
- [ ] OpenAPI regenerated

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_pay_rules.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_pay_rules_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(api/billing): organizations, rate cards, work orders, quotes, vendor invoices, client portal
description:
## Problem / goal
Billing router — clients & vendors and everything that flows
between them and the workspace: rate cards, work orders, quotes,
vendor invoices, plus the client-portal read surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Clients, work orders,
  invoices (§22)" (full subsection including
  \`property_workspace\` invites).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ClientsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/RateCardsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/WorkOrdersPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/QuotesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/VendorInvoicesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/client-portal/web/src/pages/PortalPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes

- Sub-resources: \`POST /work-orders/{id}/lines\`,
  \`POST /work-orders/{id}/invoice\` (transitions
  \`completed -> invoiced\`),
  \`POST /quotes/{id}/send\` (audits + emails),
  \`POST /quotes/{id}/decision\`.
- Client-portal endpoints under \`/portal/...\` accept a portal
  token (issued via \`POST /property-workspace-invites\`); they
  expose only what the spec defines as portal-visible.
- Money in integer cents; multi-currency supported via the
  organization's \`default_currency\`.
- Idempotency on every mutation; cursor pagination on every
  listing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Routes for organization, rate-card, work-order, quote,
      vendor-invoice
- [ ] Work-order \`POST /invoice\` creates linked invoice +
      transitions state
- [ ] Quote send + decision endpoints audited; SSE on decision
- [ ] Portal endpoints reject missing/invalid portal tokens
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`billing\`

\`\`\`

title: feat(config): pydantic-settings config module
description:
## Problem / goal
Central config loader for every adapter, worker, and API router. Pulls
from env (\`CREWDAY_*\`) and a \`.env\` file; enforces types via pydantic.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md\` §"Environment
  variables", \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants".
- All env vars prefixed \`CREWDAY_\` — DB URL, data dir, bind address,
  trusted interfaces, public URL, SMTP, OpenRouter key, etc.
- \`CREWDAY_DATA_DIR\` defaults to \`./data\`. Never write outside it.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/config.py\` exposes \`settings: Settings\` (a pydantic
  \`BaseSettings\` singleton). Fields include:
  \`database_url\`, \`data_dir\`, \`bind_host\`, \`bind_port\`,
  \`trusted_interfaces\` (list of glob patterns, default
  \`['tailscale*']\`), \`allow_public_bind\` (bool),
  \`public_url\`, \`smtp_host\`, \`smtp_port\`, \`smtp_user\`,
  \`smtp_password\`, \`openrouter_api_key\`, \`demo_mode\`,
  \`worker\` (\`internal | external\`), \`storage_backend\`
  (\`localfs | s3\`).
- Use \`SecretStr\` for every credential field.
- \`.env.example\` committed at repo root listing every var with an
  empty/default value and a one-line comment.
- Never log \`Settings()\` directly — add a \`safe_dump()\` helper
  returning a dict with secrets masked.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`from app.config import settings\` works in every module
- [ ] Missing required env var raises a clear \`ValidationError\`
- [ ] \`settings.safe_dump()\` hides every \`SecretStr\`
- [ ] Unit tests cover default values, env override, and secret redaction

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_config.py -xvs
CREWDAY_DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///:memory: \
  uv run python -c "from app.config import settings; print(settings.safe_dump())"
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/layouts): copy App.tsx, main.tsx, all layouts verbatim from mocks
description:
## Problem / goal
Bring the route tree and per-role layouts into `app/web/` so the
production SPA's top-level shape matches the mocks pixel-for-pixel.
Adapt only what's strictly necessary to talk to the real auth
context (the rest is verbatim).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Route tree",
  §"Layouts per role", §"PreviewShell".

title: feat(audit): append-only audit_log writer + migration
description:
## Problem / goal
Every mutation writes one row to \`audit_log\` in the **same
transaction**. Reads are never audited. Writer is a tiny append-only
API, called by every domain service on the write path.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"audit_log",
  \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Audit log",
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants" #3.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/audit/models.py\`: \`AuditLog\` table — id (ULID),
  workspace_id, actor_id, actor_kind, actor_grant_role,
  actor_was_owner_member, entity_kind, entity_id, action, diff JSON,
  correlation_id, created_at. No FKs for speed; soft refs only.
- \`app/audit/__init__.py\`: \`write_audit(ctx, entity_kind,
  entity_id, action, diff)\` function taking a
  \`WorkspaceContext\` and a JSON-serialisable \`diff\`.
- Alembic migration: \`0001_audit_log\` — creates \`audit_log\` with
  indexes on \`(workspace_id, created_at desc)\` and
  \`(workspace_id, entity_kind, entity_id)\`.
- Register \`audit_log\` in the workspace-scoped table registry.
- Domain adapters already open a UoW; the writer must use the **same**
  session so audit lands in the same transaction.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Table + indexes present after \`alembic upgrade head\`
- [ ] \`write_audit\` inside a UoW commits together with the mutation
- [ ] Rollback of the UoW rolls back the audit row too
- [ ] Attempting \`write_audit\` without a \`WorkspaceContext\` raises
      \`TenantFilterMissing\`
- [ ] Unit tests cover success + rollback + context-missing cases

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_audit_writer.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/admin): UsagePage — per-workspace LLM spend + cap edit + budget ledger
description:
## Problem / goal
Operators need a per-workspace spend view with the ability to bump
or trim a budget cap and a ledger of recent LLM calls — the runaway
spend kill switch lives here.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/usage` — per-workspace spend, cap adjust, pause state;
  §11 LLM and agents (`budget_ledger`).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/UsagePage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `StatCard`, `ProgressBar`, `Chip`,
  `Loading`; `formatMoney`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /admin/api/v1/usage/summary`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/usage/workspaces`,
  `PATCH /admin/api/v1/workspaces/{id}/budget` (body: `{ cap_cents }`).
- **Types**: `AdminUsageSummary`, `AdminWorkspaceRow`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/admin/UsagePage.tsx`.
- Inline cap-edit per row (mock keeps `editing` + `draftCap` local
  state). On submit, optimistic update; rollback on failure.
- Pause / resume actions emit
  `admin.workspace.budget_paused` server-side; client refreshes
  via SSE `admin.workspace.changed`.
- Preserve the StatCard layout and ProgressBar tone thresholds
  (>=70% sand, >=90% rust) from the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Summary cards + per-workspace list match mock pixel-for-pixel.
- [ ] Cap edit round-trips with optimistic update.
- [ ] SSE refresh keeps the list live across two open tabs.
- [ ] Permission-gated; non-admin sees not-authorised card.

title: chore(boundaries): import-linter config + CI gate
description:
## Problem / goal
Boundary rules in §01 must be enforced mechanically — no good-will
allowed. \`import-linter\` (or equivalent) runs in CI on every PR.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Enforcement",
  \`docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md\` §"Import-linter contracts".

## Implementation notes
- \`pyproject.toml\` \`[tool.importlinter]\` contracts:
  1. \`domain\` forbids \`app.adapters\` (except \`ports\`).
  2. \`domain\` forbids \`app.api\`, \`app.web\`, \`app.cli\`.
  3. \`domain.<X>\` forbids \`domain.<Y>.<submodule>\` — only the
     \`__init__\` surface.
  4. \`adapters\` forbids \`app.api\`, \`app.web\`, \`app.cli\`.
  5. Allowed cross-cutting: \`app.util\`, \`app.audit\`,
     \`app.tenancy\`, \`app.events\`, \`app.adapters.*.ports\`.
- CI job \`boundaries\` (part of \`ci.yml\`) runs \`uv run
  lint-imports\` and fails on any violation.
- Add a deliberate failing test: \`tests/boundary/bad_import.py\`
  imports \`app.adapters.db.session\` from \`app.domain.identity\` —
  assert that \`lint-imports\` rejects it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`uv run lint-imports\` passes on the empty context tree
- [ ] Deliberate cross-boundary import fails \`lint-imports\`
- [ ] CI job wired and red on a broken branch

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run lint-imports
uv run pytest tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(util): clock / ULID / hashids / money helpers
description:
## Problem / goal
Shared kernel helpers that every context depends on. Pins the abstractions
so domain code never reaches for \`datetime.now()\` or raw \`secrets\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Shared kernel",
  §"Runtime invariants" #2 (domain never touches \`datetime.now()\`).
- Money handling: spec §09 pins \`decimal.Decimal\` with minor-unit
  integers at the DB layer.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/util/clock.py\`: \`Clock\` Protocol + \`SystemClock\` impl +
  \`FrozenClock\` test double. Exposes \`now()\` returning aware UTC
  \`datetime\`.
- \`app/util/ulid.py\`: \`new_ulid() -> str\`, deterministic in tests
  via \`ulid.new(timestamp=clock.now())\`.
- \`app/util/hashids.py\`: short-lived stay / guest token encoder.
- \`app/util/money.py\`: \`Money\` value object (amount minor units +
  ISO-4217 currency), addition, formatting per locale.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`Clock\` protocol + \`SystemClock\` + \`FrozenClock\` (tests can
      freeze time)
- [ ] \`new_ulid()\` is monotonic and URL-safe
- [ ] \`Money(100, "EUR") + Money(200, "EUR") == Money(300, "EUR")\`
- [ ] Cross-currency math raises \`CurrencyMismatchError\`
- [ ] Unit tests cover every helper

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_util_clock.py tests/unit/test_util_ulid.py \
              tests/unit/test_util_money.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): send_daily_digest worker with LLM body fallback
description:
## Problem / goal
Per-user morning email summarising today: scheduled occurrences,
overdue items, pending approvals, and the pay-period state. Body
copy comes from the LLM (Phase 12 capability \`daily_digest\`); falls
back to a plain Jinja template when the capability is unmapped or
budget-blocked.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Daily
  digests".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/workers/messaging/daily_digest.py\` scheduled by
  APScheduler at the user's local 07:00 (compute via
  \`property.timezone\` for managers, \`user.timezone\` for workers).
- Pulls per-user data from query helpers (occurrences read, overdue
  count, pending approvals, latest \`pay_period\` state).
- Renders via the LLM client (\`capability='daily_digest'\`); on
  exception or empty result, fall back to
  \`templates/digest_daily.{subject,body_md}.j2\`.
- Writes a \`digest_record\` row; sends via \`NotificationService\`
  (\`kind=daily_digest\`).
- Skips users with no actionable items unless they've opted in to
  "always send" in preferences.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One \`digest_record\` per (user, day); idempotent re-run
- [ ] LLM call respects budget ledger; fallback path covered
- [ ] Empty-day users skipped unless opted in
- [ ] Unit tests cover both LLM-success and fallback paths
- [ ] Integration test: 3-user workspace, verifies one email each

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_daily_digest.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_daily_digest_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(tenancy/orm_filter): recursively rewrite scoped tables inside WHERE subqueries
description:
## Problem / goal
The current tenant filter only walks `stmt.get_final_froms()`. Scoped
tables embedded in WHERE-clause subqueries, scalar subqueries,
correlated selects, or UNION legs (e.g.
`select(Task).where(Task.property_id.in_(select(Property.id)))`)
pass through unchanged: no `TenantFilterMissing` raised, no filter
injected. The outer target name is what the walker sees; references
buried deeper are invisible.

## Current mitigation
- The module docstring of `app/tenancy/orm_filter.py` documents the
  hole (top-level FROM only).
- A regression test
  (`test_where_clause_subquery_scoped_table_is_currently_unfiltered`)
  nails down the current behaviour so any unintentional change trips.
- Developers writing these shapes must thread `workspace_id` into the
  inner query by hand until this lands.

## Proposal
Recursively descend the compiled statement (e.g. via
`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse`) and either (a) rewrite each
scoped inner reference with a `WHERE alias.workspace_id = :ctx`
predicate, or (b) fail closed when no safe rewrite is possible. Pair
with the existing `tenant_agnostic()` escape for legitimate
cross-tenant analytics.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `select(Bar).where(Bar.id.in_(select(Foo.id)))` injects a
      `foo.workspace_id` filter (Foo scoped).
- [ ] Same without a ctx raises `TenantFilterMissing(table='foo')`.
- [ ] Correlated subquery (`Bar.any(...)`) and UNION legs handled.
- [ ] `tenant_agnostic()` still bypasses.
- [ ] No regressions in current 21 orm_filter tests.

## Spec
`docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Tenant filter enforcement".

## Dependencies
Discovered during self-review of cd-52k (see cd-3cq).

title: feat(web/employee): HistoryPage — completed occurrences + chats + expenses + leaves feed
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's personal history (§14 worker history). Tabbed feed across Tasks / Chats / Expenses / Leaves so the worker can find what they did last week without scrolling the schedule. Tab is reflected in `?tab=...` for shareable links.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/HistoryPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/HistoryPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/HistoryPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `Loading`, `formatMoney`, `fmtDate`, `fmtDateTime`, `cap`. Types: `HistoryPayload`, `Property`. `useSearchParams` for the tab.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me/history?tab=tasks|chats|expenses|leaves&page=N` returning `HistoryPayload` with the union shape.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/HistoryPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/HistoryPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Preserve the `isTab` type-guard and the `TABS` ordering exactly.
- Empty states per tab.
- Pagination via cursor; preserve infinite-scroll behaviour if present in the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/HistoryPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/HistoryPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/HistoryPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/HistoryPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: Self-review: test(integration): testcontainers PG harness + dual-backend CI matrix
description:

title: chore(ci/cli): parity gate — crewday --help ≡ _surface.json ≡ openapi.json
description:
## Problem / goal
The CLI is only useful if it stays in lockstep with the API. A CI gate
must fail when any of these three diverge:
1. `crewday --help` (the resolved Click command tree),
2. `_surface.json` (the codegen output, committed),
3. `openapi.json` (the API source of truth, committed).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Parity & CI",
  `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"CLI parity".
- **Builds on**: Task `p15.codegen --check` mode + a help-tree dump.

## Implementation notes
- New script `scripts/cli_parity_check.py`:
  1. Re-run codegen in `--check` mode (fails if `_surface.json`
     would change).
  2. Dump `crewday --help` recursively to a stable tree
     representation (resource → command → param signatures).
  3. Build the same tree from `_surface.json`.
  4. Diff (1) vs (2); diff (2) vs (3) by `operationId` set minus
     exclusions.
  5. Non-empty diff → exit 1 with a focused report (added /
     removed / changed signatures).
- New CI job `cli-parity` in the GitHub Actions workflow runs the
  script after the build step.
- README pointer in `cli/README.md` explains how to refresh
  (`python -m crewday._codegen` + commit) when the API changes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `scripts/cli_parity_check.py` exits 0 on a clean tree
- [ ] Adding an op to `openapi.json` without regenerating fails CI
- [ ] Adding an exclusion without regenerating fails CI
- [ ] Failure report names the offending operation(s)
- [ ] Wired as a required check in CI

## Test plan
```bash
uv run python scripts/cli_parity_check.py
# induce drift then re-run to confirm failure mode
```

title: feat(ops): crewday admin backup/restore — sqlite dump or pg_dump + uploads tarball
description:
## Problem / goal
A deployment that cannot be backed up and restored from scratch is
a deployment that will lose data. `crewday admin backup` produces
a single `.tar.zst` archive containing the DB dump, the uploads
tree, and the encrypted `secret_envelope` rows; `crewday admin
restore` validates the bundle (root-key fingerprint match) and
reconstructs the deployment.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe A →
  Backup" (SQLite WAL checkpoint, copy `crewday.db` + `data/files/`
  + `secret_envelope` into tar.zst, rotate last 30 daily + 12
  monthly), §"Recipe B → Backup" (`pg_dump -Fc` + `mc mirror`),
  §"Restore-with-wrong-key guard" (`crewday admin restore` refuses
  to start if any envelope's `key_fp` does not match the current
  root key or a loaded legacy key).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Root key compromise playbook" — restore is the inverse path of
  rotation; the `key_fp` machinery is shared.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/admin/backup.py`:
  - `backup(out_dir, *, encrypt_with_passphrase=None)`:
    1. SQLite: `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(FULL)` then sqlite3 backup
       API; PG: `pg_dump -Fc` via subprocess.
    2. tar.zst the DB dump + `data/files/` + a JSONL of every
       `secret_envelope` row.
    3. Manifest file with timestamps, row counts, current `key_fp`,
       and a content hash.
    4. Optional `--encrypt-with-passphrase` (age-encrypted) for
       ad-hoc transfer.
  - Rotation: keep last 30 daily + 12 monthly (configurable).
- New `crewday admin restore <bundle>`:
  - Read manifest, verify content hash.
  - Walk every envelope's `key_fp`; refuse start if any does not
    resolve under the current root key or a loaded legacy key
    (`--legacy-key-file <path>` repeatable).
  - Apply DB dump into a fresh DB, copy uploads, run migrations,
    then verify.
- Composite CLI command exposed via the §13 CLI surface
  (delegates to the admin module).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crewday admin backup --to /tmp` produces a tar.zst
      containing the DB dump + uploads + `secret_envelope` JSONL +
      manifest
- [ ] Restore round-trip on SQLite reconstructs identical data
      (row-count fingerprint match)
- [ ] Restore round-trip on PG reconstructs identical data
- [ ] Restore refuses to proceed if a `key_fp` doesn't match
- [ ] `--legacy-key-file` allows restore against an older key
- [ ] Rotation deletes backups beyond the keep window
- [ ] Cron snippet from spec works end-to-end

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_backup_restore.py -xvs
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_backup_restore.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/employee): EmployeeAssetPage — read-only asset info + log action
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's view of an individual asset (§21). Read-only metadata + history + the ability to log an action (service / repair / inspect / read meter). Managers get a richer view elsewhere; this page is the field-tech variant.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `Chip`, `Loading`, `AssetIcon`. Types: `AssetDetailPayload`. Helper `fmtDate` (en-GB locale).
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/assets/{aid}` returning `AssetDetailPayload`; `POST /api/v1/assets/{aid}/actions` (kind, notes, meter_reading, optional photo).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Worker has no edit affordance — only `/actions` is writable from here.
- Preserve the optimistic mutation in `markDone` exactly.
- Subscribe to SSE `asset.updated` to refresh history.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

description:
## Problem / goal
Time router — shifts (clock-in/out, manual edits, approvals),
leave requests (vacation/sick/comp/other), and per-property
geofence settings.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Time, payroll,
  expenses" (Time + Leaves + Geofence subsections).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/ClockPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TimesheetsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/LeavesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/LeavesReviewPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/GeofencePage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/time.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`time\`.
- \`POST /shifts/clock-in\` and \`POST /shifts/clock-out\` are
  idempotent + accept optional GPS for geofence verification.
- Manual shift edits go through \`PATCH /shifts/{id}\` and
  require the \`time.edit\` cap.

  decisions audited.

  per spec are sugar over the generic endpoints with

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Clock-in/out idempotent (replays return same shift)
- [ ] Manual edit gated by \`time.edit\` cap
- [ ] Self-service \`/me/leaves\` rejects \`user_id\` overrides

\`\`\`

title: sec(session): cookie flags, idle/absolute timeout, fingerprint, invalidate-on-credential-change
description:
## Problem / goal
Session cookies and lifetime policy are load-bearing for every
authenticated surface. Phase 2 ships a working session; this task
hardens it: `__Host-` prefix with the right flag set, idle +
absolute timeouts, a device fingerprint sanity check, and
mandatory invalidation of every live session when the underlying
credential changes (passkey added/revoked, password — none today,
recovery code consumed).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Cookies"
  (`__Host-crewday_session`, `Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax;
  Path=/`, opaque 192-bit value), §"Shared-origin XSS containment"
  (`__Host-` cookies, origin-locked).
- **Spec**: §"Passkey specifics" (sign-count rollback auto-revoke
  → must invalidate sessions for the rolled credential).
- **Phase 2**: `p2.session` already exists; this task extends it.

## Implementation notes
- Cookie writer enforces every flag at one chokepoint
  (`app/api/auth/session_cookie.py`); a unit test reads the
  resulting `Set-Cookie` header and asserts each attribute.
- Idle timeout: 30 days default (configurable per workspace via a
  setting in §15 reach). Absolute timeout: 90 days. Both stored
  on the `session` row; middleware bumps `last_seen_at` on each
  request and refuses sessions exceeding either.
- Device fingerprint: hash of `(User-Agent, Accept-Language)` at
  mint time, stored on the row, mismatch → log
  `audit.session.fingerprint_mismatch` and require re-auth.
- Wire `session.invalidate_for_user(user_id)` into:
  - passkey register / revoke (§03)
  - magic-link recovery consumption
  - sign-count rollback auto-revoke (§15) — invalidates only the
    sessions tied to the revoked credential id
- Audit `session.invalidated` with cause.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `Set-Cookie` carries `__Host-`, `Secure`, `HttpOnly`,
      `SameSite=Lax`, `Path=/`, no `Domain`, no `Max-Age` past 90 days
- [ ] Idle past 30 days → 401 + `session_idle_timeout`; absolute
      past 90 days → 401 + `session_absolute_timeout`
- [ ] Fingerprint mismatch logs audit and forces re-auth
- [ ] Adding or revoking a passkey kills every other live session
      for the same user
- [ ] Sign-count rollback (§15 auto-revoke) kills sessions tied to
      the revoked credential id
- [ ] Tenant isolation regression still passes after the changes

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_session_cookie.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_session_lifetime.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_session_invalidation.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/public): RecoverPage — email -> magic link recovery
description:
## Problem / goal
Self-service recovery for users who lost every passkey device (§03). Workers/clients/guests submit email only; managers and owners-group members must additionally enter an unused break-glass code (step-up). Server replies 'sent_if_exists' to avoid role enumeration.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `.surface--login`, `.login__card`, the step-up toggle for owners.
- **API endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/auth/recover/begin` (returns 202 sent_if_exists).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Magic links never issue a session on their own; the link lands on `/recover/enroll` (a separate page, out of scope here) and walks the user through a fresh passkey ceremony.
- Preserve the step-up branch (`stepUp` state) exactly as in the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-public/RecoverPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-public/RecoverPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-public/RecoverPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/public/RecoverPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(auth/webauthn): relying-party config (rp_id, origin, attestation policy)
description:
## Problem / goal
Centralise the WebAuthn relying-party configuration so every passkey
ceremony (registration + assertion) reads the same `rp_id`, expected
origin, user-verification policy, and algorithm list. This is the
foundation every other passkey task depends on.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"WebAuthn specifics",
  §"Login", §"Privacy" (what we are allowed to store).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Passkey specifics".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/login/` (UI affordance only — no
  real ceremony today).
- **DB tables already present** (Phase 1): `passkey_credential` rows
  carry `public_key`, `sign_count`, transports, AAGUID, last_used_at.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/auth/webauthn.py` exposing:
  - `RelyingParty(rp_id, rp_name, origin, allowed_origins)` dataclass
    derived from `app.config.Settings` (`crewday_public_origin`,
    `crewday_rp_id`).
  - `policy()` returning a frozen `WebAuthnPolicy` (UV=`required`,
    attestation=`none`, attachment=`platform` preferred + cross-platform
    allowed, resident_keys=`preferred`, algs `[ES256, RS256]`).
  - Thin wrappers around the `webauthn` package's `generate_*` /
    `verify_*` helpers so callers never reach the lib directly.
- Validate `rp_id` against `origin` at startup (registers a check via
  `app.config.capabilities`); refuse to boot if mismatched.
- Tenant-agnostic: the RP is process-wide, not per-workspace.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `RelyingParty` populated from settings; startup fails fast on
      mismatched origin / rp_id
- [ ] Policy values match §03 `WebAuthn specifics` exactly
- [ ] No call site imports `webauthn` directly — only via this module
- [ ] Unit tests cover policy frozenness + origin/rp_id validation
- [ ] `mypy --strict` clean

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_webauthn_rp.py -xvs
uv run mypy app/auth/webauthn.py
```
Manual: boot the dev container with a deliberately mismatched
`CREWDAY_RP_ID` and confirm the bind-guard refuses to start.

title: Refresh mocks/web pins + configs to match app/web scaffold
description:
## Problem / context
`app/web/` (cd-ul7f) was scaffolded by copying `mocks/web/` verbatim
and running a fresh `npm install --legacy-peer-deps`. The build only
succeeds after two deviations that `mocks/web/` today silently avoids
because its `node_modules/` still holds packages installed before its
`package.json` was bumped to vite 8 / TS 6.

### Forced deviations in app/web vs mocks/web
1. `tsconfig.app.json` — `baseUrl` removed (TS 6 deprecates it to an
   error unless `ignoreDeprecations: "6.0"` is set); `paths` values
   prefixed with `./` so they remain relative to the tsconfig dir.
2. `vite.config.ts` — `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`
   rewritten from the record form (`{ vendor: [...], query: [...] }`)
   to the function form. Rollup 4's TypeScript typings (bundled with
   Vite 8) only accept the function form; the record form is a type
   error.
3. `package.json` — `@testing-library/dom@^10.4.1` added as an explicit
   devDependency. Under npm 11 with `--legacy-peer-deps`, peer deps of
   `@testing-library/react` aren't auto-installed.

### Evidence
`mocks/web/node_modules/vite/package.json` reports `5.4.21` while
`mocks/web/package-lock.json` pins `8.0.8` and `package.json` declares
`^8.0.8`. Same for `typescript` (installed 5.9.3 vs declared ^6.0.3).
A fresh `npm ci` or `rm -rf node_modules && npm install` in
`mocks/web/` would hit the same three errors I hit in `app/web/`.

## Proposal
Sync `mocks/web/` to the same three fixes (or pick the alternative fix
— e.g. add `ignoreDeprecations` instead of dropping `baseUrl`), then
refresh its node_modules + lockfile so `npm ci` produces the same
tree the lockfile claims.

## Acceptance criteria
- `rm -rf mocks/web/node_modules && npm ci --legacy-peer-deps` inside
  `mocks/web/` succeeds.
- `npm run build`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- --run` all
  pass from a clean `mocks/web/` install.
- `mocks/web/` and `app/web/` stay in lockstep on toolchain config
  (no diff beyond name/description/title/entry content).

## Context links
- cd-ul7f task + spec (docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md).
- app/web/vite.config.ts + tsconfig.app.json + package.json carry
  comments pointing at this follow-up.

title: feat(demo): scenario fixture loader (workspace + users + schedules + stays + tasks + expenses)
description:
## Problem / goal
On first visit to a `?scenario=<key>` URL, the demo server mints a
fresh `workspaces` + `demo_workspace` row, seeds it from the YAML
fixture under `app/fixtures/demo/<key>.yml`, and binds the
visitor's signed cookie to it. Resolving relative timestamps
(`T-2d`, `stay:+7d`) keeps every demo "current".

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/24-demo-mode.md` §"Seeding" (relative
  timestamp resolver, ASCII cast, pre-bundled `_media/`),
  §"Scenarios shipped with v1" (villa-owner, rental-manager,
  housekeeper).
- **Spec**: §"Visitor lifecycle" → §"First visit" — mint flow,
  cookie binding, signed-cookie validation.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/mocks/mock_data.py` is the canonical
  cast and should be the source of names where practical.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/fixtures/demo/`: three YAML files plus `_media/` blobs.
  Each fixture declares `default_persona`, `personas` allowlist,
  workspace metadata, properties, units, role grants, schedule
  templates, stays, in-flight tasks, and a few past expenses.
- New `app/demo/seeder.py`: `seed_workspace(scenario_key) ->
  workspace_id`, called from the demo cookie middleware on cache
  miss. Resolves relative timestamps against `now()`. Re-uses
  existing service layer (`PropertyService`, `UserService`,
  `TaskTemplateService`, `StayService`) — never raw SQL.
- Cookie minting + `__Host-crewday_demo_<scenario_id>` plumbing
  also lands in this task (one cookie per scenario, signed via
  `itsdangerous`).
- Idempotency: re-seeding the same scenario produces structurally
  identical rows (ids differ but counts and shapes match, asserted
  in tests).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All three v1 scenarios seed successfully with no errors
- [ ] Relative timestamps resolve to absolute UTC, no future
      `created_at` outside `[now-30d, now+30d]`
- [ ] Cookie minted with correct flags
      (`__Host-`, `Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=None; Partitioned`),
      single-binding, scenario-name-scoped
- [ ] Tampered or stale-key cookie treated as absent (re-mint path)
- [ ] Unit tests for relative-timestamp resolver
- [ ] Integration test: hit `/app?scenario=rental-manager` cold,
      assert workspace count delta and a coherent task list
- [ ] Loading two scenarios in two iframes yields two distinct
      cookies and two distinct workspaces

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/demo/test_seeder.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/demo/test_mint_flow.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/shared): StyleguidePage — design tokens + component library preview (dev/staging only)
description:
## Problem / goal
A static page enumerating the palette, typography, shape, motion,
and every common component (buttons, chips, cards, stat cards,
progress bars, etc.). Its primary job is **visual-regression
canary**: any change to `tokens.css` or `globals.css` shows up as
a pixel diff here first, before reaching real pages.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Design language"
  (palette, typography, shape, motion); §"Public" route block
  notes `/styleguide` is dev + staging only.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/StyleguidePage.tsx` (plus its CSS:
  `mocks/web/src/styles/styleguide.css`).
- **Components**: self-contained — only the `Camera` icon from
  `lucide-react` and ad-hoc `swatch`, `type-row`, `type-sample`
  semantic classes.
- **API endpoints**: none (static page).
- **Types**: none.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/StyleguidePage.tsx`; copy its CSS verbatim
  too.
- Mount only when `import.meta.env.DEV === true` or when an
  explicit `STAGING` env flag is set; production builds must 404
  the route per spec.
- Page is intentionally static — no queries, no SSE, no
  permission gate (it's behind the dev/staging build flag, not
  authz).
- The page must reference `tokens.css` directly (not duplicate
  hex codes) so a token change diffs visibly.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page renders identical to mock at all breakpoints.
- [ ] Production build does not include the route (404 in prod).
- [ ] Hex codes in the page come from `tokens.css` (no
      duplication).
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes (this is the
      canary — must be exact).

## Test plan
```bash
npx playwright test tests/web/styleguide.spec.ts
# Manual: verify route 404s on a production build.
```

title: feat(web/manager): per-property closures with date range + reason
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager declares blackout windows on a property (renovation, owner stay, deep clean) so the rota and stay schedule know to skip them.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyClosuresPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/property/<pid>/closures` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/property_closures?property_id={pid}, GET /api/v1/me, POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/property_closures.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyClosuresPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The query uses a `?property_id=…` filter — keep that exact param name; the Phase 14 places router must honour it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/property/<pid>/closures`.

title: feat(domain/places): property↔workspace junction — transfer, share, revoke
description:
## Problem / goal
Manage the multi-belonging junction (\`property_workspace\`): invite a
second workspace, change membership_role, revoke access, transfer
\`owner_workspace\`. The owner workspace is the only party allowed to
grant/revoke; managed/observer rows are operational/read-only.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Multi-belonging
  (sharing across workspaces)" — covers role semantics, agency
  switching flow, PII boundary.
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"property_workspace".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  ("Sharing & client" tab).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/membership_service.py\` —
  \`invite_workspace\`, \`accept_invite\`, \`revoke_workspace\`,
  \`update_membership_role\`, \`transfer_ownership\`,
  \`list_memberships\`.
- Authorization: only members of the \`owner_workspace\` row may
  invite/revoke/transfer; managed/observer can list themselves out
  but cannot mutate.
- Enforce invariant: exactly one \`owner_workspace\` row per property
  (transfer is two-step in one TX: demote old to observer or revoke,
  promote new).
- Surface \`share_guest_identity\` toggle on managed/observer rows
  (PII boundary, §15).
- Audit every mutation; emits \`property_workspace.invited|accepted|
  revoked|role_changed|ownership_transferred\` log lines.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Only owner_workspace members can invite/revoke
- [ ] Exactly one owner_workspace per property invariant holds
- [ ] Revoke cascades visibility (managed workspace stops seeing it)
- [ ] \`share_guest_identity\` defaults false; toggling audits
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover all roles + denial paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_membership_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_membership_flow.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(tasks): detect_overdue worker job
description:
## Problem / goal
`overdue` is a soft state that lights up `/today` and the manager
dashboard the moment a task slips past `due_by_utc + grace`. A
periodic worker flips eligible tasks and emits `task.overdue` so
notifications + dashboards can react.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"State machine"
  (overdue is soft, never terminal; manual transitions clear
  `overdue_since`); `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` for
  APScheduler.
- Boundary: `app/workers/tasks/overdue.py`. Reads + writes
  `tasks.state, tasks.overdue_since` only.

## Implementation notes
- Entry: `detect_overdue(ctx: WorkspaceContext, *, now: datetime |
  None = None) -> OverdueReport`.
- Pulls every `task` row where:
  - `state IN ('scheduled', 'pending', 'in_progress')`
  - `due_by_utc IS NOT NULL`
  - `due_by_utc + tasks.overdue_grace_minutes < now`
  - `deleted_at IS NULL`
- `tasks.overdue_grace_minutes` is a workspace setting (default 15).
- For each match: set `state = 'overdue'`, `overdue_since = now`;
  emit `task.overdue` with `task_id`, `workspace_id`,
  `assigned_user_id`, `slipped_minutes`.
- Idempotent: a task already in `overdue` is skipped (no second
  event).
- APScheduler interval: 5 minutes (configurable via
  `tasks.overdue_tick_seconds`).
- Audit summary row per run: `tasks.overdue_tick` carrying the
  count and per-property breakdown.
- The completion / skip / cancel paths in `p5.completion` already
  clear `overdue_since` on any manual transition (per §06); make
  sure the reverse is also true here — manual edits made between
  ticks should not be re-overdued unless they slip again.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tick is idempotent (re-running with no new slips emits no
      events)
- [ ] Soft state never overwrites a manual transition that
      happened between ticks
- [ ] `task.overdue` carries `slipped_minutes`
- [ ] Audit summary written per tick
- [ ] WorkspaceContext threaded through; multi-workspace runs
      iterate per tenant
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover slip / clear / reslip cycles

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_overdue.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tasks_overdue_tick.py -xvs
```

title: feat(auth/passkey): assertion challenge + verify (login) creates session
description:
## Problem / goal
Implement the passkey login ceremony. A successful assertion verifies
a `passkey_credential`, bumps `sign_count` + `last_used_at`, and
issues a session via `app.auth.session.issue`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Login",
  §"WebAuthn specifics" (UV=required, conditional UI), §"Sessions".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/login/Login.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `PasskeyCredential` (per-user lookup by credential id
  byte-string).

## Implementation notes
- Routes: `POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/login/start` (bare host;
  conditional-UI lookup is by credential id, not by username) and
  `.../login/finish`.
- `start` returns options with `userVerification: "required"` and an
  empty `allowCredentials` (discoverable credentials).
- `finish`: look up `passkey_credential` by raw credential id, verify
  assertion against stored public key, reject if `sign_count` regresses
  (clone-detection per §15 §"Passkey specifics"), bump counter +
  `last_used_at` in the same transaction, then call
  `session.issue(user_id, has_owner_grant=resolve_owner(user_id))`.
- Whether the user holds an owner grant on **any** workspace decides
  the longer lifetime (§03). Resolve via `permission_group_member`
  membership in any `owners` system group.
- Audit: `audit.passkey.assertion_ok` on success; `…rejected` with
  the failure reason on bad assertion (no PII in payload).
- Rate-limit failed attempts per credential id and per source IP
  (§15 §"Rate limiting and abuse controls").

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Successful assertion issues a session + cookie
- [ ] Sign-count regression triggers `audit.passkey.cloned_detected`
      and refuses login
- [ ] Failed attempts are throttled (per IP + per credential id)
- [ ] Audit rows written in the same tx as `passkey_credential` update
- [ ] No bare-host route requires `WorkspaceContext`
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DB backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_passkey_login_pg.py -xvs
```
Manual: Playwright with virtual authenticator drives `/login` and
verifies the cookie + redirect.

title: feat(agent/staff-chat): worker chat assistant with delegated tool use
description:
## Problem / goal
The worker-side embedded chat agent (§11 "Worker-side agent")
runs on the same runtime as the manager agent but with a tighter
tool catalog scoped to the calling worker's permissions: today's
tasks, mark done, report issue, get instruction, my bookings,
inventory low. Per-channel context and turn semantics from
`p12.agent-runtime`.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Worker-side
  agent", §"Staff chat assistant".
- Auth: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Agent tokens".
- Cross-ref: §10 "Chat" — consumed by `p10.chat-message.crud`
  (message persistence).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/agent/staff_chat.py` registering the
  worker tool catalog (`get_tasks_today`, `mark_task_done`,
  `report_issue`, `get_instruction`, `get_my_bookings`,
  `amend_booking`, `decline_booking`, `propose_booking`,
  `get_inventory_low`). Each tool is a thin facade over the
  REST surface invoked under the delegated token.
- Capability key: `chat.worker` (seeded per §11).
- `X-Agent-Channel: web_worker_chat` set on every dispatched
  call so inline approval cards land in the worker's chat
  surface.
- Voice input gated by `voice.enabled` workspace setting +
  `voice.transcribe` assignment (off by default).
- Cannot create arbitrary `chat_message` rows — message
  persistence flows through `p10.chat-message.crud`. Cannot
  fabricate tasks: only the exposed tools are reachable.
- Per-channel context: each thread carries its own compaction
  state (one live summary per thread, span-based, recent-window
  floor of 24 h or 20 turns).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A worker asks "what's on my plate today" → exactly
  `get_tasks_today` is called → reply lists the tasks.
- [ ] "Mark the kitchen task done" → resolves to one task →
  calls `mark_task_done` → workspace policy gates the action
  if configured.
- [ ] Tool catalog scoped to the worker — no admin / payroll /
  expense-write tools reachable.
- [ ] Each turn brackets correctly with
  `agent.turn.started/finished`.
- [ ] Compaction kicks in once the recent-window floor is
  exceeded; one live summary per thread.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/agent/test_staff_chat.py`.
- Integration test: worker session → chat thread → marks a task
  done → audit row attributes to the worker user + delegated
  token.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(db/places): property + unit + area + property_workspace + closures
description:
## Problem / goal
Places tables. \`property_workspace\` implements the multi-belonging
invariant (same villa, multiple workspaces).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"property", §"unit",
  §"area", §"property_workspace", §"property_closure",
  \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/places/models.py\`:
  - \`Property\`: id, address, timezone, lat, lon, tags_json,
    created_at.
  - \`PropertyWorkspace\`: (property_id, workspace_id) PK, label,
    created_at. **The workspace_id filter attaches here.**
  - \`Unit\`: id, property_id, label, type, capacity.
  - \`Area\`: id, property_id, label, icon, ordering.
  - \`PropertyClosure\`: id, property_id, starts_at, ends_at, reason,
    created_by.
- Alembic migration \`0006_places\`.
- Register tables in tenancy registry: all workspace-scoped via
  \`property_workspace\` join. Adapters for places must filter by
  \`workspace_id\` using the junction, not the bare \`property\` row
  (since a property can belong to many workspaces).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tables present with indexes on (\`property_id\`), (\`workspace_id\`)
- [ ] A property can have multiple \`property_workspace\` rows
- [ ] Closure dates are \`TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\` (PG) / ISO-8601
      (SQLite)
- [ ] Unit tests cover multi-belonging + closures

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_places.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(db/workspace): workspace + user_workspace + slug index
description:
## Problem / goal
The \`workspace\` table is the tenancy anchor. \`user_workspace\` is the
membership junction. Every authz check reads these two.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"workspace",
  §"user_workspace", §"Multi-tenancy tables",
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Workspace addressing".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/identity/models.py\`:
  - \`Workspace\`: id (ULID PK), slug (unique), name, created_at,
    plan (enum), quota_json (JSON), owner_onboarded_at.
  - \`UserWorkspace\`: (user_id, workspace_id) composite PK, role
    cache, created_at.
- Alembic migration \`0003_workspace\`.
- Register \`workspace\` (tenant-agnostic for the slug lookup itself —
  add to the agnostic-allowed list) and \`user_workspace\` (scoped) in
  the tenancy registry.
- Seed helper \`bootstrap_workspace(slug, name, owner_user_id)\` in a
  fixtures module for tests — NOT the signup flow (that's Phase 2).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tables present, indexes per spec
- [ ] Slug is unique; unique violation surfaces a clear error
- [ ] \`user_workspace\` enforces referential integrity
- [ ] Migration is reversible (\`alembic downgrade -1\`)
- [ ] Unit tests on both SQLite and Postgres

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_workspace.py -xvs
uv run alembic downgrade -1
\`\`\`

title: feat(api): FastAPI app factory wires per-context routers under /w/<slug>/api/v1
description:
## Problem / goal
A single FastAPI app factory that mounts every per-context APIRouter
under \`/w/<slug>/api/v1/<ctx>\`, exposes the merged OpenAPI 3.1 doc
at \`/api/openapi.json\`, and centralises the dependency that hands
each handler the current \`WorkspaceContext\` resolved by the Phase 0
tenancy middleware. The factory is the single import seam between
\`app/main.py\` and the router modules; routers never touch the global
app object.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Versioning",
  §"Base URL", §"Authentication", §"OpenAPI",
  §"`operationId` convention", §"Common conventions",
  §"Request/response shape".
- **Architecture**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §api.v1.*,
  §web.*, §"Handlers are thin".
- **Mocks consuming**: \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\` (single fetch
  client; expects \`/w/<slug>/api/v1/...\`), every page under
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/{manager,worker,admin}/*\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/factory.py\`: \`create_app(settings) -> FastAPI\`.
  Mounts a workspace router at \`/w/{slug}\` and an admin router at
  \`/admin\`; each prefixes \`/api/v1\` for the context routers.
- \`app/api/deps.py\`: \`def current_workspace_context() ->
  WorkspaceContext\` — reads the ContextVar set by the Phase 0
  tenancy middleware. Routers depend on this, NEVER read the request
  directly for tenancy.
- Custom OpenAPI generator (\`app.openapi = custom_openapi\`) merges
  per-context tags, sets the \`openapi: 3.1.0\` version, and emits
  the canonical \`operationId\` (\`<context>.<verb>_<resource>\`) per
  spec §"`operationId` convention".
- Mounts the openapi.json at \`/api/openapi.json\` (no slug — the
  schema is identical across workspaces).
- Routers are discovered via an explicit registry in
  \`app/api/v1/__init__.py\` (no autoload — keeps import-linter happy).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`create_app()\` returns a FastAPI instance with all 13 context
      routers mounted under \`/w/{slug}/api/v1\` and admin under
      \`/admin/api/v1\`
- [ ] \`GET /api/openapi.json\` returns valid OpenAPI 3.1 with one tag
      per context
- [ ] \`current_workspace_context\` dependency raises 404 when slug
      is unknown, 403 when caller lacks membership
- [ ] All routes carry the correct \`operationId\` (snake-case,
      \`<ctx>.<verb>_<resource>\`)
- [ ] Import-linter contract: \`app.api.v1.*\` may import \`app.services.*\`
      but not \`app.domain.*\` directly
- [ ] Smoke test boots the app + asserts every router is mounted

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_factory.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_openapi_shape.py -xvs
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json | jq '.openapi'
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/manager): dashboard with KPIs, on-booking, approvals + leaves
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager opens the workspace and immediately sees today's posture: who is on the clock, what's overdue, what needs their approval. The dashboard is the morning hub and the chat agent's primary landing surface.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DashboardPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/dashboard` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, StatCard, Avatar, Chip, Panel, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/dashboard, GET /api/v1/me, POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/approve|reject, POST /api/v1/leaves/{id}/approve|reject.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DashboardPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The mock fans out to four mutations (approval decide, leave decide) — keep them as separate `useMutation` hooks, do not collapse into one.
- The greeting line uses `me.manager_name`; honour that field name from `/api/v1/me` (don't rename to `display_name`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/dashboard`.

title: feat(llm/budget): rolling-30d workspace envelope (check + record)
description:
## Problem / goal
Every LLM call is gated by a workspace-wide rolling 30-day USD
envelope. The envelope wraps every capability, so a noisy chat
thread can't burn another capability's headroom. Pre-flight refuses
any call where `cost_30d_usd + projected_call_cost > cap_usd_30d`;
post-flight records actual usage and updates the cached aggregate.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Workspace usage
  budget" (Meter, Cap, At-cap behaviour, Pricing source, Visible
  surfaces, Demo mode overrides).
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`budget_ledger`
  (a.k.a. `workspace_budget` + `workspace_usage` materialised
  aggregate), §`llm_call`, §`workspace.quota_json`.
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LlmPage.tsx` (deployment
  spend tile), workspace `/settings` usage tile (no edit
  control).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/budget.py` exposing
  `check_budget(ctx, projected_cost_cents) -> None | raises
  BudgetExceeded` and `record_usage(ctx, usage: LlmUsage) ->
  None`. `LlmUsage` carries `prompt_tokens`,
  `completion_tokens`, `cost_cents`, `provider_model_id`,
  `assignment_id`, `capability`.
- Aggregate `workspace_usage.cost_30d_cents` refreshed by the
  background worker every 60 s; warm-started from `llm_call` on
  process boot. `check_budget` reads cached aggregate + projected
  cost (estimated from `prompt_tokens + max_output_tokens` × the
  serving `llm_provider_model` per-million prices).
- Cap row sourced from `workspace_budget` (defaults: prod 5.0 USD,
  demo 0.10 USD), inserted in the same transaction as the workspace
  row by the workspace-create handler. `workspace.quota_json
  .llm_budget_cents_30d` is the legacy mirror — keep it in sync
  for older readers.
- `BudgetExceeded` carries `{ capability, window: '30d_rolling',
  message }` per the spec; refused calls do **not** write
  `llm_call` (the meter only counts calls that left the client).
- `record_usage` writes `llm_call` with provider-reported actual
  tokens and recomputes `cost_cents` from current pricing; bumps
  the cached aggregate atomically.
- Free-tier (`:free` suffix) priced at zero; unknown
  `api_model_id` falls back to (0,0) per-million and emits a
  WARNING per call (no per-call audit row — operational telemetry
  only). Refusals log `event="llm.budget_exceeded"` at INFO.
- Per-IP aggregate cap (§15) is *out of scope here* — it lives in
  its own module; `check_budget` only enforces the per-workspace
  envelope.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] First call against a fresh workspace passes; aggregate ticks
  up post-call.
- [ ] Pre-flight refuses when projected + cached aggregate > cap.
- [ ] Refused call writes no `llm_call` row.
- [ ] Aggregate respects the 30-day window: a 31-day-old call no
  longer contributes.
- [ ] `record_usage` is idempotent on `(workspace_id,
  correlation_id, attempt)` — a retry doesn't double-count.
- [ ] Lowering the cap below the current aggregate immediately
  refuses the next call (within the 60 s refresh window).
- [ ] Free-tier model contributes 0 to the aggregate; the row is
  still written for telemetry.
- [ ] Demo workspaces seed cap = 0.10 USD.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/test_budget.py` for every
  branch above; uses fake clock and fake pricing.
- Integration test: 50 concurrent `record_usage` calls don't
  drift the aggregate (atomicity).

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").
