title: feat(web/manager): asset detail — actions history, attached docs, completion flow
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager opens one asset to see its action history, attached documents (manuals, warranties), and complete a pending action with a note + photo.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/asset/<aid>` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, AssetActionForm, DocPreview.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/assets/{aid}, POST /api/v1/assets/{aid}/actions/{actionId}/complete.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetDetailPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- Action completion accepts an optional photo upload; reuse the shared photo-upload hook (no inline FormData).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/asset/<aid>`.

title: feat(cli): scaffold crewday Click-based entrypoint + core layout
description:
## Problem / goal
Stand up the `cli/crewday/` Python package and the `crewday` Click
entrypoint so every later CLI task has a stable shell to plug into.
This is the foundation for the codegen-driven command tree, the HTTP
client, output formatters, profiles, and composite commands.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` (entire file — esp. §"Package
  layout", §"Command shape", §"Auth & profiles").
- **Architecture**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"cli/" and
  §"Repo layout".
- **Conventions**: project uses `click` + `httpx` + `pydantic` (see
  Phase 0 `pyproject.toml`). Python 3.12+, `mypy --strict`.

## Implementation notes
- New package `cli/crewday/` (entry point declared in `pyproject.toml`
  as `[project.scripts] crewday = "crewday._main:main"`).
- Core modules — leading underscore so they are not part of the
  generated command surface:
  - `_main.py`     — Click root group, version flag, `--profile`,
                     `--output (json|yaml|table|ndjson)`,
                     `--workspace <slug>`, common error handling.
  - `_client.py`   — placeholder (Task `p15.http-client` fills it in).
  - `_output.py`   — placeholder (Task `p15.output-formatters`).
  - `_config.py`   — placeholder (Task `p15.profile-config`).
  - `_globals.py`  — Click context dataclass: profile, workspace,
                     output mode, idempotency-key generator, logger.
- Wire `_main.main()` so `crewday --help` and `crewday --version`
  work end-to-end without any subcommand registered yet.
- `cli/README.md` — one-paragraph overview pointing at §13.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crewday --help` prints the root help with the global flags
- [ ] `crewday --version` prints the package version
- [ ] `_main.py` exposes a `main()` function and is importable
- [ ] `_globals.CrewdayContext` carries profile / workspace / output
- [ ] `mypy --strict cli/` passes; `ruff check cli/` clean
- [ ] Unit test invokes the Click runner against `crewday --help`

## Test plan
```bash
uv run crewday --help
uv run crewday --version
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_main.py -xvs
uv run mypy --strict cli/
```

title: docs(specs/16): reconcile env-var naming with app.config
description: §16 "Environment variables" table uses `CREWDAY_BIND` (host:port), `CREWDAY_STORAGE`, `CREWDAY_WORKER=inprocess`; `app/config.py` (landed under cd-ec1) uses split `CREWDAY_BIND_HOST` + `CREWDAY_BIND_PORT`, `CREWDAY_STORAGE_BACKEND`, `CREWDAY_WORKER=internal`. title: feat(inventory): item CRUD with SKU + barcode uniqueness
description:
## Problem / goal
Inventory items are scoped per property within a workspace. SKU and
barcode must be unique per (workspace, property) so scanner-driven
flows resolve unambiguously.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"Model" (`inventory_item`
  fields), §"Barcode scanning"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"item"; `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` action
  `inventory.adjust` (the action covers manual adjustments; CRUD
  itself uses `scope.edit_settings` at workspace level).
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InventoryPage.tsx`
  (manager catalog view).
- DB tables from `p1.inventory`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/inventory/item_service.py` with `create`,
  `update`, `archive`, `restore`, `list`, `get_by_barcode`,
  `get_by_sku`.
- Uniqueness enforced at the DB layer with a partial unique index
  `(workspace_id, property_id, sku) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`
  and similarly for `barcode_ean13`.
- `unit` validated against the spec's enumerated set
  (`each|pack|kg|liter|roll|...`); free-form rejected.
- Soft delete via `deleted_at`; restore clears it.
- `unit_cost_cents` updated whenever a `restock` movement records a
  new value (denormalised cache; movement service writes through —
  out of scope here, but the column lives on the model).
- Audit on every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/properties/{p}/inventory/items` creates
      item; duplicate SKU or barcode → 409.
- [ ] `PATCH` updates allowed fields; unknown fields rejected; SKU
      change re-validates uniqueness.
- [ ] Soft-delete idempotent; restore idempotent.
- [ ] `GET ?barcode=...` returns single item or 404.
- [ ] Audit row per mutation.
- [ ] Unit tests cover uniqueness collisions, soft-delete + create
      with same SKU (allowed), unit validation.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_items.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_inventory_items.py -xvs
```

title: feat(api/admin): rotate tokens, export, purge, resend magic link, deployment_setting CRUD, workspaces list
description:
## Problem / goal
Admin router for deployment-level operations: rotate tokens,
trigger workspace exports / purges, resend a magic link, manage
deployment settings (capability registry, feature flags, mail
provider), and list / inspect workspaces. Distinct from the
chat-gateway, which lives in messaging, and the LLM admin, which
lives in llm.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Admin surface"
  (Workspace lifecycle, Self-serve signup, Deployment settings +
  capability registry, Admin team, Deployment audit).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/WorkspacesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/DeploymentSettingsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AdminTeamPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/SignupPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/admin.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`admin\`. Mounted
  under \`/admin/api/v1\` (no slug — these endpoints are
  deployment-scoped).
- Workspace operations:
  - \`GET /workspaces\` — paginated list (admin cap only).
  - \`POST /workspaces/{slug}/exports\` — async; returns job id.
  - \`POST /workspaces/{slug}/purge\` — destructive; requires
    confirmation token; audited at deployment level.
  - \`POST /workspaces/{slug}/rotate-tokens\` — invalidates all
    PATs and agent tokens for the workspace.
  - \`POST /auth/resend-magic-link\` — admin-only resend (the
    user-facing one is rate-limited per email).
- Deployment settings: \`GET /settings\` + \`PATCH /settings\`
  for the capability registry, feature flags, mail provider,
  storage backend, etc. Reads OK for any admin team member;
  writes require \`deployment.settings.edit\` cap.
- Admin team CRUD; \`POST /admin-team/{user_id}/grant\` /
  \`/revoke\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All admin routes require admin-team membership; non-admin
      returns 403
- [ ] Purge requires \`confirm=<workspace-slug>\` body field
- [ ] Rotate-tokens invalidates and emits SSE
      \`api_token.bulk_revoked\`
- [ ] Settings PATCH validates against the capability registry
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`admin\`
- [ ] Deployment audit shows every admin mutation

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_admin.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_admin_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(identity/membership): user_workspace invite/accept/remove + workspace switch
description:
## Problem / goal
Users join workspaces via invitation. The membership service governs
invite, accept, remove, and the in-session "switch workspace" action
(both web SPA and API).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md`
  §"Additional users (invite → click-to-accept)".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` (membership +
  surface grants).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/team/Invite.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/workspace/Switcher.tsx`.
- Phase 1 tables: `user_workspace`, `role_grant`.

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/identity/membership.py`:
  - `invite(ctx, *, email, grant_role, scope_property_id=None)` —
    creates a pending `user_workspace` row (state=`invited`) +
    sends a magic link `purpose=invite_accept`. Existing `user`
    is reused; new user row created on accept.
  - `accept(token)` — bare host, consumes the magic link, flips
    `user_workspace.state` to `active`, creates the matching
    `role_grant` in one tx. Issues / refreshes the session.
  - `remove(ctx, *, user_id)` — owners can remove anyone except the
    last owner; managers can remove workers/clients/guests; users
    can remove themselves except the last owner.
  - `list_for_user(user_id)` — used by the workspace switcher.
  - `switch(session_id, *, workspace_id)` — updates
    `Session.workspace_id` after verifying membership.
- Routes:
  - `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/team/invite`
  - `POST /invite/accept` (bare host)
  - `DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/team/{user_id}`
  - `GET /api/v1/me/workspaces`
  - `POST /api/v1/me/workspaces/switch` `{workspace_id}`
- Audit on every mutation; redact email in audit (hash only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Invite → accept end-to-end works on both DBs
- [ ] Removal respects owner-protection rules (cross-checked with
      `p2.role-grants` + `p2.owners-group`)
- [ ] Switcher updates the session's `workspace_id` and the response
      `Set-Cookie` reflects it
- [ ] A user who is not a member of `<slug>` gets 404 from
      `/w/<slug>/...` (covered by `p2.tenancy-identity-wire`)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_membership.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_membership_invite_accept.py -xvs
```
Manual: from `/team`, invite a fresh email, accept in a private
window, switch workspaces, confirm `/today` reflects it.

title: feat(api/payroll): pay rules, periods, payslips, exports
description:

## Implementation notes

- [ ] Export endpoint returns job id; SSE event fires on ready

title: feat(llm/router): capability → provider_model resolver with fallback chain
description:
## Problem / goal
Every LLM caller asks for a *capability* (e.g. `chat.manager`,
`expenses.autofill`, `tasks.intake`), never a model id. The router
resolves capability → ordered list of `llm_provider_model` rows
(priority-ascending) so the client can walk the chain on retryable
upstream failures, and falls back through capability inheritance
when the capability itself has no enabled assignments.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Model assignment",
  §"Capability inheritance", §"Capability defaults (seeds)",
  §"Provider / model / provider-model registry".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`model_assignment`
  (a.k.a. `llm_assignment`), §`llm_provider_model`,
  §`llm_capability_inheritance`.
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LlmPage.tsx` (assignment
  table, reorder, inheritance rows).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/router.py` exposing
  `resolve_model(ctx, capability) -> list[ModelPick]` (chain) and
  `resolve_primary(ctx, capability) -> ModelPick` (head only).
  `ModelPick` carries `provider_model_id`, `api_model_id`,
  `max_tokens`, `temperature`, `extra_api_params`,
  `required_capabilities`, `assignment_id`.
- Resolver order: enabled `llm_assignment` rows for the requested
  capability ordered by `priority ASC`; if none, walk
  `llm_capability_inheritance.inherits_from` (cycle-safe; reject
  cycles at write time elsewhere) and repeat; if still none, raise
  `CapabilityUnassignedError` (mapped to `503 capability_unassigned`
  by the API layer, plus a `CRITICAL` audit row at the call site).
- Caches the per-capability chain in-process for 30 s with an SSE-
  invalidation hook (`llm.assignment.changed`) so admin edits land
  fast without per-call DB reads.
- Inheritance resolution returns the **child**'s prompt overrides if
  any, else the parent's (`llm_prompt_template` lookup is delegated
  to the prompt-library task).
- Pure read path; no audit on resolve. Records resolve metadata on
  the eventual `llm_call` row (assignment id, fallback rung) for
  observability.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `resolve_model(ctx, 'chat.manager')` returns the seeded
  Gemma assignment when only the seed exists.
- [ ] Adding a higher-priority assignment (priority 0) demotes the
  seed (priority 1) on the next call without restart.
- [ ] `chat.admin` with no enabled assignments inherits
  `chat.manager`'s chain (per the v1 seed).
- [ ] An unknown capability raises `CapabilityUnassignedError`.
- [ ] Disabling every assignment for a capability with no parent
  raises `CapabilityUnassignedError`.
- [ ] Cache invalidates on `llm.assignment.changed` SSE event.
- [ ] Resolver does not perform any I/O against the LLM provider
  itself.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/test_router.py` exercise
  every branch above with seeded `llm_provider_model` and
  `llm_assignment` fixtures.
- Property test: random chains of length 1..5 always tried in
  ascending priority order.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(payroll/payslip): compute gross / multipliers / deductions / net
description:
## Problem / goal
For a `(pay_period, user)` pair: aggregate the user's shifts within
the window, apply the matching `PayRule` (with overtime / night /
weekend multipliers), subtract deductions, store the resulting
`Payslip` row.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Payslip",
  §"components_json schema", §"Pay-rule selection when multiple
  rules overlap".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`Payslip`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayPage.tsx` shows the
  payslip line items.

## Implementation notes
- Pure function `app/domain/payroll/compute.py::compute_payslip`:
  - Inputs: `(ctx, period, user_id)`.
  - Pulls shifts via `shifts.list_for_period(ctx, user_id, period)`.
  - Splits each shift into base / overtime / night / weekend buckets
    (helpers in `app/util/timewindow.py`).
  - Looks up effective `PayRule` per shift start.
  - Builds `components_json` per spec.
  - Computes `gross_cents`, `deductions_cents` (start with empty
    JSON list — populated by deduction plugins later), `net_cents
    = gross - sum(deductions)`.
- Wraps in `payslip_recompute(ctx, period_id)` that iterates all
  engaged users and upserts (`unique(period_id, user_id)`).
- Recompute is idempotent and **only allowed on `draft` or
  `locked` periods** (paid → 409).
- Emits `payslip.computed` event.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Shifts split correctly across midnight, weekend boundaries
- [ ] Overtime applied above weekly threshold per rule
- [ ] Multi-rule windows pick the rule active at shift start
- [ ] Idempotent recompute (running twice yields identical row)
- [ ] Money math in integer cents only — no floats
- [ ] Unit tests cover canonical cases from §09

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_payslip_compute.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_payslip_recompute.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): Schedule CRUD with RFC 5545 RRULE + DTSTART/UNTIL
description:
## Problem / goal
A schedule says "create a task from this template on these dates at
these times". Stored as RFC 5545 RRULE plus the property-local
DTSTART. Without it, recurring tasks cannot be authored.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Schedule",
  §"UI expose-levels", §"Pause / resume", §"Deleting and editing",
  §"Pause vs active range"; §"Assignment algorithm" for the
  `default_assignee` + `backup_assignee_user_ids` shape.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx` renders
  `name`, `template_id`, `property_id`, `rrule_human`,
  `default_assignee_id`, `duration_minutes`, `paused`, `active_from`.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/schedules.py`.
- Pydantic DTOs: `ScheduleCreate`, `ScheduleUpdate`, `ScheduleView`.
  Fields per §06 table: `name`, `template_id`, `property_id`,
  `area_id`, `default_assignee` (user id),
  `backup_assignee_user_ids` (ordered list), `rrule`,
  `dtstart_local`, `duration_minutes`, `rdate_local`, `exdate_local`,
  `active_from`, `active_until`, `paused_at`.
- RRULE validation: parse with `dateutil.rrule.rrulestr` against
  `dtstart_local`; reject if it does not produce at least one
  occurrence or if it is unbounded with no `active_until` AND no
  `COUNT/UNTIL` (configurable safeguard — record an audit warning
  but allow, since §06 expects open-ended weeklies).
- `RDATE` / `EXDATE` are line-separated ISO-8601 local timestamps;
  store as `text` exactly as written and re-parse on use.
- `default_assignee` resolution: validate the user holds a
  `user_work_role` matching `template.role_id` at the schedule's
  workspace; same for every entry in `backup_assignee_user_ids` —
  return 422 `error = "backup_invalid_work_role"` per §06.
- `pause(id)` / `resume(id)` toggle `paused_at`. Pause does not
  cancel already-materialised tasks.
- `delete(id)` soft-deletes and cancels every linked task with
  `state IN ('scheduled')` to `cancelled` with
  `cancellation_reason = 'schedule deleted'` (per §06).
- `update(id, payload, *, apply_to_existing=False)` — when
  `apply_to_existing=True`, patch tasks with
  `state IN ('scheduled', 'pending')` only (per §06 "Deleting and
  editing").
- Helper: `preview_occurrences(rrule, dtstart_local, *, n=5,
  rdate, exdate)` returning the next N local datetimes — drives the
  `/schedules` "Preview — next 7 days" panel and the editor's "next
  5 occurrences" preview.
- `WorkspaceContext` for every method; audit on every mutation
  (`schedule.create | update | pause | resume | delete | apply_to_existing`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD + pause/resume working end-to-end
- [ ] Backup-list validation rejects users without the right
      work-role at write time
- [ ] `delete()` cascades to `state=scheduled` task cancellation
- [ ] `apply_to_existing` patches `scheduled|pending` only
- [ ] `preview_occurrences` honours RDATE/EXDATE
- [ ] Audit row per mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover RRULE round-trip, pause-vs-active-range
      precedence, and each invariant

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_schedules.py -xvs
```

title: feat(ops): secret rotation CLI for SMTP, OpenRouter, HMAC, session secret + docs
description:
## Problem / goal
Operators need a documented, low-risk way to rotate the
deployment's third-party secrets — SMTP credentials, OpenRouter
key, the HMAC signing key (for guest welcome URLs and
file-signed-URL handouts), and the session-cookie signing
secret — without an outage. Today only the root-key rotation has
a CLI; this task adds parity for the others.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Secrets
  management" (root-key rotation is the model: never via argv,
  file or stdin only, zero-fill in-memory copy); §"Cookies"
  (session cookie value is opaque random — rotation = invalidate
  all sessions).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"TLS"
  + Recipes A/B for where the env vars are wired.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/admin/rotate_secret.py` with one entrypoint per secret:
  - `crewday admin rotate-smtp --new-cred-file <path>`: validates
    the new credentials by issuing a benign `EHLO` to `SMTP_HOST`,
    then writes them via the deployment-setting writer (atomic),
    audits `secrets.smtp.rotated`.
  - `crewday admin rotate-openrouter --new-key-file <path>`:
    validates by calling `models.list` against OpenRouter,
    then atomic-swap, audits `secrets.openrouter.rotated`.
  - `crewday admin rotate-hmac --new-key-file <path>`: writes the
    new key into the active slot and keeps the old key in a 72-hour
    legacy slot so in-flight signed URLs continue to verify;
    audits `secrets.hmac.rotated`.
  - `crewday admin rotate-session-secret --new-key-file <path>`:
    swap atomically AND invalidate every live session row (the
    session cookie value is opaque so a fresh secret does not by
    itself invalidate anything; we explicitly clear `sessions`).
- Same argv / stdin / file rules as `rotate-root-key` (no key on
  argv, file mode `0600`, stdin must not be a TTY, zero-fill).
- New docs section `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md →
  "Secret lifecycle"` (added in this task, cross-referenced from
  §15) listing each secret + rotation cadence + verification step.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each rotation command runs end-to-end against the dev stack
      and audits its action
- [ ] Argv-delivered key forms refuse with the spec error message
- [ ] HMAC rotation keeps a 72-hour legacy slot — old signed URLs
      still verify until expiry
- [ ] Session-secret rotation clears every live session row
- [ ] OpenRouter rotation validates the new key with a real probe
      before persisting
- [ ] Docs section published with each command + cadence

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_smtp.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_openrouter.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_hmac.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_session.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/auth): passkey login, 401 → /login, token refresh, workspace selector
description:
## Problem / goal
Wire passkey-based authentication into the SPA: silent token refresh
where possible, redirect to `/login` on any 401 (except public
routes), and surface a workspace-selector when the user has no active
slug.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Auth", §"Workspace
  selector"; `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Passkey",
  §"Sessions".
- **Reference**: any auth-aware bits in `mocks/web/src/pages/login*`
  (verbatim where present) and `WorkspaceSwitcher`.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/web/src/auth/` module:
  - `authStore.ts` — current user, token, expiry; persists token to
    `httpOnly`-equivalent storage where supported, falls back to
    `localStorage` with sensible CSRF posture.
  - `useAuth()` hook returns user + login/logout helpers.
  - `RequireAuth.tsx` route guard — wraps protected route
    branches; on 401 from `api.ts`, redirects to `/login?next=...`.
  - `LoginPage.tsx` — passkey ceremony via WebAuthn API; on success
    populates `authStore` and replays `next`.
  - `WorkspaceGate.tsx` — when authenticated but no slug active,
    show `WorkspaceSwitcher` modal with the user's workspace list
    (from `/me/workspaces`); blocks render until a slug is picked.
- `api.ts` calls a single `onUnauthorized()` hook so the redirect
  is centralised.
- Public routes (login, public booking pages, share links) are
  whitelisted in `RequireAuth`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Hitting any protected route while logged-out → `/login?next=`
- [ ] Successful passkey login lands on `next` (or `/`)
- [ ] 401 mid-session triggers redirect + clears auth store
- [ ] Workspace selector appears when user has no active slug
- [ ] Logout clears storage + closes SSE
- [ ] Unit + Playwright tests cover the full ceremony

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/auth
# Playwright: register passkey, log out, log back in, switch workspace
```

title: feat(web/context): copy + wire SseContext, RoleContext, WorkspaceContext, ThemeContext, NavHistoryContext, ShellNavContext
description:
## Problem / goal
The contexts power role gating, workspace selection, theme, SSE
fan-out, navigation history (for back/forward), and the shell nav
bar. Copy them verbatim from `mocks/web/src/context/` and rewire to
the real API + auth surfaces.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Contexts",
  §"SSE-driven invalidation", §"Workspace switching".
- **Reference (COPY THEN ADAPT)**:
  - `mocks/web/src/context/SseContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/RoleContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/WorkspaceContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/ThemeContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/NavHistoryContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/ShellNavContext.tsx`

## Implementation notes
- Copy each file into `app/web/src/context/` unchanged where
  possible.
- **Adaptations** (kept tight; each called out in the PR):
  - `SseContext` — open `EventSource('/w/<slug>/events')` (real
    workspace slug from `WorkspaceContext`); reconnect with
    exponential backoff; expose typed event subscription API as in
    mocks. Real query-key invalidation lands in Task
    `p16.sse-wiring`.
  - `WorkspaceContext` — load `/me/workspaces` for the slug list;
    persist active slug in `localStorage`; fire `onChange` so SSE +
    api client reconfigure.
  - `RoleContext` — derive role from `/me` for the active workspace
    (no more mock role toggle); keep the same hook signature.
  - `ThemeContext`, `NavHistoryContext`, `ShellNavContext` —
    verbatim; they don't touch the API.
- Public hook signatures (`useSse`, `useRole`, `useWorkspace`,
  `useTheme`, `useNavHistory`, `useShellNav`) must remain identical
  so component imports don't churn.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each context file in `app/web/src/context/` exports the same
      hook signatures as the mock
- [ ] `SseContext` reconnects with backoff; closes on logout
- [ ] `WorkspaceContext` persists + restores the active slug
- [ ] No mock-data imports left behind
- [ ] Unit tests cover hook behaviour with mocked fetch / EventSource

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/context
cd app/web && npm run build
```

title: feat(web/admin): LlmPage — providers, capability→model, prompts, sandbox
description:
## Problem / goal
The deployment-wide LLM control panel: configure providers, map
capabilities to models (`model_assignment`), preview prompt
templates, and run a test prompt against any provider for parity
checking. Without it the operator can't switch providers safely.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/llm`; §11 LLM and agents §"Capability registry"; §12
  model-router.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LlmPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `StatCard`, `Chip`, `Loading`;
  three-column miller layout (provider → model → assignment →
  capability) with selection state.
- **API endpoints**:
  `GET /admin/api/v1/llm/graph`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/llm/calls`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/llm/prompts`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/llm/sync_pricing`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/llm/assignments` (body: `{ capability,
  model_id, provider }`),
  `POST /admin/api/v1/llm/sandbox` (test sandbox call).
- **Types**: `LLMCall`, `LlmAssignment`, `LlmGraphPayload`,
  `LlmPromptTemplate`, `LlmSyncPricingResult`.

## Implementation notes
- The mock is ~825 lines — **split it** during promotion into a
  tree under `app/web/src/pages/admin/llm/`, keeping
  `LlmPage.tsx` itself under ~300 lines. Suggested split:
  - `ProviderColumn.tsx` — providers list (left column in the
    miller layout).
  - `ModelColumn.tsx` — models list for the selected provider.
  - `AssignmentColumn.tsx` — capability→model assignments for the
    selected model.
  - `CapabilityChain.tsx` — capability chain render (right column).
  - `ProviderModelPricing.tsx` — "Provider-model pricing" section
    (sync-pricing button + table at h2 `Pricing sync`).
  - `RecentCalls.tsx` — `Recent calls` table.
  - `PromptLibraryDrawer.tsx` — prompts drawer toggled by
    `promptsOpen`.
  - `lib/llmIndexes.ts` — the `assignmentsByCapability` / graph
    index helpers.
  - `LlmPage.tsx` — orchestrator composing the columns and owning
    query / SSE wiring.
- Refactoring is **only allowed** if rendered DOM + class names are
  unchanged — visual-diff is the hard gate.
- Preserve the column-selection mechanics + `useLayoutEffect` scroll
  pinning behaviour from the mock — visual parity matters.
- Mutations: invalidate `qk.adminLlmGraph()`, `qk.adminLlmCalls()`,
  `qk.adminLlmPrompts()` on success.
- SSE `llm.assignment.changed` invalidates the graph; SSE
  `llm.call.appended` prepends to the call list.
- Sandbox call posts the prompt + selected model and renders the
  raw provider response inline; do **not** persist to `llm_usage`
  unless the user opts in (mock has the toggle).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Provider / model / assignment / capability columns sync
      identically to mock.
- [ ] Capability→model assignment writes back and refreshes the
      graph.
- [ ] Sandbox call returns latency + tokens + cost preview.
- [ ] `sync_pricing` triggers a server refresh and shows a toast
      with the result.
- [ ] Permission gate: deployment-admin only.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~300 lines.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_llm.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-llm.spec.ts
```

title: feat(web/admin): AuditPage — deployment-scope audit log + filters
description:
## Problem / goal
Every admin action is auditable; this page is the read-side. Filter
by actor kind (user / agent / system), scope, action, time range.
Required for incident response and compliance per §0 audit spec.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/audit` — deployment-scope audit log; §0 audit
  requirements.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`, ACTOR_TONE map
  (`user→moss`, `agent→sky`, `system→ghost`).
- **API endpoints**:
  `GET /admin/api/v1/audit?actor_kind=&action=&since=&until=`.
- **Types**: `AuditEntry` (must include `scope_kind`,
  `actor_kind`, `actor_id`, `action`, `payload_json`,
  `created_at`).

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx`;
  filter UI controls + URL search-params sync.
- Server filters where `scope_kind = 'deployment'` only; this is
  enforced server-side, not client-side.
- TanStack Query keyed by the filter object so URL ↔ cache stay
  in sync.
- Wire SSE `audit.appended` (with `scope_kind=='deployment'` filter)
  to invalidate the current query.
- Preserve every column, the actor-tone chip, and the timestamp
  format from the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page lists deployment-scope rows only; cross-tenant data
      never appears in the response.
- [ ] Filter controls round-trip via URL search-params.
- [ ] SSE appends a fresh entry to the top of the list without a
      full re-render.
- [ ] Non-admin gets the standard not-authorised card.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_audit.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-audit.spec.ts
```

title: feat(inventory): reorder rule engine + low-stock notification
description:
## Problem / goal
When stock for an item drops to or below its `reorder_point`, the
system should ensure exactly one open restock task exists and emit a
`inventory.low_stock` event so notifications and external dashboards
can react. Hourly worker job runs the check; movement events trigger
incremental re-checks.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"Reorder logic"; event bus
  `docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md`.
- DB tables from `p1.inventory`; restock task creation depends on the
  task service (eventually `p5.assignment` will own task creation
  helpers — until then, call the existing template service directly).

## Implementation notes
- Worker job `app/jobs/inventory_reorder.py` registered with the
  scheduler (runs hourly).
- Subscriber on `inventory.item_changed` for incremental triggering
  on the items just touched.
- For each item where `on_hand <= reorder_point` and no open
  restock task exists for (property, item), create one from the
  property's "restock" template (default name "Restock {item}",
  role-assigned via the workspace's `restock_assignee_role`
  setting — fall back to `property_manager`).
- Emit `inventory.low_stock` (payload: workspace_id, property_id,
  item_id, on_hand, reorder_point, restock_task_id).
- Idempotent: if a restock task is already open, no second one is
  created and no second event is fired (dedupe on item_id).
- Audit row when a restock task is auto-created (action
  `inventory.auto_restock_created`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Below threshold → restock task created + event fired (once).
- [ ] Subsequent dips while task is open → no new task, no new
      event.
- [ ] Restock task completion is handled by the existing task
      completion flow (out of scope; only verify the seam).
- [ ] Items above threshold ignored.
- [ ] Hourly job is idempotent.
- [ ] Unit tests + integration test exercising full
      movement → low-stock → task → completion → movement loop.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_reorder.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_inventory_reorder_loop.py -xvs
```

title: feat(domain/stays): guest welcome pages — /w/<slug>/guest/<token>
description:
## Problem / goal
Public, no-login guest welcome pages served by signed tokens
(\`itsdangerous\`). Shows wifi, house rules, check-out checklist,
emergencies. Token includes \`property_id\` + \`reservation_id\` and a
short TTL; never exposes \`workspace_id\` directly.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Guest welcome
  link" — covers fields, what the page shows, privacy + 410 Gone
  semantics.
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"guest_link".
- **Auth**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` for the signed-token
  format.
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/public/GuestPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (GUEST_LINK fixture).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/stays/guest_link_service.py\` — \`mint_link\`,
  \`revoke_link\`, \`resolve_link\`, \`record_access\`.
- \`itsdangerous.URLSafeTimedSerializer\` keyed off the deployment
  secret; payload \`{stay_id, property_id, exp}\` — no workspace_id.
- Slug obfuscation via the Phase 0 hashids util; route is
  \`/w/<workspace_slug>/guest/<token>\` per existing URL grammar.
- Page renderer merges welcome data: \`stay.wifi_password_override\`
  > \`unit.welcome_overrides_json\` > \`property.welcome_defaults_json\`.
- Filter check-out checklist to items where
  \`guest_visible = true\`.
- Revoke / expiry → 410 Gone with the spec's wording; no stay payload.
- Access log records hashed-IP-prefix + UA-family only (last 10).
- No cookies; no JS beyond the minimal manifest.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Token format signed with TTL; tampered token returns 410
- [ ] Revoked / expired link returns 410 with the spec wording
- [ ] No cookies set on the guest page
- [ ] Welcome merge order matches the spec
- [ ] Equipment section appears only when setting + visible assets
- [ ] Access log truncates to last 10 + hashes IP
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover happy, expired, revoked, tampered token paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_guest_link_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_guest_welcome_page.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(cli/runtime): _runtime.py dynamic Click command builder
description:
## Problem / goal
At CLI startup, `_runtime.py` reads the committed `_surface.json` and
attaches every generated command to the Click root, so `crewday
<resource> <verb>` works without any hand-written wiring. Each command
maps its parameters to Click options, wires the HTTP client, and
invokes the formatter.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Runtime", §"Parameter mapping",
  §"Pagination", §"Idempotency-Key".
- **Inputs**: `_surface.json` (Task `p15.codegen`), `_client.py`
  (Task `p15.http-client`), `_output.py` (Task
  `p15.output-formatters`).

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_runtime.py`:
  - `register_generated_commands(root: click.Group) -> None`.
  - For each surface entry: build `click.Command` with
    type-converted Click options for path params, query params, and
    body fields. Required flags map to required Click options.
  - JSON body construction: `--field key=value` repeating option +
    `--body-file <path>` escape hatch.
  - Pagination: when response is a list with `next_cursor`, expose
    `--all` to follow cursors and stream NDJSON or aggregate JSON
    depending on `--output`.
  - Mutating verbs (POST/PATCH/DELETE): auto-attach
    `Idempotency-Key` header (ULID); allow `--idempotency-key`
    override.
  - Errors: 4xx/5xx mapped to `click.ClickException` with the
    server-supplied message.
- Wire from `_main.py` so generated commands appear under the root
  group automatically.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every entry in `_surface.json` becomes a callable command
- [ ] Required + optional params map to Click options correctly
- [ ] `--field key=value` builds JSON bodies; `--body-file` works
- [ ] Mutating calls send `Idempotency-Key` automatically
- [ ] `--all` follows pagination cursors
- [ ] Unit tests cover parameter mapping + pagination

## Test plan
```bash
uv run crewday tasks list --help
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_runtime.py -xvs
```

title: feat(ops): /healthz, /readyz, /version endpoints + worker heartbeat in readiness
description:
## Problem / goal
Reverse proxies, container orchestrators, and the `crewday admin
status` CLI all need a stable contract for "is this app alive and
ready?". `/healthz` is liveness (process up), `/readyz` is
readiness (DB reachable, migrations current, worker heartbeat
fresh, root key loaded), `/version` exposes the build digest.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe A"
  (Caddy reverse-proxy posture; `app:8000` healthcheck assumption),
  §"Recipe B" (DB + MinIO healthchecks point at the
  app-side endpoints implicitly).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Rate limiting"
  ("Non-auth health endpoints: unlimited but with expensive DB
  calls deferred").

## Implementation notes
- New `app/api/health.py`:
  - `GET /healthz` → 200 with `{"status": "ok"}` if process is
    serving requests; bypasses every middleware that could 5xx
    (auth, tenancy).
  - `GET /readyz` → 200 only when:
    - DB engine reachable (`SELECT 1`, < 100 ms timeout)
    - Migrations current (`alembic current` matches head)
    - Worker heartbeat written within the last 60 s
      (a row in a small `worker_heartbeat` table the worker
      updates every 30 s)
    - Root key loaded
    - Otherwise 503 with a JSON body listing failing checks.
  - `GET /version` → `{"version": "<semver>", "git_sha": "<sha>",
    "build_at": "<utc>", "image_digest": "<sha256>"}`. Built by
    the release pipeline and baked into the image.
- All three are exempt from rate-limit, no DB calls on `/healthz`
  (so a degraded DB does not 5xx liveness), no auth, no tenancy
  context.
- Capability `runtime.background_worker` keys whether the readiness
  check waits for the heartbeat (single-container with in-process
  worker → yes; orchestrated worker container → also yes; demo →
  yes).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `/healthz` returns 200 even when the DB is down (asserted via
      a fault-injected DB)
- [ ] `/readyz` returns 503 when migrations lag head (asserted by
      stamping an older `alembic_version`)
- [ ] `/readyz` returns 503 when the worker heartbeat is older than
      60 s (asserted by stopping the worker)
- [ ] `/version` returns the baked build metadata
- [ ] All three endpoints respond < 50 ms p95 under steady load
- [ ] Cookies / auth not required; CSP not blocking JSON response

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_health.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_readyz.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/manager): instruction detail — markdown editor + version history
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager edits an instruction's markdown body, scope, and notification policy; previous versions are kept and visible in the history drawer.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/instructions/<iid>` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, MarkdownEditor, VersionDrawer.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/instructions/{iid}, GET /api/v1/properties, PATCH /api/v1/instructions/{iid}, GET /api/v1/instructions/{iid}/versions.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The markdown editor is the shared component from Phase 16 (no third-party WYSIWYG); preserve the same toolbar order.
- Saving a new version must SSE-broadcast `instruction.upserted` so the worker /kb cache invalidates.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/instructions/<iid>`.

title: feat(web/manager): permission groups + role grants + capability resolver
description:
## Problem / goal
Owner / manager curates permission groups (e.g. Cleaners, Senior Manager), grants capabilities by action catalog, and previews the effective permission set for any user.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look

responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PermissionsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/permissions` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, PermissionRulesEditor, ResolverPanel.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/workspaces, GET /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/permissions/groups, GET /api/v1/permissions/groups/{gid}/members, GET /api/v1/permissions/action_catalog, PATCH /api/v1/permissions/groups/{gid}/rules, POST /api/v1/permissions/resolve.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PermissionsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**,
  but the mock is ~450 lines and already has internal
  sub-components (`GroupsTab`, `RulesTab`, `RuleChip`,
  `WhoCanDoThis`) — **split it** during promotion into a tree
  under `app/web/src/pages/manager/permissions/`, keeping
  `PermissionsPage.tsx` itself under ~200 lines. Suggested split:
  - `GroupsTab.tsx` — groups list + editor (from mock l.71).
  - `RulesTab.tsx` — rules list + editor (from mock l.218).
  - `RuleChip.tsx` — inline chip (from mock l.352).
  - `WhoCanDoThis.tsx` — capability resolver panel (from mock l.374).
  - `lib/usePermissionIndexes.ts` — `useWorkspaces`,
    `useUsersIndex` hooks (from mock l.53 / l.60).
  - `PermissionsPage.tsx` — orchestrator: tab state, route-level
    guard, SSE wiring.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons —
  rendered DOM and class names must be byte-equivalent to the mock.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The resolver call is read-only and idempotent — never fire it on every keystroke; debounce 300ms.
- Editing rules opens a draft state; nothing persists until "Save" — preserve the mock's draft model.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~200 lines.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline

title: feat(api): cursor-based pagination helpers (ULID cursors, no offset)
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §"Pagination" mandates cursor-based pagination on every
collection endpoint, with \`limit\` default 50 / max 500 and
opaque \`next_cursor\`. This task provides a single helper that
every router calls: encode/decode cursors over (sort key, ULID),
clamp limit, and assemble the \`{data, next_cursor, has_more,
total_estimate}\` envelope.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Pagination",
  §"Request/response shape".
- **Mocks consuming**: every paginated list in
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/*\` and \`worker/*\` (tasks,
  occurrences, reservations, expenses, payslips, work orders…)
  uses TanStack Query's \`useInfiniteQuery\` keyed off
  \`next_cursor\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/pagination.py\`:
  - \`@dataclass class Cursor:\` — opaque base64url of
    \`(last_sort_value, last_id_ulid)\`. Signed with the
    deployment secret to prevent tampering.
  - \`paginate(query, *, sort, limit, cursor) -> Page[T]\` —
    SQLAlchemy generic helper.
  - \`Page[T]\` pydantic generic with \`data\`, \`next_cursor\`,
    \`has_more\`, optional \`total_estimate\` (only when caller
    explicitly opts in via \`?include_total=true\`).
- Limit clamp: default 50, max 500; out-of-range returns 400
  \`validation\`.
- ULID-based tie-break ensures stable pagination across writes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Helper works for any sort field documented per resource
- [ ] Tampered cursor → 400 \`validation\`
- [ ] limit > 500 → 400; limit absent → 50
- [ ] \`has_more=false\` when fewer than \`limit\` rows returned
- [ ] \`total_estimate\` only present when opted-in
- [ ] Tests cover stable-pagination-under-write scenario

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_pagination.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_pagination_stability.py -xvs
\`\`\`

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Autofix complete. Findings fixed: (1) origin not normalised — uppercase host, path/query/fragment, userinfo would each produce a runtime mismatch with clientDataJSON.origin (py_webauthn does byte-exact comparison); added _canonical_origin() that parses + rebuilds scheme://host[:port] with lowercasing and validates scheme in {http,https} + non-empty host + valid port. (2) rp_id override compared case-sensitively even though DNS is case-insensitive; now casefold-ed on both sides and stored lowercased. (3) Dev fallback scheme heuristic flipped https:// for non-loopback bindings which would mismatch plaintext dev; simplified to unconditional http://. Added 10 new tests covering each edge case (30 total, all pass). 749 unit+integration tests pass total (was 739), mypy strict, ruff + format, lint-imports kept. cd-65n7 capability probe remains deferred as noted.

title: feat(workspace): owner-only settings (name, timezone, locale, currency)
description:
## Problem / goal
A workspace's identity-level settings — display name, default
timezone, default locale, default currency — are mutable but only by
members of the `owners` permission group on the workspace. The rest
of the settings cascade lives elsewhere; this task ships the four
top-level fields plus their audit + cache-invalidation hooks.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"workspace" (the
  base columns), §"Settings cascade" (this task is the
  workspace-layer for the four named keys); `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md`
  §"Surface grants at a glance" (owners as governance anchor);
  `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` for the settings UI surface.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SettingsPage.tsx` (one
  surface; this task delivers the API + service the page targets).
- Authorization: `scope.edit_settings` on `workspace` scope, but
  the four root-level fields are restricted to `owners` per spec
  intent — gate via `groups.manage_owners_membership`-adjacent
  check using the `owners` group.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/workspace/settings_service.py` with
  `update_basics(ctx, *, name, timezone, locale, currency)`.
- Validate timezone against IANA tz database; locale against the
  shipped locale list (`p0.util`); currency against ISO-4217.
- Changing timezone DOES NOT rewrite stored timestamps (UTC at
  rest); display-time conversion is responsibility of the
  frontend/property tz override layer.
- Bump a `workspace.updated_at` column so SSE subscribers can
  refresh the workspace picker.
- Audit row per change with old/new values per field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `PATCH /workspaces/{ws}/settings/basics` accepts the four
      fields, partial update, ignores extras.
- [ ] Non-owner caller → 403.
- [ ] Invalid tz / locale / currency → 422 with field-specific
      error.
- [ ] Audit row written with field-level diff.
- [ ] Unit tests: valid update, partial update, each validation
      failure, unauthorized caller.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_workspace_settings.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_workspace_settings.py -xvs
```

title: test(e2e): playwright scaffold + auth helper + screenshot diff harness
description:
## Problem / goal
End-to-end browser tests gate the §17 release journeys: install +
first-boot enrollment, property/area/user creation + invite flow,
iCal → turnover → completion → guest-welcome flow, expense → autofill
→ approval → payslip flow, and an agent-driven task lifecycle. The
visual-regression harness keeps the styleguide pixel-stable.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"End-to-end"
  (Playwright Python bindings, headed locally, headless in CI; five
  GA journeys; passkey via WebAuthn virtual authenticator),
  §"Visual regression" (Playwright + `pixelmatch`,
  `/styleguide` baseline at 0.1% diff, other routes at 0.5%),
  §"360 px viewport sitemap" (full sitemap walk at 360×780, fail
  on any horizontal scroll / tap target < 44×44 / unreachable nav).

## Implementation notes
- New `tests/e2e/` with `playwright.config.ts` and Python bindings.
  Browsers: Chromium + WebKit (passkey ceremony coverage).
- `tests/e2e/_helpers/auth.py`:
  - `enroll_owner(page, email)` — full first-boot magic-link →
    passkey via WebAuthn virtual authenticator
  - `login_as(page, email)` — re-auth via virtual authenticator
- `tests/e2e/_helpers/visual.py`:
  - `compare_screenshot(page, name)` — pixelmatch against
    committed baseline; threshold per route (0.1% on
    `/styleguide`, 0.5% elsewhere)
- Pilot journey: install + first-boot owner enrollment lands in
  this task; the other four are filed as follow-up Beads tasks.
- 360 px sitemap walk: parametrise over the full authenticated
  sitemap (built from `_surface.json`), fail on any horizontal
  scroll or undersized tap target.
- Headless in CI; tracing on (first failure → trace.zip artefact).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `pnpm playwright test` runs locally (headed) and in CI
      (headless) against the dev stack
- [ ] First-boot owner-enrollment journey passes end-to-end on
      Chromium + WebKit
- [ ] WebAuthn virtual authenticator successfully completes both
      registration and assertion
- [ ] `/styleguide` screenshot diff fires on a deliberate
      pixel change
- [ ] 360×780 sitemap walk fails on a deliberately-introduced
      horizontal scroll
- [ ] CI uploads trace.zip on failure

## Test plan
```bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d
pnpm playwright test --project=chromium tests/e2e
pnpm playwright test --project=webkit tests/e2e
```

title: fix(tenancy): SQLAlchemy event dispatch quirk — listener registered on sessionmaker but Session.dispatch empty
description:
## Observed behaviour
Building multiple sessionmaker instances in sequence, each bound to the same engine and each receiving an install_tenant_filter() call, can leave the third+ session with an EMPTY session.dispatch.do_orm_execute list even though event.contains(factory, 'do_orm_execute', hook) returns True. The hook silently does not fire and tenant-filter enforcement is bypassed.

## Repro (as of 2026-04-19, SQLAlchemy 2.x)
Reliable with three consecutive tests in tests/integration/test_audit_writer.py (commit → rollback → filter-missing) when filtered_factory is function-scoped and rebuilt each test. The third test's fresh factory reports event.contains==True but list(session.dispatch.do_orm_execute) == [] and the SELECT runs unfiltered.

## Workaround shipped
Module-scoped filtered_factory in the integration test (one sessionmaker per file). Idempotence of install_tenant_filter keeps this safe. Documented inline.

## Risk
Any future production code path that builds multiple sessionmakers on the same engine chain (e.g. per-request sessionmaker for request isolation) could silently drop the tenant filter. The _default_sessionmaker_ is singleton so today's runtime is unaffected, but this is a latent trap.

## Possible root causes
1. SQLAlchemy 2.x sessionmaker event propagation uses a weak-reference dispatch cache that the third factory's creation evicts.
2. Interaction with the join_transaction_mode='create_savepoint' sessionmaker in tests/integration/conftest.py::db_session — even though we don't use that fixture in the filtered test, the factory still goes through the Session class dispatch.
3. SQLAlchemy's _Session.dispatch lazily composes from the sessionmaker and Session-class dispatches; some ordering produces an empty composition.

## Follow-ups
1. Minimal repro outside pytest (pure script).
2. Report upstream if confirmed.
3. Consider moving tenant filter installation to the Session class directly (one-time, at import of app.adapters.db.session) so per-sessionmaker churn cannot drop it.

## Related
- cd-3yhd — sibling heisenbug where get_final_froms() on a cold ORM Join silently loses a side.
- cd-52k / cd-ehf — consumers of this filter.

title: feat(web/client): BillableHoursPage — booking_billing rollup, redacted of pay rates
description:
## Problem / goal
The client sees the agency's hourly billing for work performed on
their properties — totals per booking, per worker, per period.
Worker pay rates are deliberately **not** shown; only the agency's
charge rate (`booking_billing.hourly_cents`).



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell";
  §22 clients-and-vendors §"Redactions" — never expose
  `pay_rule.rate`, only `booking_billing.hourly_cents`.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/BillableHoursPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`; `formatMoney`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`, `GET /api/v1/users`,
  `GET /api/v1/organizations`,
  `GET /api/v1/booking_billings?client_org_id=<oid>` (one call per
  binding org, then flatten — mirrors mock).
- **Types**: `BookingBilling`, `Me`, `Organization`, `User`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/client/BillableHoursPage.tsx`.
- The server endpoint MUST strip pay-rate fields from the response
  payload when called by a client role (defence in depth even
  though the schema doesn't expose them).
- Group rows by booking; show worker name (first name only per
  §15 cross-workspace) + role + hours + agency rate + total.
- TanStack Query keyed by `qk.bookingBillings(orgIds.join(","))`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page renders identical to mock with the redacted columns.
- [ ] Server response never includes `pay_rule.rate` for a client
      caller (verified by integration test).
- [ ] Sums round correctly to the displayed total per booking.
- [ ] Permission gate: client role on the active workspace.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_client_billable_hours.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/client-billable-hours.spec.ts
```
