comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Autofix complete. Findings:
- BUG: _probe_features used 'postgres or _sqlite_has_fts5()' which returned True on unsupported backends (mysql/oracle) whenever the interpreter's SQLite had FTS5. Fixed by gating on an explicit _is_sqlite() check; added mysql/oracle regression tests.
- RISKY: refresh_settings mutated fields as it coerced them, so a bad JSON payload on any later field left earlier fields half-applied. Fixed by staging every coercion into locals and only writing back to self.settings after all four succeed; added integration test with a non-numeric budget payload.

cd-3yhd flake investigation: narrowed root cause to _collect_scoped_targets mis-walking the _ORMJoin's left side on cold cache paths (only one of the two scoped tables is collected). Any side-effect inside the walker (print, sys.stderr.write) warms the state and the second table gets collected. Tried 5 non-trivial fixes (list(), and_() single-where, session-scoped engine, dispose teardown, getattr touches) — none help. Left cd-3yhd OPEN with detailed analysis + suggested next-step fixes (replace get_final_froms with a manual visitor traversal). The flake still reproduces on the full suite at ~20% rate; isolated tenancy tests pass consistently.

title: feat(ops): crewday admin init/user-invite/workspace-bootstrap composite commands
description:
## Problem / goal
First-boot of a self-host deployment needs a deterministic CLI
path: `crewday admin init` provisions the deployment-scope row +
the root key + the first owner; `crewday admin user invite`
mints a magic-link for a new user without going through the HTTP
surface; `crewday admin workspace bootstrap` is the recovery hook
when an operator needs to re-create a workspace from scratch.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe A"
  ("Lockout recovery" is host-CLI-only; this task lands the
  composite verbs that recovery uses), §"Recipe D"
  ("admin worker container") for the SaaS path.
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth.md` (referenced via §02) — magic
  link generation; this task reuses the same code path,
  `via='cli'` actor.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/admin/init.py`:
  - `crewday admin init [--data-dir /data] [--root-key-file
    <path>]`: idempotent. If no root key found in env or
    `--root-key-file`, generates one and prints it once with a
    "store this immediately" warning. Creates the `deployment_setting`
    seed row, applies migrations, writes initial capability flags.
  - `crewday admin user invite --email … --workspace <slug>
    [--role owner|manager|worker|client]`: mints a magic-link
    using the §03 generator with `via='cli'`, prints the URL to
    stdout (no email send — operator may copy it manually).
  - `crewday admin workspace bootstrap --slug … --name …
    --owner-email …`: composite of `workspace.create` +
    `user_workspace.add(role=owner)` + `user invite`. Useful
    for re-seeding a deployment without web UI.
- Each command writes `audit_log` rows directly with `actor_kind=
  'system'`, `via='cli'`. Composite commands wrap their work in a
  single transaction.
- Argv parsing uses the existing CLI surface (§13 group `admin`).
- When `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1`, every command refuses with the spec
  message ("admin commands not available in demo").

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crewday admin init` is idempotent — second run is a no-op
- [ ] Generated root key is printed once; not stored anywhere on
      disk by the command
- [ ] `crewday admin user invite` prints a working magic-link URL;
      consumed once → workspace member
- [ ] `crewday admin workspace bootstrap` produces a usable
      workspace + owner row in a single transaction
- [ ] Every command writes audit rows under `actor_kind='system'`,
      `via='cli'`
- [ ] All three refuse to run under `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1`

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_init.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_user_invite.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_workspace_bootstrap.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/pwa): vite-plugin-pwa service worker + IndexedDB offline queue + background sync
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers complete tasks in low-connectivity properties; the SPA must
queue completions in IndexedDB and replay them via background sync
when connectivity returns. Also gives the app installable PWA
behaviour (install prompt, offline shell).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"PWA", §"Offline
  queue", §"Background sync".

## Implementation notes
- Add `vite-plugin-pwa` to `app/web/package.json`; configure in
  `vite.config.ts` with:
  - `registerType: 'autoUpdate'`
  - `manifest`: name, short_name, icons (256/512), theme_color from
    `tokens.css`, display `standalone`.
  - `workbox.runtimeCaching` — stale-while-revalidate for
    `/static/*`; network-first for `/w/*/api/*`; cache-first for
    `/icons/*`.
- New `app/web/src/lib/offlineQueue.ts`:
  - IndexedDB store `pending-mutations` with `id, kind, payload,
    createdAt, attempts`.
  - `enqueue(mutation)` from `useDecideMutation` /
    `useCompleteOccurrence` when navigator is offline.
  - Background-sync registration (`sw-sync`) drains the queue when
    online; per-item retry with exponential backoff; `Idempotency-
    Key` per mutation so server dedupes safely.
  - Surface a small "queued — will sync" badge in the shell when
    pending count > 0.
- Service worker stays tiny — defer heavy logic to the page.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] App is installable (Lighthouse PWA audit passes)
- [ ] Offline `complete occurrence` enqueues and replays on reconnect
- [ ] Each enqueued mutation carries a stable `Idempotency-Key`
- [ ] Pending-count badge visible while queue is non-empty
- [ ] Unit tests cover queue + retry; Playwright covers offline →
      online round-trip

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run build
# Lighthouse PWA audit on the built dist
# DevTools offline → mutate → online → verify replay
```

title: feat(agent/runtime): plan loop, tool dispatch, audit on every action
description:
## Problem / goal
The agent runtime executes a turn: render system + history + user
message, ask the LLM for a plan / tool call, dispatch the tool
against the CLI/REST surface using the **delegating user's
delegated token**, append the tool result, loop until reply or
gate. Two trigger modes — event-driven (chat-gateway / message
arrived) and scheduled (cron capabilities like daily digest).

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Embedded agents",
  §"Agent turn lifecycle", §"Conversation compaction",
  §"Agent audit trail", §"The agent-first invariant",
  §"Agent authority boundary".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`agent_token` (the
  delegated token), §`audit_log`, §`chat_message`.
- Auth: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Agent tokens".
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/components/AgentSidebar.tsx` (turn
  indicator, message thread), `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- New package `app/domain/agent/` with `runtime.py` exposing
  `run_turn(ctx, scope, thread_id, user_message, *,
  trigger: 'event'|'schedule') -> TurnOutcome`.
- Per turn: emit `agent.turn.started` SSE → resolve capability
  via `p12.model-router` → assemble context (system prompt from
  `llm_prompt_template`, compacted history, knowledge tool
  results) → loop `llm.complete + tool_dispatch` until reply,
  pending action, error, or server cap → emit
  `agent.turn.finished` (`replied | action | error | timeout`).
- Tool dispatch invokes the OpenAPI surface (`app/api/v1/...`) with
  the delegated token. Routes carrying `x-agent-confirm` or matching
  the workspace always-gated list raise the approval flow
  (`p12.approval-flow`) instead of executing.
- Every executed tool call writes an `audit_log` row attributed to
  the delegating user (`actor_kind=user`, populated `token_id`,
  `agent_label`, `agent_conversation_ref`, `reason`,
  `correlation_id`). Reads do **not** audit.
- Compaction is a system operation, not a turn — runs on a separate
  worker, replaces the single live summary row, never emits turn
  events.
- Cap on per-turn LLM calls (default: 8 tool-loop iterations) and a
  wall-clock timeout (default 60 s); cap exhaustion emits
  `agent.turn.finished` with `outcome=timeout` and a friendly
  "the request was too large" reply.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A turn that needs no tools emits started + finished
  (`replied`) and one `chat_message` row.
- [ ] A turn that calls one read tool emits started + finished
  (`replied`); zero audit rows for the read; the turn is one
  `llm_call` minimum.
- [ ] A turn that calls a write tool that is **not** gated emits
  one `audit_log` row attributed to the delegating user, with
  `token_id` and `agent_label` populated.
- [ ] A turn that calls a gated write tool emits started + finished
  (`action`), creates an `agent_action` row, and pauses.
- [ ] A scheduled trigger (no chat thread) records the turn and
  audits per above.
- [ ] Tool-loop cap and wall-clock timeout both emit
  `outcome=timeout`.
- [ ] Reads against rows the delegating user can't see return
  empty / 403 — the runtime does not bypass tenant/permission
  filters.
- [ ] `X-Agent-Channel`, `X-Agent-Conversation-Ref`,
  `X-Agent-Reason` headers propagated to every dispatched tool
  call.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/agent/test_runtime.py` cover
  every outcome + every audit-field shape.
- Integration test: a manager agent turn that creates a task →
  audit_log shows actor_id = manager user, token_id = delegated
  token id, agent_label = 'manager-chat-agent'.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(web/manager): workspace document library with extraction badge + retry
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager browses every document attached anywhere in the workspace (manuals, warranties, leases, regulations), with the §21 extraction status badge per row and a retry control for owners / managers.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DocumentsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/documents` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, ExtractionBadge, ExtractionDisclosure.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/documents, GET /api/v1/assets, GET /api/v1/properties, POST /api/v1/documents/{id}/extraction/retry, GET /api/v1/documents/{id}/extraction/pages/{n}.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DocumentsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The Extraction badge vocabulary is fixed (`pending`, `extracting`, `succeeded`, `failed`, `unsupported`, `empty`) — match §14 / spec exactly.
- Page text is lazy-loaded one page at a time; never prefetch the whole document.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/documents`.

title: test(load): locust scenarios — 100 workers clocking in + 10k occurrences listing
description:
## Problem / goal
Load tests catch performance regressions before customers do. The
§17 budget is "100 workers clocking in at 08:00" and "Task list
render for a property with 100k tasks history". This task lands a
Locust harness with both scenarios plus the budgets from §00.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Load" (Locust
  scenarios, pass criteria from §00),
  §"Quality gates" → release gates ("Full Locust load" before
  release).

## Implementation notes
- New `tests/load/` with `locustfile.py`:
  - `ClockingInUser`: simulates 100 workers calling `POST
    /shifts/clock-in` over a 60 s window (Poisson distribution
    around 08:00); steady-state 1 RPS thereafter for 5 min.
  - `OccurrencesListUser`: 10 concurrent users paginating through
    a 10k-task history with keyset pagination; asserts p95 latency
    against the §00 budget.
  - `TurnoverDayUser`: 5 simultaneous task completions with photo
    uploads (5 MiB JPEGs) — the upload-throughput stress.
- Seed harness: `scripts/seed_load.py` writes the 10k-task history
  for the listing scenario. Idempotent, deterministic.
- Targets: a locally-run dev container by default; CI nightly
  runs against an ephemeral compose stack.
- Pass criteria asserted in `--exit-code-on-error 1` mode so a
  budget breach fails CI.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All three scenarios run locally against
      `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml`
- [ ] Clocking-in scenario: 100 users, p95 < 500 ms over the
      60 s burst window
- [ ] Occurrences-list scenario: p95 < 250 ms on 10k history
- [ ] Turnover-day scenario completes 5 photo uploads each within
      the §00 upload latency budget
- [ ] Nightly CI job runs the full set with seeded data; failure
      blocks the next release

## Test plan
```bash
uv run python scripts/seed_load.py
locust -f tests/load/locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 \
  --run-time 5m --host http://127.0.0.1:8100
```

title: feat(capabilities): env probes + deployment_setting + frozen registry
description:
## Problem / goal
Features consult a **capability registry** — they never sniff the DB
URL, check env vars, or branch on "SaaS vs self-host". Registry
combines immutable env probes (RLS, FTS, storage, LLM caps) with
mutable operator settings from \`deployment_setting\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Capability registry".
- **Settings** (initial): \`settings.signup_enabled\` (default true),
  \`settings.signup_throttle_overrides\`,
  \`settings.require_passkey_attestation\`,
  \`settings.llm_default_budget_cents_30d\`.
- **Probes** (initial): \`features.rls\`, \`features.fulltext_search\`,
  \`features.concurrent_writers\`, \`features.object_storage\`,
  \`features.wildcard_subdomains\`,
  \`features.email_bounce_webhooks\`, \`features.llm_voice_input\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/capabilities/models.py\`: \`DeploymentSetting\`
  table — key (PK string), value (JSON), updated_at, updated_by.
  **Not** workspace-scoped — deployment-wide.
- Alembic migration \`0002_deployment_setting\`.
- \`app/capabilities.py\`: frozen dataclass \`Capabilities\` populated
  once at boot by \`probe(settings, db)\`. Exposes \`features\` and
  \`settings\` namespaces; \`refresh_settings()\` re-reads the DB for
  the mutable subset (called by admin settings endpoints).
- Boot log: print the capability snapshot at INFO level (redaction
  filter keeps it safe).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`capabilities.features.rls\` is True on Postgres, False on SQLite
- [ ] \`capabilities.settings.signup_enabled\` reflects
      \`deployment_setting\` rows
- [ ] \`refresh_settings()\` picks up a runtime change
- [ ] Boot log shows the snapshot once
- [ ] Unit tests cover every probe + refresh

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_capabilities.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(cli/output): json | yaml | table | ndjson formatters
description:
## Problem / goal
`crewday --output <fmt>` (default `table`) routes every command's
response through a formatter. Tables render to a TTY-aware width;
NDJSON streams cleanly through `jq`; YAML and JSON for scripting.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Output formats", §"Tables",
  §"NDJSON streaming for paginated lists".

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_output.py`:
  - `format_response(value, mode, schema_hint=None) -> str`.
  - `json` — `json.dumps(value, indent=2, sort_keys=False, default=…)`.
  - `yaml` — `yaml.safe_dump(value, sort_keys=False)`.
  - `table` — pick visible columns from `schema_hint` (the response
    schema's `x-cli-columns` extension) or fall back to top-level
    scalar keys; render with `rich.table.Table` width-aware.
  - `ndjson` — one object per line, no indent (used by `--all` +
    streamed iterators).
  - Colour off by default when stdout is not a TTY (`NO_COLOR` env
    respected).
  - Error formatter for `ApiError` — table for humans, JSON for
    scripts.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each mode round-trips a sample payload deterministically
- [ ] Table mode honours `x-cli-columns` schema extension
- [ ] NDJSON mode is line-buffered + safe for `| jq`
- [ ] Colour disabled when stdout is not a TTY or `NO_COLOR` set
- [ ] Unit tests cover all four modes + error formatting

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_output.py -xvs
crewday tasks list --output json | jq .
crewday tasks list --output ndjson | head -5
```

title: feat(billing): vendor_invoice record + PDF upload + approve/pay state
description:
## Problem / goal
Record an invoice received from a vendor org, attach the PDF, and
push it through receive → approved → paid (or disputed). Backs
the manager's accounts-payable surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\`
  §"\`vendor_invoice\`", §"Invariants",
  §"Approval flow", §"Proof of payment (client upload)",
  §"Payment-due reminders".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/vendor_invoices.py\`:
  \`VendorInvoiceService(ctx)\` with
  \`create / attach_pdf / approve / mark_paid / dispute / list\`.
- PDF upload via the storage layer (Phase 0); records
  \`pdf_blob_hash\` only.
- \`approve\` requires capability \`billing.invoice.approve\`;
  \`mark_paid\` requires \`billing.invoice.pay\` and a \`paid_at\`,
  payment method, optional proof-of-payment blob.
- State machine guarded; bad transitions → 422.
- Audit each transition; publish \`vendor_invoice.paid\` so a future
  reconciliation worker can match it.
- Compute \`due_at\` reminder windows for the digest worker (date
  math only, sending lives elsewhere).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cannot approve an invoice without a PDF attached
- [ ] Cannot mark paid without method + paid_at
- [ ] Unique constraint (\`vendor_org_id\`, \`invoice_number\`) — dup
      detection surfaces a clear error
- [ ] State transitions audited; capability checks tested
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_vendor_invoices.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_vendor_invoices_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/employee): SchedulePage — desktop bidirectional infinite agenda + phone day view
description:
## Problem / goal
The worker's planning surface (§14 worker schedule). Desktop renders a bidirectional infinite agenda (scroll past + future, both directions paginated); phone collapses to a single-day view with prev/next. Includes leave + override dialogs.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/SchedulePage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/SchedulePage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/SchedulePage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `DeskPage`, `Loading`, `LeaveDialog`, `OverrideDialog` (`@/components/ScheduleDialogs`), `useRole` (`@/context/RoleContext`). Uses `useInfiniteQuery` for the bidirectional agenda.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me/schedule?from=...&to=...` (paginated forward + backward), `POST /api/v1/leaves`, `POST /api/v1/occurrences/{id}/override`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/SchedulePage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/SchedulePage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- The mock is ~2,200 lines — **split it** during promotion into a
  digestible tree under `app/web/src/pages/employee/schedule/`.
  Target: the main `SchedulePage.tsx` under ~400 lines (route
  mount + top-level layout + query orchestration), with the rest
  extracted into siblings. Suggested split:
  - `lib/dateHelpers.ts` — `startOfIsoWeek`, `addDays`, `isoDate`,
    `parseIsoDate`, `sameDate`, `isoWeekday`, `dayLabel`, `timeOfTask`.
  - `lib/bookingHelpers.ts` — `bookingMinutes`, `fmtDuration`,
    `fmtHM`, `bookingNeedsAttention`, `BOOKING_STATUS_LABEL`,
    `hhmmToMin`, `isoToMinOfDay`, `computeWindow`,
    `TASK_CHIP_MIN_PX` / `TASK_CHIP_GAP_PX`.
  - `lib/palette.ts` — `PALETTE`, `PALETTE_SOLID`, `propertyColor`,
    `propertySolid`, `propertyName`, `WEEKDAYS`.
  - `lib/availability.ts` — `availability`, `hoursLabel`.
  - `lib/useIsPhone.ts` — `useIsPhone` breakpoint hook.
  - `lib/buildCells.ts` — `buildCells`, `mergeSchedulePages`.
  - `DesktopAgenda.tsx` — bidirectional infinite agenda view (incl.
    IntersectionObserver pagination from commit `262b30a`).
  - `PhoneDay.tsx` — single-day mobile view.
  - `DayColumn.tsx` / `BookingFolder.tsx` / `TaskChip.tsx` —
    reusable row pieces.
  - `SchedulePage.tsx` — orchestrator: picks Phone vs Desktop via
    `useIsPhone`, wires `useInfiniteQuery`, hosts the `LeaveDialog`
    / `OverrideDialog` mounts from `@/components/ScheduleDialogs`.
- Refactoring is **only allowed** if the rendered DOM + class names
  are unchanged — visual-diff gate is the hard constraint.
- Preserve the IntersectionObserver-based bidirectional pagination (recent commit `262b30a`).
- Phone vs desktop is decided by a CSS breakpoint — preserve it.
- Preserve the IntersectionObserver-based bidirectional pagination (recent commit `262b30a`).
- Phone vs desktop is decided by a CSS breakpoint — preserve it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/SchedulePage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~400 lines.
- [ ] Every helper listed above has a unit test covering its current behaviour.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/SchedulePage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/SchedulePage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/SchedulePage.tsx.spec.ts
```

Create the empty \`app/\` package structure that every downstream task
will fill in. Layout must match \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Repo
layout" exactly so the import-linter gate (separate task) can enforce
boundaries from day 1.

  §"Repo layout".

  inventory, assets, time, payroll, expenses, billing, messaging, llm.

done
uv run mypy app
\`\`\`

title: feat(demo): CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE env flag, capability + UI banner
description:
## Problem / goal
Demo mode is a per-deployment kill-switch (`CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1`)
turning a regular crewday image into the demo server at
`demo.crew.day`. Every other Phase 18 task hangs off this flag.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/24-demo-mode.md` §"Deployment shape" and
  §"Visitor lifecycle" — the env var + bootstrap guard that refuses
  to enable demo mode against a non-demo `CREWDAY_PUBLIC_URL` /
  prod `CREWDAY_DATABASE_URL`.
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe C —
  Demo deployment" for the compose snippet and DB-denylist guard.
- **Capability registry**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md`
  §"Capability registry" — `runtime.demo_mode` is the new probe.

## Implementation notes
- Read `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE` in the existing pydantic-settings
  config; surface as `settings.demo_mode: bool`.
- Bootstrap guard runs in `app/bootstrap.py` after config load:
  refuse start if `demo_mode and not _is_allowed_demo_url(public_url)`
  or if `database_url in CREWDAY_DEMO_DB_DENYLIST`. Exit non-zero
  with the spec-quoted error message.
- Register `runtime.demo_mode` capability so downstream code can
  probe via `caps.has("runtime.demo_mode")` instead of reading env.
- Inject a single banner component slot into every server-rendered
  shell ("Demo data — resets on inactivity") gated by the
  capability. SPA reads the value from `/api/v1/runtime/info`.
- `demo_workspace` table migration added (see §24 entity table).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1` + non-`.crew.day` `CREWDAY_PUBLIC_URL`
      refuses to boot with a clear error
- [ ] `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1` + a URL on the `CREWDAY_DEMO_DB_DENYLIST`
      refuses to boot
- [ ] Capability registry exposes `runtime.demo_mode`
- [ ] Banner renders on every shell when demo mode is on; absent
      otherwise
- [ ] `demo_workspace` migration applies cleanly on SQLite + PG
- [ ] Unit tests cover guard truth-table; integration test boots the
      app under demo env and asserts banner + capability

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/bootstrap/test_demo_guard.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/demo/test_demo_flag_boot.py -xvs
```

title: feat(instructions): instruction + revision CRUD with auto version bump
description:
## Problem / goal
Owners and managers author instructions (SOPs, house rules, safety
notes). Every body change must mint a new immutable
`instruction_revision` and re-point `current_revision_id`; readers
always see the latest revision.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md` §"Data model",
  §"Editing semantics"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"instruction", §"instruction_revision";
  `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` action `instructions.edit`.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionsPage.tsx`
  (list + filter), `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx`
  (editor + revision history teaser).
- DB models from `p1.instructions`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/instructions/service.py` with
  `create`, `update_metadata` (title/tags/scope without minting a
  revision), `update_body` (mints a new revision, monotonic
  version), `archive`, `restore_to_revision` (ships in
  `p6.version.history` — leave the seam).
- Body change detection: hash the markdown post-normalisation; only
  bump version if the hash changes (matches mock UX where save is
  idempotent).
- Scope validation per spec constraint table: `global` ⇒ no
  property/area; `property` ⇒ property set, area null; `area` ⇒
  both set and property mirrors area's parent.
- Tag list: trim, dedupe, lowercase; cap at 20 tags per instruction.
- Audit on every mutation including the revision id minted.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/instructions` creates instruction +
      first revision atomically; `version=1`.
- [ ] `PATCH /workspaces/{ws}/instructions/{id}` with body change
      mints a new revision and increments version monotonically.
- [ ] Metadata-only edit does NOT bump the version.
- [ ] Scope constraint violations → 422 (field-specific).
- [ ] Archived instruction can be re-listed with `?include=archived`
      but cannot be edited until restored.
- [ ] Audit rows include `revision_id` for body edits.
- [ ] Unit tests for body-change detection, scope validation, version
      monotonicity, archive idempotency.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_service_instructions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_instructions.py -xvs
```

description:
## Problem / goal
Messaging + notifications tables — web push, email digests, and chat
channels/messages (staff chat + chat-gateway inbound).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"notification",
  §"push_token", §"digest_record", §"chat_channel",
  §"chat_message", \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\`,
  \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/messaging/models.py\`:
  - \`Notification\`: id, workspace_id, recipient_user_id, kind
    (\`task_assigned|task_overdue|expense_approved|approval_needed|…\`),
    subject, body_md, read_at, created_at, payload_json.
  - \`PushToken\`: id, workspace_id, user_id, endpoint, p256dh, auth,
    user_agent, created_at, last_used_at.
  - \`DigestRecord\`: id, workspace_id, recipient_user_id, period_start,
    period_end, kind (\`daily|weekly\`), body_md, sent_at.
  - \`ChatChannel\`: id, workspace_id, kind (\`staff|manager|
    chat_gateway\`), source (\`app|whatsapp|sms|email\`),
    external_ref (nullable), title, created_at.
  - \`ChatMessage\`: id, workspace_id, channel_id, author_user_id
    (nullable for inbound from gateway), author_label, body_md,
    attachments_json, dispatched_to_agent_at (nullable), created_at.
- Alembic migration \`0016_messaging\`.
- Register in tenancy registry.

title: feat(web/styles): copy tokens.css + reset.css + globals.css verbatim from mocks
description:
## Problem / goal
The visual identity (colours, spacing, shadows, typography, semantic
class roots) lives in three CSS files in the mocks. Copy them
**byte-for-byte** so the production app inherits the design system
and the look is identical from day one.

## Technical context

  §"Semantic CSS classes only".
- **Reference (COPY VERBATIM)**:
  - `mocks/web/src/styles/tokens.css`
  - `mocks/web/src/styles/reset.css`
  - `mocks/web/src/styles/globals.css`

## Implementation notes
- Copy the three files into `app/web/src/styles/` unchanged.
- Import order in `app/web/src/main.tsx` (next task will use this):
  `import './styles/reset.css'`,
  `import './styles/tokens.css'`,
  `import './styles/globals.css'`.
- Do NOT introduce Tailwind / utility classes / inline styles. If a
  visual difference appears against the mocks, the bug is on the
  consuming component, not in these files.
- Keep this task scoped to *just the CSS copy*. No component edits.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `diff -q app/web/src/styles/tokens.css   mocks/web/src/styles/tokens.css` is silent
- [ ] `diff -q app/web/src/styles/reset.css    mocks/web/src/styles/reset.css` is silent

title: feat(web/manager): employees list with today's bookings + invite CTA
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager sees the full roster, who is booked today and where, and invites a new employee via magic-link onboarding.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeesPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/users` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Avatar, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/employees, GET /api/v1/properties, GET /api/v1/bookings, POST /api/v1/employees (invite).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The invite CTA opens the shared invite modal (Phase 16 shared-components); do not inline a new form.
- The `bookings` query is per-day (today only) — pass the same query params the mock implies.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/users`.

title: feat(api/static): mount app/web/dist as SPA catch-all; dev proxies Vite HMR
description:
## Problem / goal
Switch the FastAPI app's static-asset mount from `mocks/web/dist/` (the
Phase 0 placeholder) to `app/web/dist/`, the production build. In
the dev profile, proxy `/` to the Vite dev server so HMR keeps
working. This is the cut-over moment from mocks to real frontend.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Serving the SPA",
  `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"FastAPI static mount".
- **Refs**: Phase 0 `app/main.py` currently mounts
  `mocks/web/dist/` — this task replaces that wiring.

## Implementation notes
- Edit `app/main.py`:
  - In **prod profile**: mount `app/web/dist/` as a `StaticFiles`
    instance with a SPA catch-all route that returns `index.html`
    for any unmatched non-API GET (so client-side routing works).
  - In **dev profile** (`CREWDAY_PROFILE=dev`): instead of the
    static mount, proxy `/` (and `/@vite/`, `/src/`, `/node_modules/`)
    to the Vite dev server on `127.0.0.1:5173` (or the port set in
    `vite.config.ts`); preserve websockets for HMR.
  - Remove the `mocks/web/dist` import / mount.
- Update `mocks/docker-compose.yml` so the dev container mounts the
  app/web build output (or runs Vite dev) — keep loopback-bound,
  never the public IP.
- API routes (`/api/*`, `/w/<slug>/api/*`, `/events`, `/auth/*`)
  must take precedence over the SPA catch-all.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Prod profile serves `index.html` for unknown GETs (SPA)
- [ ] API routes still answer correctly (not shadowed by SPA fallback)
- [ ] Dev profile proxies HMR; editing a `.tsx` reloads in browser
- [ ] `mocks/web/dist` no longer referenced by `app/main.py`
- [ ] Bind-guard still passes (no public-interface bind)
- [ ] Smoke test: `curl http://127.0.0.1:8100/anything` returns SPA HTML

## Test plan
```bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
curl -sI http://127.0.0.1:8100/foo/bar | head
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/ | grep -i 'crew'
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_static_mount.py -xvs
```

title: feat(instructions): scope resolver returning merged applicable instruction set
description:
## Problem / goal
The worker app and agent both ask "which instructions apply to this
context?" Inputs are some combination of (template, property, area,
asset, stay, work_role). The resolver returns the deduplicated,
ordered list with provenance, applying the inheritance order spelled
out in spec §07.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md` §"Resolution: which
  instructions apply to a given task?"; ordering: more specific
  first (area > property > global), then explicit links
  (template/schedule/work_role/task) appended with provenance
  badges; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"instruction_link" for
  the polymorphic link table.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx`
  shows what links exist; the worker-side render lives in
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/`.

## Implementation notes
- Pure function `resolve_instructions(ctx, *, property_id=None,
  area_id=None, template_id=None, schedule_id=None, task_id=None,
  asset_id=None, stay_id=None, work_role_id=None) → List[ResolvedInstruction]`.
- Each `ResolvedInstruction` carries the instruction id, current
  revision id, body_md, and `provenance` (`scope:area` |
  `scope:property` | `scope:global` | `link:task_template` | etc.).
- Walk in spec order; stop adding once an instruction id is
  already present (highest-specificity provenance wins).
- Pull only `status=active` instructions.
- Unit-test the matrix: each provenance, dedup behaviour, empty
  context (returns globals only), area implies property.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Returns globals when no property given.
- [ ] Returns globals + property when property given, no area.
- [ ] Returns globals + property + area when both given, with area
      first in order.
- [ ] Explicit links (template/schedule/work_role/task) appended,
      each with the right `provenance` badge.
- [ ] Same instruction reachable through two routes appears once,
      labelled with the most-specific provenance.
- [ ] Archived instructions excluded.
- [ ] Unit tests for every quadrant of the resolution matrix.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_instructions_resolver.py -xvs
```

title: feat(integrations/webhooks): outbound HMAC-signed delivery + retries
description:
## Problem / goal
External integrations (and the per-spec `approval.pending`,
`approval.decided` notifications) need an outbound webhook
channel: subscription CRUD, HMAC-SHA256 signed payloads, retry
with exponential backoff, dead-letter on permanent failure.
Used by the agent runtime to notify external systems and by the
approval flow to push pending decisions.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent action
  approval" (mentions `approval.pending`, `approval.decided`
  webhooks); cross-ref §10 "Webhooks".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` —
  `webhook_subscription`, `webhook_delivery` (DLQ row when
  retries exhausted).
- Auth scope: `webhook.manage` on the workspace agent token.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/integrations/webhooks.py` with
  `sign(payload_bytes, secret) -> sig`,
  `enqueue(workspace_id, event, payload) -> delivery_id`, and
  the worker `deliver(delivery_id)`.
- Signature format: `X-Crewday-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex>`,
  HMAC-SHA256 over `f"{t}.{body}"` with the subscription
  secret. (Stripe-style; documented in `docs/specs/12`).
- Retry schedule: 0 s, 30 s, 5 m, 1 h, 6 h, 24 h (six attempts
  total). Permanent failures (HTTP 4xx other than 408/429) skip
  retries and dead-letter immediately.
- Dead-letter rows visible in `/admin/webhooks` (managed
  elsewhere). Manual replay via `POST
  /admin/api/v1/webhooks/deliveries/{id}/replay`.
- CRUD endpoints for subscriptions: `POST/GET/PUT/DELETE
  /api/v1/webhook_subscriptions`. Secrets are returned only at
  create time (subsequent reads return `secret_last_4`).
- Subscription mutations write `audit.webhook_subscription.{
  created,updated,deleted}`. Successful deliveries do not audit
  (operational telemetry); permanent failures audit
  `audit.webhook_delivery.dead_lettered`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sign / verify round-trips with the documented header
  format.
- [ ] A 500 from the receiver triggers the retry schedule;
  delivery succeeds on the third attempt.
- [ ] Six failures dead-letter the row + audit.
- [ ] A 400 dead-letters immediately (no retries).
- [ ] Replay re-attempts the same payload with a new signature
  timestamp.
- [ ] Subscription secret is returned only at create.
- [ ] Workspace at-cap does not block webhook delivery (envelope
  is LLM-only).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/integrations/test_webhooks.py`
  with a stub HTTP server.
- Integration test: agent triggers `approval.pending` → mock
  receiver gets a signed POST.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(web/lib): api.ts + queryClient.ts + queryKeys.ts wired to /w/<slug>/api/v1/*
description:
## Problem / goal
The web app's data layer: a typed `api.ts` fetch wrapper, a TanStack
Query v5 client with sensible defaults, and a single `queryKeys.ts`
factory so SSE invalidation has one source of truth.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Data layer",
  §"TanStack Query", §"Optimistic mutations",
  §"SSE-driven invalidation".
- **Reference (COPY THEN ADAPT)**:
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts`
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/queryClient.ts`
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/queryKeys.ts`
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/{dates,money,preferences,strings,tones,
        routeParents,agentTyping,useBannerHeightVar,
        useDecideMutation}.ts` (also copy verbatim — used by
        components).
  - **Not in scope here**: `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts` — it is
    copied and extended by `cd-y4g5` (SSE wiring). Don't also
    copy it from this task; defer to `cd-y4g5` so the invalidation
    map and reconnect logic land together.

## Implementation notes
- Copy each `mocks/web/src/lib/*.ts` into `app/web/src/lib/`
  **except `sse.ts`** (handled by `cd-y4g5`).
- **Adaptations**:
  - `api.ts` — base URL `/w/<slug>/api/v1`; slug resolved from
    `WorkspaceContext`; attach `Authorization: Bearer <token>` from
    the auth store; throw a typed `ApiError` on non-2xx mirroring
    the CLI's shape.
  - `queryClient.ts` — TanStack Query v5 with `staleTime: 30s`,
    `retry: 2 (exp backoff)`, `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` (SSE
    invalidates instead).
  - `queryKeys.ts` — keyed factory `tasks.list(filters)`,
    `tasks.detail(id)`, `properties.list()`, ... — every key
    starts with the workspace slug so workspace switching evicts
    cleanly.
  - Mutations support optimistic updates via the `onMutate` /
    `onError rollback` pattern; helper `useDecideMutation` (copied
    verbatim) wraps approval-style decisions.
- No mock-data imports remain.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All `lib/*.ts` files present in `app/web/src/lib/`
- [ ] `api.ts` builds the URL from `useWorkspace().slug`
- [ ] `queryKeys` always namespace by slug
- [ ] Optimistic mutation pattern works end-to-end against dev API
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes; ESLint clean
- [ ] Unit tests cover key factory + error mapping

```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/lib
cd app/web && npm run build
```

title: chore(boundaries): add per-context sibling-boundary import-linter contract
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Boundary rules" rule 1 says
each `domain.<X>` may only reach sibling contexts through the
sibling's `__init__.py` public surface — never by importing
`domain.<Y>.<submodule>`. cd-ev0 landed the three universal contracts
(domain→adapters, domain→handlers, adapters→handlers) but deferred
this per-context sibling contract because every `app/domain/<ctx>/__init__.py`
is still a docstring-only stub with no submodules to forbid.

Once domain contexts grow submodules (models.py, services.py, policy.py, etc.),
add the per-context sibling contract. The simplest shape is one
`forbidden` contract per context pair:

```toml
[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]
name = "identity forbids siblings' submodules"
type = "forbidden"
source_modules = ["app.domain.identity"]
forbidden_modules = [
  "app.domain.places.models", "app.domain.places.services", ...
  "app.domain.tasks.models", ...
]
```

A cleaner alternative is import-linter's `independence` contract, which
handles the N×(N-1) expansion automatically:

```toml
[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]
name = "Domain contexts are independent (siblings via __init__ only)"
type = "independence"
modules = ["app.domain.identity", "app.domain.places", "app.domain.tasks", ...]
ignore_imports = ["app.domain.* -> app.domain.*"]  # allow the public __init__ edge
```

Pick based on what actually reads cleanest once a handful of domain
contexts have real submodules.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Boundary rules" rule 1,
  `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Independence contract".
- Depends on domain contexts growing submodules
  (cd-chd, cd-w92, cd-i6u, cd-ijj, etc. have already added
  adapter-side tables; the domain-side service/policy/model split
  is still pending).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Per-context sibling contract present in `pyproject.toml`
- [ ] `uv run lint-imports` passes on the clean tree
- [ ] A deliberate `app.domain.tasks._bad -> app.domain.places.models`
      import fails `lint-imports`
- [ ] Existing tests in `tests/boundary/` still pass

## Test plan
```bash
uv run lint-imports
uv run pytest tests/boundary/ -xvs
```

title: feat(tenancy): WorkspaceContext + slug resolver + scoped registry
description:
## Problem / goal
Every mutation flows through a \`WorkspaceContext\` carrying

\`actor_was_owner_member\`, and \`audit_correlation_id\`. Ships the

  WorkspaceContext\` matching the Protocol in §01.
- \`app/tenancy/slug.py\`: slug regex

  listed in §01.

title: feat(cli/overrides): admin init, auth passkey register, whoami, tasks close
description:
## Problem / goal
Some flows are multi-step (workspace bootstrap, passkey registration)
or want a friendlier shape than the raw OpenAPI verb (`whoami`,
`tasks close`). These ship as hand-written commands that override or
augment the codegen output.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Composite commands",
  §"Overrides directory".
- **Auth**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` (passkey ceremony +
  attestation).

## Implementation notes
- New directory `cli/crewday/_overrides/` — every `.py` file exports
  a `register(root: click.Group)` function called from `_main.py`
  after the generated commands attach. Overrides may **shadow**
  generated commands (same name wins).
- Implementations:
  - `crewday admin init --workspace <slug> --owner-email <email>` —
    POST workspace, seed `owners` group, register the calling user
    as owner, mint and print an API token.
  - `crewday auth passkey register [--label <name>]` — guides the
    user through the WebAuthn registration ceremony via the API
    helper endpoints; prints credential id + label on success.
  - `crewday whoami` — calls `GET /me` and prints user, active
    workspace, role, token expiry, profile name in human form.
  - `crewday tasks close <id> [--note <md>] [--evidence <path>...]` —
    fetch occurrence, upload evidence blobs, transition to `done`,
    print summary.
- Each override has a `--help` block describing the multi-step
  flow and the underlying API calls.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All four composites listed in `crewday --help`
- [ ] `admin init` is idempotent (re-runs return the same workspace)
- [ ] `auth passkey register` works against the dev API end-to-end
- [ ] `whoami` prints workspace + role + token expiry
- [ ] `tasks close` uploads evidence + transitions state in one call
- [ ] Unit tests stub the HTTP client; integration tests hit dev

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_overrides.py -xvs
crewday admin init --workspace test --owner-email me@example.com
crewday whoami
```

title: feat(chat-gateway): inbound webhook normalising provider payloads → ChatMessage
description:
## Problem / goal
A single \`POST /webhooks/chat\` endpoint terminates inbound traffic
from WhatsApp, SMS, and email-to-chat providers, verifies the
provider signature, normalises the payload to a \`ChatMessage\` row
in a \`channel.kind='chat_gateway'\` channel, and acks fast.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"Channel catalog",
  §"Bindings" → §"\`chat_channel_binding\`",
  §"Routing" → §"Inbound", §"Provider abstraction",
  §"Secret envelopes", §"Security".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New router \`app/api/chat_gateway/webhooks.py\` mounted under
  \`/webhooks/chat/{provider}\` (one URL per provider — Twilio,
  Meta-WA, Postmark inbound, …). Provider name resolves the
  signature verifier + payload adapter.
- Adapters in \`app/adapters/chat_gateway/<provider>.py\` implement
  \`verify(headers, raw_body, secret) -> bool\` and
  \`normalise(payload) -> NormalisedInboundMessage\`.
- HMAC verification is mandatory; failure returns 401, no body
  written.
- Look up or auto-create the gateway channel (per spec link
  ceremony) using the binding (\`provider\`, \`external_contact\`).
- Persist a \`chat_message\` row (\`author_user_id=NULL\`,
  \`author_label=<phone or email>\`, \`source=<provider>\`).
- Publish \`chat.message.received\` (separate from
  \`chat.message.sent\` — inbound gets its own bus event for the
  gateway dispatcher).
- Audit \`chat_gateway.message.received\` with provider + binding.
- Reply with \`200\` ASAP; downstream agent dispatch is async (see
  \`p13.gateway.dispatcher\`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Unsigned / wrong-signature requests rejected, no row written
- [ ] Idempotent on the provider's message id (replay-safe)
- [ ] Auto-creates a \`chat_gateway\` channel + binding on first
      contact
- [ ] Returns < 1 s under normal payloads (no agent dispatch
      inline)
- [ ] Unit tests per provider adapter; integration test with at
      least one real-shape Twilio payload

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_webhook.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_adapters.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/chat_gateway/test_webhook_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/types): split + copy mocks/web/src/types/api.ts into domain modules
description:
## Problem / goal
The shared TypeScript types used by every web surface live in
`mocks/web/src/types/api.ts` — a single 1,548-line file covering
properties, bookings, tasks, payroll, expenses, assets, approvals,
agents, LLM wiring, admin surfaces, and more. 74 files import from
`@/types/api`. Without it, nothing that the other web tasks
"copy verbatim" will compile.

Promote these types into `app/web/src/types/` — behaviour-preserving —
**split by domain** into smaller, reviewable modules so the next
editor doesn't have to scroll through a 1.5k-line file to find
`Booking` vs. `ApprovalRequest`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Data layer" (types
  mirror the FastAPI dataclasses; dates are ISO-8601 strings, enums
  are literal string unions).
- **Reference (source of truth for shape)**: `mocks/web/src/types/api.ts`.
- The types mirror the backend dataclasses — once the real API is
  up, cross-check shape parity against the OpenAPI schema.

## Implementation notes
- **Split by domain** into `app/web/src/types/` with one file per
  bounded context, each re-exported from a barrel `index.ts` so
  consumers can keep their existing `from "@/types/api"` imports
  via a shim. Suggested split (final names negotiable but keep
  each file under ~250 lines):
  - `core.ts` — `Role`, `Theme`, `ResolvedTheme`.
  - `property.ts` — `Property`, `PropertyColor`, `PropertyKind`,
    `PropertyWorkspace*`, `PropertyClosure`, `Stay*`.
  - `employee.ts` — `Employee`, `Leave`, `PayRule`, `PayPeriod`,
    `PaySlip`.
  - `task.ts` — `Task*`, `ChecklistItem*`, `TaskTemplate`,
    `Schedule`, `Instruction`, `Issue`, `TaskComment`.
  - `booking.ts` — `Booking*`, `StayLifecycleRule`.
  - `expense.ts` — `Expense*`, `ExchangeRate*`,
    `PendingReimbursement*`, `AutofillField`, `ExpenseScanResult`.
  - `asset.ts` — asset / inventory / documents types
    (`InventoryItem`, `InventoryMovement*`, etc.).
  - `approval.ts` — `ApprovalRequest`, `GateSource`,
    `GateDestination`, `InlineChannel`.
  - `llm.ts` — `ModelAssignment`, `LLMCall`, `LlmProvider*`.
  - `index.ts` — barrel re-exporting every type.
- **Preserve every name and shape verbatim.** No renames, no shape
  tweaks, no optional-vs-required flips. If something looks wrong,
  file a separate bug — do NOT fix it here.
- Keep the spec comments (`// §02 —`, `// §06 —`, etc.) attached to
  the types they annotate; they're load-bearing for future readers.
- Update `tsconfig` path aliases only if needed; default
  `@/types/...` must still resolve.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every named export from `mocks/web/src/types/api.ts` is
      reachable from `@/types/api` in `app/web/`.
- [ ] Each per-domain file is under ~250 lines; `index.ts` barrel
      re-exports everything.
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes with `strict: true` +
      `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`.
- [ ] All 74 `from "@/types/api"` imports in the component / page
      tasks resolve without edits to the consumers.
- [ ] No behaviour drift — a structural-type diff vs mocks is empty.

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npx tsc --noEmit
# Spot-check import resolution for each domain:
grep -rE "from ['\"]@/types/api['\"]" app/web/src | head
```

title: feat(web/employee): IssueNewPage — worker flags a problem (creates task + manager notification)
description:
## Problem / goal
Lets a worker raise an issue from the field — broken thing, missing supplies, safety hazard. Submitting creates a task (or issue record) and notifies the manager. Categories: damage / broken / supplies / safety / other; severities: low / normal / high / urgent.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/IssueNewPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/IssueNewPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/IssueNewPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `AutoGrowTextarea`, `Loading`, `Camera` icon, `useNavigate`, `cap`. Types: `Issue`, `Property`. Local enums: `Category`, `Severity`, with `CATEGORIES` and `SEVERITIES` arrays preserved verbatim.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me/properties` (selector for which property the issue belongs to); `POST /api/v1/issues` (multipart with photo + body).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/IssueNewPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/IssueNewPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- On success, navigate back to the source page (today / task detail) and show a confirmation toast.
- Photo is optional but encouraged — the camera button must use the device camera on phone.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/IssueNewPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/IssueNewPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/IssueNewPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/IssueNewPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: test(integration): testcontainers PG harness + dual-backend CI matrix
description:
## Problem / goal
Every test that touches the DB must run on both SQLite (default
backend) and Postgres (the larger-deployment + RLS backend), so
SQL portability and RLS-defence-in-depth bugs are caught in CI,
not on a customer's instance.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Integration"
  (real SQLite + PG via `testcontainers`, matrix runs both),
  §"Cross-tenant regression test" (runs on both backends).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Row-level security (RLS)" (PG only; SQLite runs the
  app-filter alone).

## Implementation notes
- Add `testcontainers[postgres]` to dev deps.
- Extend `tests/integration/conftest.py` to honour
  `CREWDAY_TEST_DB={sqlite|postgres}` (default `sqlite`):
  - `sqlite`: a per-test temp file (already in p21.pytest-scaffold)
  - `postgres`: a session-scoped `PostgresContainer`
    (`postgres:15-alpine`) with `RLS` policies enabled; per-test
    schema reset via `TRUNCATE … RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE`.
- New CI job `pytest-integration-pg` runs the suite under
  `CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres`. The existing `pytest-integration`
  job (SQLite) is unchanged. Both block PR merge.
- Add a marker `@pytest.mark.pg_only` for tests that exercise RLS
  or PG-specific syntax; SQLite job skips them, PG job runs them.
- Migration runner gets a `--for-tests` flag that creates the
  schema on a fresh DB without reading `alembic_version` history.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/integration`
      passes against a freshly-spun container
- [ ] CI runs both backends; failure on either blocks merge
- [ ] `@pytest.mark.pg_only` selectively runs only on PG
- [ ] Per-test reset is < 50 ms after the first test in a session
- [ ] Migrations apply identically on SQLite and PG (asserted by a
      schema-fingerprint test)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration -xvs
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/integration -xvs
```

title: feat(web/manager): expenses approvals with receipt preview + reimbursement decision
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager triages submitted expenses: previews the receipt, accepts or rejects with a reason, and the accepted ones flow into the next pay run.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/expenses` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/expenses, GET /api/v1/employees, POST /api/v1/expenses/{id}/approve|reject.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The receipt thumbnail uses the storage signed-URL endpoint; do not blob-fetch in the browser.
- Approving an expense should optimistically update both `['expenses']` and `['pay-pending-reimbursement']`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/expenses`.

title: feat(api/identity): users, tokens, passkeys, sessions, role-grants, permission-groups, invites
description:
## Problem / goal
Identity router covering everything the auth + people-management UIs
need: users, passkeys, sessions, API tokens (PATs), role grants,
permission groups, invites, and the click-to-accept flow. Endpoints
defined under spec §12 §"Auth" + §"Users / work roles / settings"
(permission groups + rules subsection).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Auth" (including
  \`/auth/email-change\`, \`/auth/invites/accept\`, PATs, push
  tokens), §"Users / work roles / settings" (permission groups +
  rules), §"Settings" (workspaces).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/UsersPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/RolesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/InvitesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/PermissionGroupsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/SecurityPage.tsx\` (passkeys, PATs,
  sessions, email change),
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/auth/AcceptInvitePage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/identity.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`identity\`.
- Pydantic schemas in \`app/api/v1/schemas/identity.py\` —
  separate \`Read\`, \`Create\`, \`Update\`, \`Patch\` per resource;
  PATs surface only the prefix on listing, full secret only at
  creation.
- All mutations honour \`Idempotency-Key\`; all listings are
  cursor-paginated.
- Email-change endpoint enforces passkey-session-only via the
  Phase 2 auth dependency (PATs/agent tokens denied at the
  router edge).
- Invite acceptance endpoint runs anonymously, validates token
  purpose=='accept', then either kicks off a passkey ceremony
  (new user) or returns the Accept card payload (existing user).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All routes mounted under \`/w/{slug}/api/v1/{resource}\`
      with correct verbs (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE)
- [ ] Per-resource pydantic Read/Create/Update/Patch schemas
- [ ] PAT secret returned only on creation; subsequent reads
      show prefix only
- [ ] Email-change rejects PATs / agent tokens with 403
      \`forbidden\`
- [ ] Invite-accept rejects mismatched purposes with 403
- [ ] OpenAPI tag \`identity\` lists every operation; schemathesis
      passes

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_identity.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_identity_routes.py -xvs
uv run schemathesis run --checks all http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json --tag identity
\`\`\`

title: feat(billing): work_order CRUD + hour accrual from shift.ended event
description:
## Problem / goal
A work order tracks billable hours and price for a job at a property
for a client. Hours accrue automatically when shifts close on a
property linked to an open work order. Publishes
\`work_order.completed\` when manager marks it done.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"\`work_order\`",
  §"State machine", §"Invariants",
  §"\`booking_billing\`" (cross-context accrual).

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/work_orders.py\`:
  \`WorkOrderService(ctx)\` with
  \`create / get / list / update / mark_in_progress / complete /
  invoice\`.
- Subscribe to \`shift.ended\` on the event bus: when the shift's
  property has an open work order for its org, append
  \`(shift_hours_decimal, hourly_rate)\` to \`total_*\` cached
  columns. Use SELECT … FOR UPDATE to keep the cache consistent.
- API \`app/api/billing/work_orders.py\` standard REST + a
  \`POST /work-orders/{id}/complete\` action.
- On \`complete\`: validate state machine, persist, publish
  \`work_order.completed\` with payload
  \`{work_order_id, total_cents, total_hours_decimal}\`.
- Audit every state transition.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Status transitions match the spec state machine; bad
      transitions → 422
- [ ] Cached \`total_hours_decimal\` / \`total_cents\` exact across
      concurrent shift-end events (test with two concurrent
      handlers)
- [ ] \`work_order.completed\` event fires exactly once per WO
- [ ] Cannot edit \`rate_card_id\` once shifts have accrued
- [ ] Unit + API + integration tests for shift-driven accrual

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_work_orders.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_work_orders_api.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/billing/test_work_order_accrual.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/stays): stay_bundle generation driven by property templates
description:
## Problem / goal
Generate \`stay_bundle\` rows (welcome, deep-clean, etc.) at stay
ingestion based on property-level lifecycle rule templates. A bundle
groups N occurrences under one logical "stay-task package" so the UI
can render and edit them coherently.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Stay task
  bundles" — covers trigger types (\`after_checkout\`,
  \`before_checkin\`, \`during_stay\`) and edit semantics (4h shift
  patches in place; bigger shifts cancel + regenerate).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"stay_bundle".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\` (Stay rules
  tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (STAY_BUNDLES).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/stays/bundle_service.py\` —
  \`generate_bundles_for_stay\`, \`reapply_bundles_for_stay\`,
  \`cancel_bundles_for_stay\`, \`list_bundles\`.
- Reuses the same \`reservation.upserted\` subscriber as turnover-gen;
  bundle generation is the broader code path that includes turnover.
- Rule lookup walks property → unit cascade for lifecycle rules.
- Honour \`guest_kind_filter\` on each rule.
- Edit semantics per spec: < 4h shift on \`after_checkout\` /
  \`before_checkin\` patches scheduled times in place; otherwise
  cancel \`scheduled|pending\` tasks with reason
  \`stay rescheduled\` and regenerate.
- Each created occurrence carries \`stay_task_bundle_id\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One bundle per (stay, rule); idempotent on re-fire
- [ ] All three trigger types exercised
- [ ] Edit-in-place < 4h, regenerate >= 4h
- [ ] Cancellation reason recorded on regenerate path
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover all three trigger types + edit semantics

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_bundle_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_bundle_lifecycle.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(api/tasks): templates, schedules, occurrences, completion, evidence, comments, approvals
description:
## Problem / goal
The tasks router — the product's centre of gravity. Covers
templates, schedules (RRULE), generated occurrences, occurrence
completion (with evidence), comments, and the approval-required
flow when a manager review is needed.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Tasks / templates /
  schedules" (full subsection).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulerPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/MyDayPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/TaskDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/tasks.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`tasks\`.
- Sub-resources nested only one level (e.g.
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/complete\`,
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/evidence\`,
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/comments\`,
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/approve\`).
- Pydantic Read/Create/Update/Patch per resource. Occurrences
  expose computed fields (\`overdue\`, \`time_window_local\`) via
  a separate \`OccurrenceRead\` model populated by the service.
- Completion endpoint is idempotent via \`Idempotency-Key\` —
  re-submitting the same payload returns the same occurrence.
- Approval endpoint returns 409 \`approval_required\` with
  \`approval_request_id\` if the caller lacks the cap.
- Listings support \`?state=\`, \`?assignee_user_id=\`,
  \`?property_id=\`, \`?scheduled_for_utc_gte=\` per spec.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Routes for templates, schedules, occurrences, evidence,
      comments, approvals
- [ ] Completion is idempotent; re-submission returns same
      occurrence
- [ ] Schedule POST validates RRULE via the Phase 0 util port
- [ ] Filtering + cursor pagination on every list endpoint
- [ ] OpenAPI tag \`tasks\` matches spec; schemathesis passes
- [ ] Cross-tenant occurrence access returns 404 (not 403, to
      avoid existence leak)

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_tasks.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_tasks_routes.py -xvs
uv run schemathesis run --checks all http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json --tag tasks
\`\`\`

title: feat(i18n): pybabel extraction + per-locale JSON bundles + CI gate
description:
## Problem / goal
Without an extraction pipeline and a CI gate, translation keys
silently rot: a developer adds `t("foo.bar")` and the catalog
never learns about it until a translator complains. This task
wires `pybabel extract → update → compile`, generates per-locale
JSON bundles for the SPA, and fails CI on missing keys.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/18-i18n.md` §"Library choices" (`pybabel`),
  §"Rules followed in v1" (rule 1: tests assert no raw strings).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Quality gates"
  (the new `i18n-keys` gate fits next to `cli-parity`).

## Implementation notes
- Add `babel` config (`babel.cfg`) listing extractor rules for
  Python (`gettext`-style) + Jinja2 (`{% trans %}`) +
  TypeScript/TSX (`t("…")` regex extractor).
- New `scripts/i18n_extract.py` runs `pybabel extract → update →
  compile` for every locale and additionally writes the SPA's JSON
  bundle (`mocks/web/src/i18n/bundles/<locale>.json`) by walking
  the compiled `.mo` files.
- Locales shipped: `en-US` (default, fully populated), `fr` and
  `es` placeholders (en-US strings copied verbatim — translators
  fill them later). `qps-ploc` is generated, not hand-translated.
- CI gate `i18n-keys`: runs the extractor against the working tree,
  diffs against the committed catalog, fails on a non-empty diff
  (same posture as `cli-parity` / `surface-parity`). The fix is to
  run `make i18n-extract` and commit.
- Pre-commit hook surfaces stale catalogs locally.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `make i18n-extract` regenerates `.po`, `.mo`, and JSON
      bundles deterministically
- [ ] CI gate fails on a missing key; PR diff shows what to add
- [ ] `fr` and `es` placeholder bundles are present and load
      without runtime error (UI shows English text under each)
- [ ] `qps-ploc` bundle is generated automatically; `?locale=
      qps-ploc` shows accented + inflated copy
- [ ] Pre-commit hook detects the same staleness condition

## Test plan
```bash
make i18n-extract
git diff --exit-code app/i18n/locales mocks/web/src/i18n/bundles
uv run pytest tests/unit/i18n/test_extraction.py -xvs
```
