title: feat(instructions): version history view + restore-to-revision
description:
## Problem / goal
Authors need to see the change history of an instruction and roll
back to a prior revision. Restore is implemented as "mint a new
revision whose body equals revision N's body" — never mutate or
delete an existing revision (immutable per §07).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md` §"Editing semantics"
  ("every save creates a new revision"), §"Bulk operations"
  (rescope mention); `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"instruction_revision"
  (immutable).
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx`
  shows the revisions side panel.

## Implementation notes
- Service methods on the existing instructions service:
  - `list_revisions(instruction_id)` — paginated, newest first,
    each row carries author, created_at, change_note, version.
  - `restore(instruction_id, revision_id, change_note)` — copies
    body_md + summary_md + attachments from the chosen revision into
    a brand-new revision (via the regular `update_body` path).
- The new revision's `change_note` is auto-prefixed with
  `"Restored from v{n}: "` if no explicit note is given.
- Audit row records `restored_from_revision_id` alongside the new
  revision id.
- Endpoint: `GET /workspaces/{ws}/instructions/{id}/revisions`
  + `POST /workspaces/{ws}/instructions/{id}/restore`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] List endpoint returns revisions newest-first with author +
      change_note.
- [ ] Restore creates a new revision with the prior body, increments
      version, never mutates the source revision.
- [ ] Restoring to the current revision is a 409 (no-op explicitly
      rejected — keeps the ledger clean).
- [ ] Audit row carries `restored_from_revision_id`.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: list pagination, restore happy path, restore
      to current rejected, restore by non-author allowed (only the
      author of the new revision is the caller).

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_instructions_versions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_instructions_revisions.py -xvs
```

title: test(scaffold): unit + integration test dirs, shared fixtures, factory-boy factories per context
description:
## Problem / goal
Phase 0 ships skeleton tests but no organised harness. This task
lays the §17 test pyramid scaffolding: per-context unit dirs that
import only their context's public surface, an integration dir
with real-DB fixtures, and a `factory-boy` factory module per
bounded context so tests build coherent rows in one line.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Unit" (one test
  package per bounded context, fakes for `Clock`/`Storage`/
  `Mailer`/`LLMClient`, parametrised RRULE edge cases,
  `hypothesis` for money math, exhaustive truth tables for
  capability resolution / assignment / approval detection),
  §"Integration" (real SQLite + PG via testcontainers, per-test DB,
  migrations once per worker, snapshot+truncate between tests,
  real filesystem Storage in `tmp_path`, real Jinja renders).
- **Spec**: §"Test data" (`crewday admin demo` deterministic seed
  for e2e).

## Implementation notes
- New layout under `tests/`:
  - `tests/unit/<context>/` — one dir per `app/domain/<context>/`,
    each with its own `conftest.py` providing the in-memory fakes.
  - `tests/integration/` — shared `conftest.py` with the real-DB
    fixture and per-test schema reset.
  - `tests/factories/<context>.py` — `factory-boy` factories for
    every entity, building a coherent graph (workspace + user +
    role grant + property + …).
  - `tests/api/`, `tests/property/`, `tests/llm/` — placeholders
    with one pilot test each.
- Add `factory-boy` and `hypothesis` to dev deps.
- `pytest.ini` sets the `testpaths` and the import-mode to
  `importlib` so per-context dirs don't collide.
- Fakes (`FakeClock`, `FakeStorage`, `FakeMailer`, `FakeLLMClient`)
  live in `tests/_fakes/` and are imported by every context's
  conftest.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/unit/<any_context>/` runs in isolation
      without importing siblings
- [ ] At least one factory + one example test per existing context
- [ ] `FakeClock`, `FakeStorage`, `FakeMailer`, `FakeLLMClient`
      satisfy their port interfaces (mypy passes)
- [ ] Integration fixture spins a per-test DB (SQLite default) in
      under 100 ms after warm-up
- [ ] Coverage helper aggregates results across all dirs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration -xvs
uv run pytest --cov=app --cov-report=term
```

title: feat(api/expenses): expenses, receipts, approvals
description:
## Problem / goal
Expenses router — claims, receipt uploads (with OCR-assisted
field pre-fill), approval workflow, and reimbursement marking.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Time, payroll,
  expenses" (Expenses + Receipts subsections).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/ExpensesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/ExpenseDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ExpensesReviewPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/expenses.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`expenses\`.
- \`POST /expenses\` creates a draft; \`POST /expenses/{id}/submit\`
  transitions to \`submitted\`.
- \`POST /expenses/{id}/receipts\` references a \`/files\`
  blob_hash and triggers OCR (async).
- \`POST /expenses/{id}/decision\` for approve/reject (manager
  cap); audited.
- \`POST /expenses/{id}/reimburse\` marks paid + records the
  channel.
- Money in integer cents; currency required.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD + submit + decision + reimburse routes
- [ ] State machine enforced
  (\`draft -> submitted -> approved/rejected -> reimbursed\`)
- [ ] Cross-user expense access denied (workers see own only)
- [ ] OCR results fetched via \`GET /expenses/{id}/receipts\`
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`expenses\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_expenses.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_expenses_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(payroll/exports): CSV export — timesheets, payslips, expense ledger
description:
## Problem / goal
Accountants want flat CSVs they can drop into their bookkeeping
software. Three exports for a date window: shifts (timesheets),
payslips, expense ledger.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Reports
  and exports".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayPage.tsx` (export
  dropdown).

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/domain/payroll/exports.py`:
  - `export_timesheets_csv(ctx, *, since, until)` — columns:
    `shift_id, user_email, property_label, starts_at_utc, ends_at_utc,
     hours_decimal, source, notes`.
  - `export_payslips_csv(ctx, *, period_id)` — columns:
    `payslip_id, user_email, period_starts_at, period_ends_at,
     hours, overtime_hours, gross_cents, deductions_cents,
     net_cents, currency, paid_at`.
  - `export_expense_ledger_csv(ctx, *, since, until,
    status_filter='approved')` — columns:
    `expense_id, claimant_email, vendor, spent_at, category,
     amount_cents, currency, property_label, decided_at,
     reimbursed_via`.
- All exports stream (`StreamingResponse`) to avoid memory blow-up.
- REST: `GET /api/payroll/exports/{kind}.csv?...`.
- Capability `payroll.export`.
- Audit each export with row count.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Three CSV endpoints stream correctly
- [ ] Header row present; UTF-8 BOM optional via query flag
- [ ] Empty windows return only the header
- [ ] Audit row records `since`, `until`, `kind`, `row_count`

```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_csv_exports.py -xvs
```

title: feat(db/billing): organization + rate_card + work_order + quote + vendor_invoice
description:
## Problem / goal
Clients & vendors — organizations, their rate cards, work orders
(billable hours against a property), quotes, and vendor-issued invoices
received by the workspace.

## Technical context

title: feat(api/inventory): items, movements, reorder rules
description:
## Problem / goal
Inventory router for items (with SKU + barcode lookup), movements
(receive / issue / adjust / consume), and reorder rules. The
movement endpoint is the hot path — workers scan a barcode and
post a one-line movement.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Inventory".
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InventoryPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/InventoryScanPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ReorderRulesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/inventory.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`inventory\`.
- \`GET /items?barcode=...\` returns a single match; 404 if
  unknown.
- \`POST /movements\` accepts \`item_id, delta, reason,
  occurrence_id?\`; idempotent via Idempotency-Key (workers'
  retries are common on flaky wifi).
- Reorder rule CRUD; \`GET /items?below_reorder=true\` lists
  shortage items for the dashboard.
- All numeric quantities serialise as decimal strings to avoid
  float drift.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Item, movement, reorder-rule routes
- [ ] Barcode lookup returns 1 row or 404
- [ ] Movement is idempotent (Idempotency-Key replays)
- [ ] Decimal serialised as string ("3.5"), never float
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`inventory\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_inventory.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_inventory_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/manager): chat channels — manage WhatsApp / Telegram bindings + gateway
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager links / unlinks the workspace's off-app chat channels (WhatsApp Business, Telegram) and inspects the gateway providers' health.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ChatChannelsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/chat/channels` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, LinkChannelDialog.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/chat/channels, GET /api/v1/chat/channels/providers, POST /api/v1/chat/channels/link/start, POST /api/v1/chat/channels/{id}/unlink.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ChatChannelsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- Link flow opens the provider's OAuth or QR pairing in a popup; the mock's `link/start` returns the URL — preserve that handshake.
- The provider status chip uses tone `moss/sand/rust/ghost` per state — copy the `providerTone` helper exactly.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/chat/channels`.

title: feat(adapters): define DbSession / Storage / Mailer / LLMClient / Clock ports
description:
## Problem / goal
Adapter Protocols pin the domain\u2194side-effect seam. Domain code depends
on these Protocols; SQLAlchemy / S3 / SMTP / OpenRouter implementations
live behind them.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters",
  §"Shared kernel".
- These Protocols are the **only** adapter-layer modules a domain
  context may import (the import-linter rule).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/ports.py\`:
  - \`DbSession\` Protocol with \`execute\`, \`flush\`, \`commit\`,
    \`rollback\`, \`add\`, \`get\`, \`scalar\`, \`scalars\` methods
    typed against SQLAlchemy 2.0 \`Session\` signatures.
  - \`UnitOfWork\` Protocol with \`__enter__\`/\`__exit__\` producing a
    \`DbSession\`.
- \`app/adapters/storage/ports.py\`:
  - \`Storage\` Protocol: \`put(hash, stream) -> Blob\`,
    \`get(hash) -> Stream\`, \`exists(hash) -> bool\`,
    \`sign_url(hash, ttl) -> str\`.
- \`app/adapters/mail/ports.py\`:
  - \`Mailer\` Protocol: \`send(to, subject, body, html, headers)\`.
- \`app/adapters/llm/ports.py\`:
  - \`LLMClient\` Protocol: \`complete\`, \`chat\`, \`ocr\`, streaming
    variants; each accepts a \`model_id\` and returns usage stats.
- \`app/util/clock.py\` already has \`Clock\` (see the util task) —
  re-export from \`app/adapters/clock/ports.py\` for uniformity.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every port is a \`Protocol\` (not ABC), \`runtime_checkable\`
      disabled by default
- [ ] \`lint-imports\` (when wired up) allows \`domain.*\` to import
      \`app.adapters.*.ports\` but nothing else under \`adapters\`
- [ ] Unit tests use dummy implementations of each port to prove the
      contract is satisfiable without external services

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_adapter_ports.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(auth/tokens): delegated agent tokens (delegate_for_user_id, short TTL)
description:
## Problem / goal
Agents (§11) act on behalf of a human user. A delegated token inherits
the user's `role_grants` rather than carrying explicit scopes; lifetime
is short by default; revocation is fast.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Delegated tokens",
  §"Per-user agent approval mode" (cross-link to §11).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Delegated authority",
  §"Approval modes".
- Phase 1 table: `agent_token` (delegating_user_id, label, prefix,
  hash, scope_json, expires_at, revoked_at, last_used_at).

## Implementation notes
- Endpoint `POST /api/v1/auth/tokens` (or
  `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens` with `{delegate: true}`) reuses
  the API-token mint path but:
  - Forces `scopes = []` (delegated tokens carry no static scopes).
  - Sets `delegate_for_user_id` from the **session user** — never
    from the request body.
  - Refuses creation when the calling identity is a token (no
    transitive delegation).
  - Default TTL: 30 days, capped by workspace setting.
- Auth middleware: when a delegated token presents, resolve the
  delegating user's `role_grants` for the requested scope and apply
  approval-mode gating (§11). Audit `actor_kind="user"`,
  `actor_id=delegate_for_user_id`, `agent_label=token.label`,
  optional `agent_conversation_ref` from the request header.
- 401 with clear `error="user_archived"` when the delegating user is
  archived or has lost every grant.
- Listing + revoke share the API-token surface; the UI distinguishes
  delegated rows by the `delegate_for_user_id` column.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Delegated token cannot be minted from another token (passkey
      session only)
- [ ] `scopes` always empty on delegated rows
- [ ] Authority follows the user — granting a new role to the user
      affects every live delegated token immediately
- [ ] Approval-mode hooks fire on every mutation
- [ ] Archive of delegating user → 401 on next request
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_agent_token.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_agent_token_authority.py -xvs
```
Manual: mint a delegated token via the dev UI, drive a write through
it, confirm `/approvals` pauses for an annotated route.

title: feat(web): scaffold app/web/ — Vite + React 19 + TS strict (mirror mocks/web)
description:
## Problem / goal
Stand up the production web SPA package `app/web/` so every later web
task has the same toolchain, lint config, and folder layout as
`mocks/web/`. The final app MUST look exactly like the mocks; the
quickest way to guarantee that is to mirror the toolchain first.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Stack", §"Dev/build",
  §"Layout".
- **Architecture**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"web.*",
  §"Repo layout" (places the SPA at `app/web/`).
- **Reference (copy verbatim)**:
  - `mocks/web/package.json`
  - `mocks/web/vite.config.ts`
  - `mocks/web/vitest.config.ts`
  - `mocks/web/tsconfig.json`
  - `mocks/web/tsconfig.app.json`
  - `mocks/web/tsconfig.node.json`
  - `mocks/web/index.html`
  - `mocks/web/eslint.config.js` (if present) or align rules
  - `mocks/web/src/test/setup.ts` (vitest jsdom polyfill for
    `EventSource` — referenced from `vitest.config.ts`
    `setupFiles`)

## Implementation notes
- Create `app/web/` with the same top-level structure as
  `mocks/web/`: `index.html`, `package.json`, `vite.config.ts`,
  `vitest.config.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.app.json`, `src/`,
  `src/test/setup.ts`, `public/`.
- **Copy the config files verbatim** from `mocks/web/`; the only
  intentional diffs are:
  - `package.json#name` → `@crewday/web`.
  - `vite.config.ts` `base` / `build.outDir` left as-is so FastAPI
    can serve `app/web/dist/` (Task `p16.fastapi-static`).
  - Path alias `@` → `src/` matches mocks.
- `package.json` deps mirror `mocks/web/package.json` exactly
  (React 19, TanStack Query v5, vitest, etc.). No Tailwind, no
  Alpine, no HTMX, no Vue.
- `npm install` (or `pnpm` matching mocks) cleanly; `npm run build`
  produces `app/web/dist/`.
- Add an empty `app/web/src/main.tsx` placeholder so the build
  completes; later tasks fill it in.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `app/web/package.json` deps == `mocks/web/package.json` deps
- [ ] `app/web/vite.config.ts` is byte-equal to mocks (modulo
      `base`/`outDir` if needed)
- [ ] `tsconfig.json` is `strict: true` with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`
- [ ] `npm run build` succeeds and emits `app/web/dist/`
- [ ] `eslint` + `tsc --noEmit` pass
- [ ] `npm run test` (vitest) runs a trivial placeholder spec against
      the copied `src/test/setup.ts` without crashing on missing
      `EventSource`

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm ci
cd app/web && npm run build
cd app/web && npm run lint
cd app/web && npx tsc --noEmit
cd app/web && npm run test -- --run
```

title: Self-review: feat(auth/tokens): API token mint / list / revoke (sha256 hash + prefix)

title: test(util): make test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid deterministic
description:
## Problem / goal
`tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid`
is flaky. It flips the last character of a signed token and asserts
`TokenInvalid` is raised, but a single flipped character doesn't always
produce an invalid signature — depending on which character was swapped
and which bit flipped, the resulting token is still cryptographically
valid. Observed failure: ran full suite once, hit `DID NOT RAISE`; re-ran
the single test and it passed.

## Technical context
- File: `tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py` TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid
- Root cause: flipping one URL-safe base64 character `last -> 'A' or 'B'`
  sometimes produces another valid signature output.
- The intent of the test is "tampering is rejected", so the fix is to
  make the tamper deterministic:
  - Option 1: flip every character in a loop, asserting at least one
    flip triggers `TokenInvalid`.
  - Option 2: use `hypothesis` to sample flip positions.
  - Option 3: deliberately mutate the signature bytes (decode the base64
    tail, xor one byte, re-encode).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Test passes deterministically across 100+ runs
- [ ] Test still proves tampering is detected end-to-end

## Test plan
```bash
for i in {1..100}; do uv run pytest tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid -xvs || break; done
```

title: feat(api): per-token / per-IP rate-limit middleware (leaky-bucket)
description:
## Problem / goal
Per-token and per-IP rate limit on every API call. Returns 429 with
\`Retry-After\` per spec §"Rate limiting". v1 ships an in-memory
leaky-bucket per process (sticky session friendly); when running
multi-process, the bucket state is persisted in Postgres
(\`rate_limit_bucket\` table) so quotas survive a worker restart and
are coherent across workers. Deployed Redis-free in v1.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Rate limiting",
  §03 "Personal access tokens" for per-token limits.
- **Mocks consuming**: \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\` retry hook
  honours \`Retry-After\` and surfaces a toast.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/middleware/rate_limit.py\`:
  - Bucket keyed by \`(token_id or ip_hash, route_class)\`.
  - Per-token defaults: 60 req/min standard, 600 req/min
    \`me:*\` PATs; per-IP fallback: 30 req/min.
  - Backend chosen by config: \`memory\` (single process) or
    \`postgres\` (multi-process). Postgres uses an UPSERT with
    advisory lock to keep bucket math atomic.
- Adds \`X-RateLimit-Remaining\` and \`X-RateLimit-Reset\`
  response headers on every call.
- 429 response uses the shared problem+json envelope and
  \`Retry-After\` header (seconds, integer).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] In-memory backend works in tests + single-worker dev
- [ ] Postgres backend correct under 4 concurrent workers
- [ ] 429 with correct \`Retry-After\` after burst
- [ ] PATs scoped \`me:*\` get the higher quota
- [ ] Anonymous (no token) requests fall back to per-IP bucket
- [ ] Header values match the actual remaining tokens

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/middleware/test_rate_limit.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_rate_limit_concurrent.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/manager): stays calendar with iCal feeds + reservation drawer
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager sees the unit calendar across all properties, drills into a reservation, manages the per-property iCal feeds (Airbnb, Booking.com, Vrbo) and the manual booking flow.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/stays` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, StayCalendar, IcalFeedPanel.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/stays, GET /api/v1/properties, GET /api/v1/employees, GET /api/v1/me, POST /api/v1/stays, PATCH /api/v1/stays/{id}, GET /api/v1/ical_feeds.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The stay-task-bundle markers (§06) appear on the calendar; respect their colours and tooltip copy from the mock.
- iCal feed health is shown inline (`last_polled_at`, error chip) — wire to the SSE `ical.poll.completed` stream.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/stays`.

title: feat(web/employee): ChatPage — staff chat channels + composer + log
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker-facing staff chat (§10 messaging; §23 chat-gateway). Renders the channel log via `ChatLog`, accepts new messages via `ChatComposer`, and shows an agent-typing indicator when the staff agent is composing a reply.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `ChatLog`, `ChatComposer` (`@/components/chat/`), `useAgentTyping`. Types: `AgentMessage`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/agent/employee/log` (channel feed); `POST /api/v1/agent/employee/message` (send); SSE channel for typing + new messages.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Preserve optimistic mutation pattern: `onMutate` adds the message client-side, `onError` rolls back, `onSettled` invalidates `qk.agentEmployeeLog`.
- Subscribe to SSE `chat_message.created` and the agent-typing channel.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/ChatPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/ChatPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/ChatPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/ChatPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(billing): rate_card CRUD with service→cents/hr map and active windows
description:
## Problem / goal
Rate cards bind a service catalogue to per-hour pricing for a given
client (or as a workspace default). Multiple cards per org with
non-overlapping active windows; resolution picks the card whose
window contains the work date.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"Billable
  rates" → §"\`client_rate\`", §"Rate resolution".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/rate_cards.py\`:
  - \`RateCardService(ctx).create(org_id, label, currency,
    rates: dict[ServiceKey, int_cents], active_from, active_to)\`
  - \`resolve(org_id, service_key, on=date)\` → cents/hr or 422 if
    no card covers \`on\`.
- API \`app/api/billing/rate_cards.py\`:
  - \`GET/POST /organizations/{org_id}/rate-cards\`
  - \`PATCH /organizations/{org_id}/rate-cards/{id}\`
- Reject overlapping windows for the same (org, service) pair on
  insert/update.
- Audit \`billing.rate_card.created/updated\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Overlap detection across active windows enforced
- [ ] Resolution picks the card by date (boundary inclusive on
      from, exclusive on to)
- [ ] Currency must match the org default unless explicit override
- [ ] Unit tests cover overlap + resolution edge cases
- [ ] API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_rate_cards.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_rate_cards_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: test(tenant): cross-workspace regression matrix on SQLite + PG (404 byte-identical, ±5 ms timing)
description:
## Problem / goal
The single most expensive bug class in a multi-tenant app is a
cross-tenant data leak. This task lands the §17 cross-tenant
regression test matrix: every workspace-scoped HTTP endpoint,
worker job, event subscription, and repository method exercised
against a wrong-workspace context, asserting `404` (never `403`,
never `200`) with a byte-identical envelope and timing bands that
overlap within ±5 ms — on **both** SQLite and Postgres.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Cross-tenant
  regression test" (the four cases: HTTP, jobs, events,
  repositories; surface-parity gate clauses).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Constant-time cross-tenant responses" (the timing + envelope
  invariants this test enforces),
  §"Row-level security (RLS)" (app filter always on; PG adds RLS
  as defence-in-depth).

## Implementation notes
- New test package `tests/tenant/`:
  - `conftest.py` seeds workspaces `A` and `B` with deliberately
    colliding rows (same property names, same emails where
    uniqueness allows) so a wrong-tenant read shows up as a wrong
    row, not just an empty one.
  - `test_http_surface.py` walks every entry in `_surface.json`
    and issues an A-token request against a B-slug path,
    asserting `404` + identical envelope across at least 100
    sample endpoints. Timing bands measured via a `pytest-benchmark`
    helper; ±5 ms tolerance.
  - `test_worker_jobs.py` parametrises over the worker registry
    and asserts every emitted SQL statement on a workspace-scoped
    table carries the `WHERE workspace_id = '<ctx>'` clause via a
    `sqlalchemy.event.listen` hook.
  - `test_event_subscriptions.py` publishes an A-scoped event,
    asserts a B-scoped subscriber does not receive it.
  - `test_repository_parity.py` parametrises over every
    workspace-scoped repository method.
- Test matrix runs on SQLite and on Postgres via `testcontainers`;
  CI runs both. RLS-clearing test (PG only) asserts `SET LOCAL
  crewday.workspace_id = ''` raises on the next query.
- Surface-parity gate: a `tests/tenant/_optouts.py` registry holds
  the explicit deployment-/identity-scope opt-outs with
  `# justification:` comments.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All four cases (HTTP, jobs, events, repositories) implemented
- [ ] Sample of ≥100 HTTP endpoints exercised; envelope
      byte-identical across "missing" and "exists-elsewhere"
- [ ] Timing bands overlap within ±5 ms under a steady-load harness
- [ ] Suite runs on SQLite + PG; both pass in CI
- [ ] RLS clearing on PG raises rather than returns rows
- [ ] Adding a new repository method without extending the suite
      fails the parity gate
- [ ] Opt-outs in `_optouts.py` carry `# justification:` comments

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/tenant/ -xvs
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/tenant/ -xvs
```

title: feat(privacy): user data export, right-to-be-forgotten purge, retention policy worker
description:
## Problem / goal
GDPR-like rights are mandatory because much of the data is
personal: a user can request their own data as a JSON + files
bundle, an owner/manager can purge a person from a workspace
(anonymising free-text and scrubbing routing identifiers while
preserving financial rows), and a daily worker enforces the
spec retention defaults across `audit_log`, `session`, `llm_call`,
`email_delivery`, `webhook_delivery`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Privacy and data
  rights" (export endpoint, erasure command, payout-specific erasure
  steps, retention defaults table), §"Audit log" (purge writes to
  the audit log; archived rows land in
  `$DATA_DIR/archive/<table>.jsonl.gz`).
- **Spec**: §"Payout-specific erasure" — drop `secret_envelope`
  rows, clear `payout_destination` columns, scrub
  `payslip.payout_snapshot_json`, return 410 on subsequent
  payout-manifest requests.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/api/me/export.py`:
  - `POST /api/v1/me/export` queues a worker job, returns 202 +
    poll url. The job builds a JSON document covering every row
    where the user is the actor or the subject, plus referenced
    file blobs zipped under `attachments/`.
  - Email delivery on completion via the §10 notification service.
  - Bundle written to storage and downloadable for 7 days via a
    one-time signed URL (re-using §15 signed-URL plumbing).
- New CLI `crewday admin purge --person <id>`:
  - Anonymises `users.legal_name`, `email`, `phone`.
  - Scrubs free-text fields in tasks, comments, expenses,
    bookings the user authored.
  - Drops referenced `secret_envelope` rows from
    `payout_destination`; clears `display_stub`, `secret_ref_id`,
    `country`, `label`; preserves `id`/`kind`/`currency`/timestamps.
  - Scrubs `payslip.payout_snapshot_json` per spec — keeps amounts
    + dates + destination_id.
  - `POST /payslips/{id}/payout_manifest` returns `410 Gone` for
    affected payslips after purge.
  - Writes `audit.privacy.purge.complete`.
- New worker `rotate_operational_logs` (daily): applies the
  spec retention table per workspace; archives to
  `$DATA_DIR/archive/<table>.jsonl.gz`.
- Retention overrides per workspace via a settings row.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /me/export` produces a complete JSON + file bundle for
      the requester only (cross-user isolation tested)
- [ ] `crewday admin purge` anonymises the user row and scrubs
      free-text fields in dependent tables in a single transaction
- [ ] Payout manifest endpoint returns 410 after purge for
      affected payslips
- [ ] Financial amounts and timestamps survive purge (legal
      retention)
- [ ] Daily worker applies the spec retention to each table on
      both SQLite and PG
- [ ] Archive files are gzipped JSONL with one row per line
- [ ] Audit row written for both export-issued and purge-complete

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/privacy/test_export.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/privacy/test_purge.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/privacy/test_retention_worker.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/cli/test_admin_purge.py -xvs
```

title: feat(assets/asset-type): CRUD + default action catalog
description:
## Problem / goal
Asset types are templates (boiler, pool pump, fridge, vehicle,
linen-set) carrying a default action catalog (e.g. boiler →
[service, descale, replace anode]). Every workspace gets a
pre-seeded base catalog they can fork.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/21-assets.md` §"Asset types (catalog)",
  §"default_actions_json shape", §"Pre-seeded catalog".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/assets/models.py`
  (`AssetType`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetTypesPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/assets/types.py`:
  - `create_type(ctx, slug, name, icon, default_actions=[])`.
  - `update_type(ctx, type_id, **fields)`.
  - `delete_type(ctx, type_id)` — refuses if any `Asset` references
    the type (use soft delete via `archived_at`, not row removal).
  - `list_types(ctx, *, include_archived=False)`.
- Default-action shape (pydantic):
  `[{ "kind": "service", "label": "Annual service",
      "interval_days": 365, "warn_before_days": 30 }, ...]`.
- Workspace bootstrap seeds the §21 base catalog (idempotent).
- Capability `assets.manage_types`.
- REST under `app/api/assets/types.py`.
- Audit on every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD endpoints + audit
- [ ] Default-action JSON validated by pydantic
- [ ] Archive instead of delete when in use
- [ ] Bootstrap seeds the base catalog exactly once per workspace

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_asset_types.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_asset_types_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/employee): TaskDetailPage — evidence, checklist, comments, photo-required gate
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's task-execution surface (§14 worker task detail; §06 occurrences). Shows the task header, property + instructions, checklist items (each with a per-item photo gate), evidence upload, and a per-task chat thread. The Complete CTA is gated when `photo_evidence === 'required'` and no photo has been uploaded.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `Chip`, `Loading`, `AutoGrowTextarea`, `ChatLog`, `ChatComposer`, `Ban`, `Camera`, `Check`, `SkipForward` icons. Types: `AgentMessage`, `Instruction`, `Property`, `Task`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/occurrences/{id}` returning `{ task, property, instructions }`; `POST /api/v1/occurrences/{id}/checklist/{item_id}`; `POST /api/v1/occurrences/{id}/evidence` (multipart photo upload); `POST /api/v1/occurrences/{id}/comments`; `POST /api/v1/occurrences/{id}/complete`; `POST /api/v1/occurrences/{id}/skip`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Photo-required gate is enforced both in the UI (button disabled) **and** server-side in `p5.completion`.
- Evidence uploads must show optimistic preview before the server responds.
- Subscribe to SSE `occurrence.updated` and `comment.added` events; invalidate `qk.occurrence(id)`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(adapters/db): SQLAlchemy 2.0 base + UnitOfWork + Alembic bootstrap
description:
## Problem / goal
Wire the DB adapter: SQLAlchemy 2.0 \`DeclarativeBase\`, session maker,
Unit-of-Work context manager, and Alembic timeline bootstrapped (env.py
gathers per-context models). SQLite and Postgres dialects both work.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters", §"Migrations
  stay shared", \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"Conventions".
- Backend selection is by \`settings.database_url\`; the same code path
  runs on both.
- Capability registry (separate task) sets \`features.rls\` for
  Postgres.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/base.py\`: \`Base = DeclarativeBase\`, shared
  metadata with a naming convention for constraints (required for
  Alembic autogenerate on SQLite).
- \`app/adapters/db/session.py\`: engine factory (connect args per
  dialect — \`check_same_thread=False\` for SQLite, pool defaults for
  PG), \`SessionLocal\` maker, \`UnitOfWorkImpl\` implementing the
  port.
- \`migrations/\` via \`alembic init\`, \`alembic.ini\` pointing at
  \`app.config.settings.database_url\`. \`env.py\` imports every
  per-context \`models\` module (initially empty, will expand as
  contexts land) and passes their metadata to \`context.configure\`.
- No tables yet — Phase 1 tasks add them.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`alembic upgrade head\` is a no-op on an empty DB
- [ ] \`alembic revision --autogenerate -m test\` produces an empty
      diff
- [ ] UoW rolls back on exception, commits on clean exit
- [ ] Works on both \`sqlite:///:memory:\` and a live PG container

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_uow.py -xvs
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m test && rm migrations/versions/*test*
\`\`\`

title: feat(db/identity): user / passkey_credential / session / api_token
description:
## Problem / goal
Identity tables for authentication: users, their passkey credentials,
web sessions, and long-lived API tokens.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"user",
  §"passkey_credential", §"session", §"api_token",
  \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §"Data model".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/identity/models.py\` (extend Phase 1 workspace file):
  - \`User\`: id, email (unique), display_name, locale, timezone,
    avatar_blob_hash, created_at, last_login_at.
  - \`PasskeyCredential\`: id (credential_id bytes), user_id,
    public_key, sign_count, transports, backup_eligible, created_at,
    last_used_at, label.
  - \`Session\`: id, user_id, workspace_id (nullable — users pick a
    workspace post-login), expires_at, last_seen_at, ua_hash, ip_hash.
  - \`ApiToken\`: id, user_id, workspace_id, label, scope_json, prefix
    (first 8 chars of hashed secret, for listing), hash (sha256),
    expires_at, last_used_at, revoked_at.
- Alembic migration \`0004_identity\`.
- Register in tenancy registry: \`session\` / \`api_token\` /
  \`passkey_credential\` are user-scoped but live in the identity
  context — tag them with their workspace via \`user_workspace\` at
  query time; the filter treats them as **user-scoped** (skip
  registration) and the identity module owns its own access rules.
  \`user\` itself is tenant-agnostic (one row globally, many workspace
  memberships).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tables present with indexes on lookup columns
- [ ] Email unique + case-insensitive
- [ ] \`PasskeyCredential.public_key\` stored as bytes, not text
- [ ] Migration reversible
- [ ] Unit tests cover CRUD + uniqueness

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_identity.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/manager): outbound webhook subscriptions with delivery log
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager subscribes external systems to workspace events (task.completed, expense.approved, ical.poll.completed, …) and inspects delivery attempts + retries.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/WebhooksPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/webhooks` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, EventPicker, DeliveryLogDrawer.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/webhooks, POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/webhooks, GET /api/v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/WebhooksPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- HMAC secret is shown once on creation, never afterwards — same pattern as API tokens.
- The event picker uses the same canonical event catalog Phase 12 publishes; do not hardcode the list.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/webhooks`.

title: feat(tasks): evidence upload (photo / voice / note / gps)
description:
## Problem / goal
A `task_evidence` row is how the system proves a task happened — a
photo, a voice note, a free-form note, or a checklist snapshot.
Files travel through the storage port (local disk by default,
S3-compatible later) and the policy cascade decides what is allowed.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Evidence",
  §"Evidence policy inheritance"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"evidence", §"file"; `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` for
  the storage adapter contract.
- Storage port lives in `app/ports/storage.py` (Phase 0
  `p0.storage`). Service must depend on the port, never the
  filesystem directly.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/evidence.py`.
- DTOs: `EvidenceUpload` (kind: `photo | voice | note |
  checklist_snapshot | gps`, `bytes`, `mime`, `text`,
  `gps_lat`, `gps_lon`), `EvidenceView`.
- Functions:
  - `upload_evidence(ctx, task_id, payload) -> EvidenceView` —
    resolves the §06 evidence cascade
    (`workspace → property → unit → work_engagement → task`,
    forbid-first then most-specific). When `forbid`, reject the
    upload with `EvidencePolicyError`. When `require` and no
    eligible kind attached, defer the rejection to the completion
    service (it may still be a partial save before completion).
  - `list_evidence(ctx, task_id)` — ordered by `created_at`.
  - `delete_evidence(ctx, evidence_id)` — soft; only the
    uploader, manager, or owner.
  - `snapshot_checklist(ctx, task_id)` — invoked by the completion
    service; writes one `task_evidence{kind=checklist_snapshot,
    checklist_snapshot_json=...}` row from the current
    `task_checklist_item` rows so reports survive later edits.
- File handling: stream to `storage.put(...)`, capture the
  returned `file_id`; store `task_evidence.file_id`. Compute and
  store `sha256` for dedup.
- Image rotation/EXIF stripping happens server-side for `photo`
  kind (privacy — see §15) before the file is handed to the port.
- GPS-kind rows must include both `gps_lat` and `gps_lon`; the
  service rejects partial coordinates.
- Audit `task_evidence.upload | delete | snapshot`; `task.evidence_added`
  on the bus.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Storage interactions go through the port (mockable in tests)
- [ ] Cascade resolution honoured (forbid first, then most-specific)
- [ ] EXIF strip happens before the put call
- [ ] Snapshot helper captures the live checklist state
- [ ] Audit + event per mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover policy branches and dedup-by-sha256

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_evidence.py -xvs
```

title: feat(api): RFC 7807 problem+json envelope and domain→HTTP exception translation
description:
## Problem / goal
Every error response across every router uses the same
problem+json envelope (RFC 7807) with the canonical \`type\` URIs
listed in spec §12 (\`validation\`, \`not_found\`, \`conflict\`,
\`unauthorized\`, \`forbidden\`, \`rate_limited\`,
\`upstream_unavailable\`, \`idempotency_conflict\`,
\`approval_required\`). Domain exceptions raised by services are
translated to the matching HTTP status without router-by-router
boilerplate.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Errors".
- **Domain exceptions**: a base \`DomainError\` hierarchy in
  \`app/domain/errors.py\` (already conceptualised in Phase 0 ports);
  this task adds the HTTP layer.
- **Mocks consuming**: \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\` parses
  \`problem+json\`; \`mocks/web/src/components/ErrorBanner.tsx\`
  renders the \`title\` + \`detail\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/errors.py\`: registers \`add_exception_handlers(app)\`
  for \`DomainError\` subclasses + \`pydantic.ValidationError\` +
  starlette \`HTTPException\`. Always emits
  \`Content-Type: application/problem+json\`.
- Maps \`NotFound -> 404\`, \`Conflict -> 409\`,
  \`Forbidden -> 403\`, \`Unauthorized -> 401\`,
  \`Validation -> 422\`, \`RateLimited -> 429\`,
  \`UpstreamUnavailable -> 502\`,
  \`ApprovalRequired -> 409\` with \`approval_request_id\` in the
  body.
- Includes \`instance\` (request path), \`type\` URI, \`status\`,
  \`title\`, \`detail\`, optional \`errors[]\` for field-level.
- Echo \`X-Correlation-Id\` on every error response.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every router uses the same envelope without local handlers
- [ ] All canonical \`type\` URIs appear in OpenAPI examples
- [ ] \`pydantic.ValidationError\` maps to 422 with \`errors[]\` array
- [ ] Unknown DomainError subclass falls back to 500 \`internal\`
- [ ] Correlation id propagates from request to error body
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover one example per canonical type

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_errors.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_error_envelope.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/employee): TodayPage — today's occurrences + quick-complete + offline queue
description:
## Problem / goal
Default landing page for workers (§14 worker shell). Lists today's task occurrences grouped by Now / Upcoming / Completed, with quick-complete action and offline-queue support so completions persist when the PWA is offline.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TodayPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TodayPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/TodayPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `TaskListCard`, `NewTaskButton` (NewTaskModal), `Chip`, `EmptyState`, `Loading`, `ProgressBar` from `@/components/common`. `Camera`, `Check` icons from lucide.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me/today` returning `{ now_task, upcoming, completed, properties }`; `POST /api/v1/occurrences/{id}/complete` (with optional photo evidence).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TodayPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/TodayPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Quick-complete must enqueue offline (Service Worker + IndexedDB queue from `p16.pwa-sw`) when the network is down, syncing on reconnect.
- The CTA label depends on task state — keep the `ctaLabel` helper exactly as in the mock.
- Subscribe to SSE `occurrence.updated` events and invalidate `qk.today`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/TodayPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/TodayPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/TodayPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/TodayPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(time/shifts): clock-in / clock-out + manager manual edit
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers open a shift (clock-in) and close it (clock-out); managers can
create or amend shifts after the fact. Shifts feed timesheets and
payroll downstream. Without this the whole §09 stack is dead.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Bookings",
  §"Pay basis (per-engagement)", §"Owner and manager adjustments".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx` (worker
  clock-in/out widget), `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayPage.tsx`
  (timesheet table the edits feed into).
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/time/models.py` (`Shift`).

## Implementation notes
- New service `app/domain/time/shifts.py`:
  - `open_shift(ctx: WorkspaceContext, *, user_id, property_id?,
    source='manual')` — rejects if the user already has an open shift
    (`ends_at IS NULL`).
  - `close_shift(ctx, shift_id, *, ends_at=None)` — defaults to
    `now()`; refuses to close a shift that is not the caller's own
    unless caller has `time.edit_others`.
  - `edit_shift(ctx, shift_id, **fields)` — manager-only; validates
    `ends_at >= starts_at`; writes audit row.
  - `list_open_shifts(ctx, *, user_id?)`.
- REST surface under `app/api/time/`:
  - `POST /api/time/shifts/open`, `POST /api/time/shifts/{id}/close`,
    `PATCH /api/time/shifts/{id}`, `GET /api/time/shifts`.
- Capabilities (Phase 0 caps): `time.clock_self`, `time.edit_others`.
- SSE event `time.shift.changed` on every mutation (consumed by the
  manager pay page for live updates).
- Every mutation calls `audit.write(ctx, 'shift.<verb>', shift_id,
  diff)`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Worker can open exactly one shift at a time
- [ ] Worker can close their own shift; cannot close someone else's
- [ ] Manager can create / amend any shift in the workspace
- [ ] `ends_at < starts_at` rejected with 422
- [ ] Audit row written on every state change
- [ ] SSE `time.shift.changed` fan-out verified
- [ ] OpenAPI regenerated; mock pages still pass type-check

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_shifts_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_shifts_api.py -xvs
# end-to-end against the mock UI
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/time/shifts/open \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <session>' \
  -d '{"property_id":"prop_..."}'
```

title: feat(llm/usage): every call writes llm_call and updates the budget
description:
## Problem / goal
The thin glue that sits between the LLM client (`p0.llm`) and the
budget module: turn a provider response into a persisted
`llm_call` row plus a `budget.record_usage` call. Carries the
correlation id end-to-end so audit, llm_call, and the originating
agent action all join up.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Cost tracking",
  §"Cost tracking — extended", §"Workspace usage budget".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`llm_usage` (a.k.a.
  `llm_call`) — fields `workspace_id`, `capability`,
  `provider_model_id`, `assignment_id`, `prompt_tokens`,
  `completion_tokens`, `cost_cents`, `fallback_attempts`,
  `finish_reason`, `correlation_id`, `actor_user_id`,
  `token_id`, `agent_label`, `at`.
- Cross-ref: §11 "Client abstraction" — the wrapper that this
  module hooks into.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/usage_recorder.py` with
  `record(ctx, request, response, *, capability, model_pick,
  fallback_attempts, correlation_id) -> LlmCallRow`.
- Hooks installed on the LLM client (`p0.llm`) so every successful
  call invokes the recorder; the wrapper also calls
  `budget.check_budget` *before* dispatch and `budget.record_usage`
  *after* a 2xx.
- Captures `finish_reason`, `fallback_attempts` (rung index that
  succeeded), `X-LLM-Fallback-Attempts` echoed to the caller.
- Propagates `correlation_id` from the inbound request
  (`X-Correlation-Id`), generating one if absent and echoing
  `X-Correlation-Id-Echo`.
- On retryable failures the recorder is **not** invoked per attempt
  — only the final outcome (success or terminal error) writes a row.
  `budget_exceeded` writes nothing per the budget spec.
- Retention: `llm_call` kept 90 days (§15); only the first 30
  contribute to the meter — enforced by the budget query, not by
  delete.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Successful call writes one `llm_call` row with non-null
  cost, provider_model, assignment, correlation_id.
- [ ] Failed-then-fallback-success writes one row with
  `fallback_attempts > 0`.
- [ ] Pre-flight refusal writes no row but increments the
  `llm.budget_exceeded` log counter.
- [ ] `X-Correlation-Id-Echo` header round-trips through the
  caller.
- [ ] When the call is delegated-token, `token_id` and
  `agent_label` are populated.
- [ ] A row written by the recorder is visible to
  `budget.check_budget` on the next call (read-after-write).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/test_usage_recorder.py`
  with a fake LLM client.
- Integration test: end-to-end agent turn writes one row whose
  correlation id matches the audit row from the resulting tool
  call.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: sec(http): strict CSP + HSTS + nonce-based scripts + headers middleware
description:
## Problem / goal
Every HTML response must carry the §15 strict CSP and the full
security-header set: HSTS (post-opt-in), `X-Frame-Options`,
`Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`, `X-Content-Type-Options`,
`COOP`, `CORP`. The CSP is nonce-based — every inline `<script>`
or `<style>` carries a per-request nonce; CI fails a build that
emits an unhashed inline.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"HTTP security
  headers" (the canonical list and exact values),
  §"Shared-origin XSS containment" (nonce-based CSP, no inline
  without nonce, `bleach` sanitiser on UGC, `__Host-` cookies),
  §"TLS" (HSTS opt-in flow).
- **Demo carve-out**: §24 `frame-ancestors` allowlist for
  `demo.crew.day` only — the middleware reads
  `caps.has("runtime.demo_mode")` and the
  `CREWDAY_DEMO_FRAME_ANCESTORS` env to widen `frame-ancestors`
  on that surface only.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/api/middleware/security_headers.py`: emits the full
  header set on every response, generates a per-request 128-bit
  nonce, exposes it on `request.state.csp_nonce` for the renderer.
- React app's HTML shell template reads the nonce from a meta tag
  injected by the server; any inline `<script>` (the bootstrap)
  carries `nonce={nonce}`.
- HSTS: emitted only when the `settings.hsts_enabled` workspace
  toggle is on (per spec — opt-in once Caddy/TLS is verified).
- `Permissions-Policy: camera=(self)` only on worker pages —
  middleware reads route group; default elsewhere is empty
  (`camera=()`). `geolocation=()` everywhere (v0 geofence is gone).
- Linter check: scans `mocks/web/dist` post-build for any
  `<script>` lacking `nonce=` or `src=`; fails the build.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every HTML response carries the §15 header set with the exact
      values; asserted in a test that hits 5 representative routes
- [ ] CSP nonce is unique per request and matches the inline
      bootstrap's `nonce` attribute
- [ ] Build-time linter rejects a deliberately-introduced unhashed
      inline `<script>`
- [ ] `frame-ancestors 'none'` on prod surface; widened on demo
      only when env is set
- [ ] HSTS absent until opt-in; present with the spec value once on
- [ ] No CSP report-uri violations on the styleguide route

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_security_headers.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_csp_nonce.py -xvs
```

title: feat(messaging): chat_message send + list + attachments + chat.message.sent event
description:
## Problem / goal
Send a message in a channel, list a channel's history with
keyset pagination, attach blobs (image, file, voice). Publishes
\`chat.message.sent\` so other contexts (LLM agent, chat-gateway
bridge) can react.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"In-app
  messaging" → §"Agent-message delivery".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/components/chat/ChatComposer.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/components/chat/ChatLog.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/messaging/messages.py\`:
  - \`send(channel_id, body_md, attachments=[blob_hash, ...])\`
  - \`list(channel_id, before=None, limit=50)\` keyset by \`created_at,
    id\`.
- API \`app/api/messaging/messages.py\`:
  - \`POST /chat/channels/{id}/messages\`
  - \`GET /chat/channels/{id}/messages?before=...\`
- Markdown sanitised (allow basic formatting + autolink, strip raw
  HTML / scripts).
- Emits \`chat.message.sent\` via Phase 0 event bus with
  \`{channel_id, message_id, author_user_id, channel_kind}\` payload.
- SSE \`chat.message.created\` invalidates the channel's TanStack
  query for connected clients.
- Audit \`messaging.message.sent\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Message persists with author + ordering preserved across two
      same-millisecond sends (id tiebreak)
- [ ] Attachments must already exist in the storage layer (FK by
      blob_hash); else 404
- [ ] Markdown sanitiser strips \`<script>\` etc. (test included)
- [ ] \`chat.message.sent\` fires exactly once per send
- [ ] Unit + API + event-bus tests

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_chat_message.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/messaging/test_chat_message_api.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_chat_message_event.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(billing): quote CRUD + send (mailer) + accept/reject
description:
## Problem / goal
Draft, send, and track acceptance of a quote to a client. Sending
emails the client a tokenised acceptance link; accept/reject
toggle status and timestamp.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"\`quote\`",
  §"\`lines_json\` shape", §"Acceptance",
  §"Supersession and rejection".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/quotes.py\`:
  \`QuoteService(ctx)\` with
  \`create / update / send / accept / reject / supersede\`.
- \`send\`: render Jinja template, mail via Phase 0 mailer with
  HTML + plain-text parts; embed signed accept token (HMAC, 30-day
  expiry) targeting \`/q/{id}?token=...\`.
- Public accept/reject endpoint validates token + idempotently
  updates status; logs a \`billing.quote.accepted/rejected\` audit
  with \`actor_kind=guest_token\`.
- \`supersede\`: cloning quote points back via
  \`superseded_by_quote_id\`.
- Editing locked once \`status != draft\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cannot edit a sent quote; must \`supersede\`
- [ ] Token verifier rejects expired / tampered tokens (test)
- [ ] One email per send; resend allowed and audited
- [ ] Public accept/reject works without a logged-in session

title: feat(web/employee): MyExpensesPage — create, OCR-autofill receipts, list claims
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's expense-claim surface (§09 expenses). Upload a receipt photo, OCR populates the form fields, the worker reviews and submits. Lists past claims with status chips; tracks pending reimbursements.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `AutoGrowTextarea`, `Chip`, `Loading`, `Search` icon, `formatMoney`, `fmtDate`. Types: `Expense`, `ExpenseCategory`, `ExpenseScanResult`, `ExpenseStatus`, `PendingReimbursement`. ScanPhase state machine: `upload | processing | review | submitted`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me/expenses`; `POST /api/v1/me/expenses/scan` (multipart -> OCR result); `POST /api/v1/me/expenses` (create); `GET /api/v1/me/expenses/pending-reimbursements`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Preserve the `ScanPhase` state machine exactly — visual feedback is tied to it.
- OCR call is async (LLM); show the processing spinner.
- Preserve `STATUS_TONE` mapping for chip colours.
- The mock is ~450 lines — **split it** during promotion into a
  tree under `app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/`, keeping
  `MyExpensesPage.tsx` itself under ~200 lines. Suggested split:
  - `SubmitExpenseForm.tsx` — "Submit an expense" form (mock l.218).
  - `ReceiptScanPanel.tsx` — OCR / ScanPhase state machine + preview.
  - `OwedToYou.tsx` — "Owed to you" reimbursement section (mock l.387).
  - `RecentExpenses.tsx` — "My recent expenses" list (mock l.407).
  - `lib/expenseHelpers.ts` — `confidenceClass`, `STATUS_TONE`,
    `CATEGORIES`.
  - `MyExpensesPage.tsx` — orchestrator: query wiring, SSE, layout.
  Rendered DOM + class names must remain byte-equivalent to the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~200 lines.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(api/instructions): KB CRUD + versions + scope query
description:
## Problem / goal
Instructions KB router: CRUD for instructions, version history,
and the scope-resolution query that the worker app calls to fetch
"all instructions visible at this template/property/area/asset/
stay/role" in one request.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Instructions",
  \`docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md\` §"Scope resolution".
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/TaskDetailPage.tsx\` (sidebar
  fetches by scope), \`mocks/web/src/components/ScopeBadge.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/instructions.py\`: APIRouter with tag
  \`instructions\`.
- \`POST /instructions\` creates v1; \`PATCH /instructions/{id}\`
  bumps version (server-managed, monotonic).
- \`GET /instructions/{id}/versions\` lists history;
  \`GET /instructions/{id}/versions/{n}\` returns a specific one.
- \`GET /instructions:scope?template=...&property=...&area=...&
  asset=...&stay=...&role=...\` runs the scope-resolution
  algorithm and returns the merged list.
- All listings cursor-paginated.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD + versions endpoints with proper schemas
- [ ] PATCH bumps version, never overwrites in place
- [ ] Scope query resolves the union per spec rules
- [ ] Slug uniqueness enforced per workspace; collision → 409
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`instructions\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_instructions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_instructions_scope.py -xvs
\`\`\`
