title: feat(demo): null mailer, LLM cap, demo_gc cron, abuse rate limits
description:
## Problem / goal
Demo is an unauthenticated surface; it will be scraped, fuzzed,
and used to burn LLM budget. This task wires every guardrail
required for safe public exposure: outbound integrations bound to
no-op adapters, LLM capabilities clamped to a tight budget +
free-tier models, and a 15-min `demo_gc` worker that purges
expired workspaces.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/24-demo-mode.md` §"Disabled integrations"
  table (SMTP, webhooks, iCal, passkeys, magic links, OCR, voice,
  digest, anomaly), §"LLM capability scope" (only
  `chat.manager`/`chat.employee`/`chat.compact`/translate/
  `tasks.nl_intake` run live, on `:free` models),
  §"Abuse controls" (10 mints/IP/h, 60 writes/WS/min, 10 turns/WS/min,
  5 MiB/file, 25 MiB/IP/day, 32 KiB text cap),
  §"Garbage collection" (`demo_gc` every 15 min + nightly orphan
  check).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe C →
  GC and scheduled jobs on demo" for the disabled-jobs list.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/config/demo.py`: declarative integration map (capability
  → null-impl). Asserted at boot — every disabled capability has a
  no-op adapter wired or a `501 Not Implemented in demo` route.
- Mailer null-impl writes `email_delivery` rows with
  `suppressed=true` so the UI continues to render "Email sent".
- Webhook delivery short-circuits to
  `webhook_delivery.status='suppressed_demo'`.
- LLM router (Phase 12) reads a demo-mode override pinning model
  suffixes to `:free` and applying the per-WS rolling cap
  (`$0.10/30d` default) and the global daily cap
  `CREWDAY_DEMO_GLOBAL_DAILY_USD_CAP` (default `$5`).
- New worker `demo_gc` (15-min cadence) and `demo_usage_rollup`
  (60 s) registered in the worker registry; on demo, disable
  `ical_poll`, `digest_compose`, `anomaly_detect`, `email_retry`,
  `webhook_retry`, `sync_llm_pricing` per spec.
- Edge rate-limit middleware (mint throttle, mutation rate, LLM
  rate, upload cap, payload cap) configurable via env.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every entry in §"Disabled integrations" returns `501` or the
      documented stub success
- [ ] `chat.manager` runs on a `:free` model under demo
- [ ] Hitting the per-WS LLM cap returns the §"At-cap behaviour"
      response
- [ ] `demo_gc` purges a workspace whose `expires_at < now()` and
      every `workspace_id`-keyed row + uploads dir
- [ ] Mint throttle (10/IP/h) returns 429 on the 11th attempt
- [ ] Nightly orphan-row check reports 0 orphans on a clean run
- [ ] Demo deployment never sends a real email regardless of
      `SMTP_*` env

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/demo/test_disabled_integrations.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/demo/test_abuse_controls.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/demo/test_gc.py -xvs
```

title: feat(messaging): notification service with multi-channel fanout
description:
## Problem / goal
Single \`NotificationService\` that creates a \`Notification\` row and
fans out to (web inbox, email, web push, SSE). Reads a \`kind →
template\` map so callers don't repeat per-channel formatting.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Channels",
  §"Email" → §"Template system", §"In-app messaging", §"Agent-message
  delivery".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/components/ChatChannelsMeCard.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx\` (consumer surfaces).

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/messaging/notifications.py\`:
  \`NotificationService(ctx: WorkspaceContext)\` with
  \`notify(recipient_user_id, kind, payload)\`.
- Persist a \`notification\` row (Phase 1 table), then dispatch to:
  - **inbox**: row already persisted, SSE event \`notification.created\`
    so the web client invalidates \`['notifications','unread']\`.
  - **email**: render template via Phase 0 mailer, respect
    \`email_opt_out\`.
  - **push**: enqueue web-push job (see \`p10.web-push.worker\`).
- Templates in \`app/domain/messaging/templates/<kind>.{subject,body_md,push}.j2\`.
  Lookup by \`kind\` enum; fail loudly on missing template.
- Honour the user's \`locale\` for subject/body rendering.
- Audit every dispatch (\`messaging.notification.dispatched\`,
  channel, kind, recipient).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`notify(...)\` writes one \`notification\` row + emits exactly
      one SSE event per recipient
- [ ] Email path skipped when recipient has matching
      \`email_opt_out\` row
- [ ] Push path enqueued only when recipient has \>=1 active
      \`push_token\`
- [ ] Missing template raises \`TemplateNotFound\` (no silent skip)
- [ ] Unit tests cover all four channels with one fanout call
- [ ] Integration test: end-to-end via SSE client subscriber

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_notification_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_notification_fanout.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/sse): EventSource('/w/<slug>/events') → queryClient.invalidateQueries by event kind
description:
## Problem / goal
A single `EventSource('/w/<slug>/events')` per tab feeds
`queryClient.invalidateQueries()` so cross-client coherence works
without polling. Each event kind maps deterministically to one or
more query keys.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"SSE-driven
  invalidation", §"Event kinds".
- **API**: `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"SSE stream".
- **Reference**: `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts` + Phase 12 SSE backend.

## Implementation notes
- Extend `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts` (copied from mocks) with the
  invalidation map:
  ```ts
  const INVALIDATIONS: Record<EventKind, (e: Event, q: QueryClient) => void> = {
    'task.updated':       (e, q) => q.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: keys.tasks.all }),
    'task.created':       ...,
    'shift.changed':      ...,
    'expense.decided':    ...,
    'inventory.changed':  ...,
    'workspace.changed':  (e, q) => q.invalidateQueries(),
    // ... full table per spec §"Event kinds"
  }
  ```
- The `SseContext` provider runs `INVALIDATIONS[event.kind]?(...)`
  on every message; unknown kinds log + ignore (do NOT crash).
- Reconnect with exponential backoff (1s → 30s cap), preserve
  `Last-Event-Id` so we replay missed events on resume.
- `queryClient.invalidateQueries` is fired with `refetchType:
  'active'` to keep idle queries cheap.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every event kind in spec §"Event kinds" has an invalidation
- [ ] Unknown kinds logged but never throw
- [ ] Backoff caps at 30s; `Last-Event-Id` honoured on resume
- [ ] Workspace switch closes + reopens the stream
- [ ] Unit tests cover invalidation map + reconnect logic

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/lib/sse
# Manual: open two tabs, mutate in one, watch the other update
```

title: feat(messaging): web push delivery worker with retry + stale-token cleanup
description:
## Problem / goal
APScheduler worker that turns queued \`Notification\` rows into actual
web-push deliveries to every active \`push_token\`. Retries with
exponential backoff; deletes tokens the provider tells us are gone.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Channels"
  (web push), §"Delivery tracking".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/workers/messaging/web_push.py\`:
  - Polls \`notification_push_queue\` (small staging table) or
    consumes the in-process job queue from \`NotificationService\`.
  - Sends via \`pywebpush\` with VAPID keys from workspace settings.
  - Backoff: 30s, 2m, 10m, 1h; drop after 5 attempts.
  - On \`410 Gone\` / \`404\` from the provider, delete the
    \`push_token\` and audit \`messaging.push.token_purged\`.
  - Updates \`push_token.last_used_at\` on success.
- Concurrency: per-workspace fan-out, bounded (max 10 concurrent
  HTTP calls per worker tick) to avoid thundering herd.
- Restart safety: take a row-level lock on the queue row so a
  second worker doesn't double-send.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Successful deliveries marked + \`last_used_at\` bumped
- [ ] 410/404 deletes the token, audited
- [ ] Backoff respected; 5xx retried, 4xx (other than 404/410)
      logged + dropped
- [ ] No double-send under two-worker race (locking covered by
      test)
- [ ] Unit tests with a mocked pywebpush; one integration test
      using a fake endpoint server

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_web_push_worker.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_web_push_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/places): unit service — CRUD scoped to a property
description:
## Problem / goal
Unit CRUD with the spec invariant that every property always has at
least one unit; single-unit properties auto-create a default unit at
property creation time and the UI hides the unit layer.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Unit",
  §"Invariants", §"Welcome overrides merge".
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"unit".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Units tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (UNITS list).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/unit_service.py\` — \`create_unit\`,
  \`update_unit\`, \`soft_delete_unit\`, \`list_units\`,
  \`get_unit\`.
- Hook into \`property_service.create_property\` to create the default
  unit (\`name = property.name\`, \`ordinal = 0\`).
- Enforce \`UNIQUE(workspace_id, property_id, name)\` at the service
  layer with a clear \`UnitNameTaken\` error before the DB integrity
  error fires.
- Refuse to delete the last remaining unit of a property
  (\`LastUnitProtected\`).
- Audit every mutation; \`WorkspaceContext\` required.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Property creation auto-creates one default unit
- [ ] Cannot delete the last unit (returns \`LastUnitProtected\`)
- [ ] Duplicate unit name in same property is rejected
- [ ] Cross-workspace access is denied
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Unit + integration tests pass

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_unit_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_unit_crud.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/client): InvoicesPage — vendor invoices + upload proof of payment
description:
## Problem / goal
Clients see vendor invoices billed to one of their binding orgs —
status, amount, due date — and can upload proof-of-payment files
once an invoice is approved. Clients cannot mark anything paid
themselves; the workspace owns that flag.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell"
  → Invoices; §22 clients-and-vendors §"Vendor invoices" +
  §"Proof of payment".
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/InvoicesPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`; `formatMoney`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`,
  `GET /api/v1/vendor_invoices?client_org_id=<oid>`,
  `POST /api/v1/vendor_invoices/{id}/proof_of_payment` (multipart
  body, appends to `proof_of_payment_file_ids`).
- **Types**: `Me`, `Property`, `VendorInvoice`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/client/InvoicesPage.tsx`.
- Upload control only renders when invoice `status == 'approved'`
  per spec — preserve the mock's inline file picker (drag + click).
- On upload success, optimistically append the file id to the
  invoice's `proof_of_payment_file_ids` and `qc.invalidateQueries`.
- SSE `vendor_invoice.changed` invalidates the list.
- No "mark as paid" affordance — assert in tests.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Lists invoices for binding-org clients only; never any
      others.
- [ ] Upload-proof works on `approved` rows; absent everywhere else.
- [ ] No mark-paid control anywhere on the page.
- [ ] Permission gate: client role.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_client_invoices.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/client-invoices.spec.ts
```

title: feat(api/messaging): notifications, push tokens, digests, chat channels and messages
description:
## Problem / goal
Messaging router — notifications (read state, list), push tokens
(register / list / delete), daily/weekly digests, and chat
channels and messages (including the chat-gateway inbound surface).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Messaging",
  \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\`,
  \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\`.
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/NotificationsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/components/NotificationBell.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/team/ChatPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/team/ChannelDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/SecurityPage.tsx\` (push tokens).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/messaging.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`messaging\`.
- Push token POST may return 501 \`push_unavailable\` per spec
  §12 until deployment FCM/APNS is provisioned; GET and DELETE
  always live.
- Notification \`PATCH /notifications/{id}\` flips \`read_at\`;
  bulk \`POST /notifications:mark-read\` accepts an array.
- Chat channel + message CRUD; messages stream in via SSE
  (consumed elsewhere, not this router) but are POSTable here.
- Chat-gateway inbound: \`POST /chat-gateway/inbound\` accepts
  signed webhooks (HMAC) — not workspace-scoped, runs at the
  deployment level (mounted on the deployment-root router but
  schema-defined here for completeness).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD + mark-read routes for notifications
- [ ] Push token POST returns 501 when feature off; GET/DELETE
      always work
- [ ] Chat message POST emits SSE \`chat_message.upserted\`
- [ ] Chat-gateway inbound validates HMAC; bad sig → 401
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`messaging\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_messaging.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_messaging_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/admin): AdminsPage — admin team list + promote / revoke
description:
## Problem / goal
Manage the deployment-admin team: invite a user as admin (optionally
as owner), revoke an admin grant, see who is owner vs admin. The
operator is the only path to a healthy admin team — this is where
they live.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/admins` — admin-team membership, deployment permission
  rules.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AdminsPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Checkbox`, `Chip`, `Loading`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /admin/api/v1/me`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/admins`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/admins` (body: `{ email, as_owner }`),
  `DELETE /admin/api/v1/admins/{id}`.
- **Types**: `AdminMe`, `AdminTeamMember`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/admin/AdminsPage.tsx`.
- Replace fixtures with TanStack Query + `useMutation`; invalidate
  `qk.adminAdmins()` on success.
- Server enforces "at least one owner" invariant on revoke / demote
  (§4); surface the API's 409 message inline (no toast).
- Wire SSE invalidation on `admin.admins.changed`.
- Preserve every label, the "you" chip on the current user, and the
  disabled-self-revoke behaviour from the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Lists members with role badge identical to the mock.
- [ ] Inviting a new admin sends `POST` and prepends the row.
- [ ] Revoke is disabled for the current user (you can't revoke
      yourself); confirm dialog matches mock copy.
- [ ] Last-owner protection 409 surfaces inline with mock styling.
- [ ] Permission gate: only admins can reach this; non-admins get
      the standard not-authorised card.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes at desktop breakpoint.

## Test plan

```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_admins.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-admins.spec.ts
```

title: feat(inventory): task.completed hook emits negative-delta consumption
description:
## Problem / goal
When a task whose template carries `inventory_consumption_json` is
completed, the system should emit one negative `consume` movement
per item in that map, attributing the movement to the completing
user and the source task. Failure to consume must NOT fail the task
completion (per spec §"Consumption on task completion": "soft
coupling").

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"Consumption on task
  completion", §"Asset action consumption"; settings cascade key
  `inventory.consume_on_task` resolved against the completing user
  (per §02 settings cascade).
- Event: `task.completed` from `p5.completion`; consumed deltas via
  the movement service from `p7.movement`.

## Implementation notes
- Subscriber `app/services/inventory/consumption_subscriber.py`
  on `task.completed`:
  1. Resolve `inventory.consume_on_task` setting for the
     completing user (via the cascade resolver — re-use, do NOT
     re-implement).
  2. If false, return.
  3. Read `task.template.inventory_consumption_json`; for each
     `(item_id, qty)`, call
     `movement_service.consume(item_id, -qty, source_task_id)`.
  4. Wrap each in its own try/except — log + emit
     `inventory.consumption_failed` audit on failure, never raise
     (soft coupling).
- Negative `on_hand` permitted; downstream digest surfaces it
  (`inventory.stock_drift` event handled by `p7.reorder` /
  notifications, not here).
- Asset-action consumption (per spec §"Asset action consumption")
  flows through the same path because asset actions copy
  `inventory_consumption_json` onto generated task templates —
  no separate code path needed; verify in test.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Task completion with non-empty consumption map emits one
      movement per entry; `source_task_id` set.
- [ ] Consumption disabled by setting → no movements.
- [ ] Movement service failure on one item does NOT block the
      others nor raise to the completion event.
- [ ] Items not in the workspace silently skipped + audit warned.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: enabled / disabled, partial failure,
      asset-action-derived template path.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_task_consumption.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_inventory_task_consumption_event.py -xvs
```

title: feat(security): public-interface bind guard
description:
## Problem / goal
Server MUST refuse to bind to a public interface unless explicitly
allowed. Default bind is \`127.0.0.1:8000\`; tailscale* interfaces pass;
\`0.0.0.0\` / public IPs require \`CREWDAY_ALLOW_PUBLIC_BIND=1\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants" #6,
  \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Bind guard",
  \`docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md\` §"Bind model".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/security/bind_guard.py\`: \`assert_bind_allowed(host, port,
  trusted_globs, allow_public)\` reads
  \`/proc/net/dev\` + \`netifaces\` to enumerate interfaces, matches
  host IP to an interface, checks the interface name against
  \`trusted_globs\`. Loopback always passes. \`0.0.0.0\` / \`::\`
  require \`allow_public\`.
- Called from \`create_app()\` before Uvicorn binds; raises
  \`BindGuardError\` with a clear message.
- CLI: \`crewday serve\` exposes the same guard.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Binding to \`127.0.0.1\` always passes
- [ ] Binding to a tailscale IP passes (integration test mocks
      interfaces)
- [ ] Binding to \`0.0.0.0\` fails unless \`allow_public\` is True
- [ ] Binding to a public IP without \`allow_public\` fails
- [ ] Clear error message: "refusing to bind to public interface eth0
      (IP ...). Set CREWDAY_ALLOW_PUBLIC_BIND=1 to override."
- [ ] Unit tests cover every branch

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_bind_guard.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(tasks): manager approval service for completed tasks
description:
## Problem / goal
Some templates flag completed tasks for manager review (e.g. a
deep clean before a high-value stay). The approval service holds
the `pending_review` state machine and exposes
approve / reject / request-changes verbs.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Completing
  a task" (approval gate behind `template.required_approval` /
  resolved-setting `tasks.required_approval`);
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"task" (the `state` enum holds
  `completed` — `pending_review` lives on a sibling
  `task_approval` row, since completion is terminal in §06).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx`
  enumerates the queue and exposes the three verbs.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/approvals.py`.
- New row `task_approval`:
  - `id`, `workspace_id`, `task_id` (unique partial when
    `state IN ('pending', 'changes_requested')`),
    `requested_at`, `requested_by_user_id`,
    `state` enum (`pending | approved | rejected |
    changes_requested`), `decided_at`, `decided_by_user_id`,
    `note_md`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- Functions:
  - `request_review(ctx, task_id)` — invoked from
    `p5.completion.complete()` when the resolved setting
    `tasks.required_approval = true`. Inserts a `pending` row
    and emits `task.approval_requested`.
  - `approve(ctx, approval_id, *, note_md)` — owners/managers
    only; sets `state = 'approved'`, `decided_at`,
    `decided_by_user_id`; emits `task.approved`.
  - `reject(ctx, approval_id, *, note_md)` — sets
    `state = 'rejected'`; emits `task.rejected`. Worker is told
    via the `task.rejected` notification that the task needs
    redoing; the task itself stays `completed` (the reject is a
    review judgment, not a re-open — re-opens are out of scope
    of v1).
  - `request_changes(ctx, approval_id, *, note_md)` — sets
    `state = 'changes_requested'`; emits
    `task.changes_requested`. Worker may post evidence /
    comments; manager re-decides via `approve` / `reject`.
- Permission: the route layer (Phase 12) checks
  `tasks.review.decide` on the deciding user; assert here too
  for defence-in-depth.
- `WorkspaceContext` everywhere; one audit row per mutation.
- Event payloads carry `task_id`, `approval_id`,
  `decided_by_user_id`, `state`, `note_md`.
- The approvals queue read used by `ApprovalsPage`:
  `list_pending(ctx, *, property_id=None) -> list[ApprovalView]`
  joining `task_approval` + `task` + `template` for the surface
  fields the manager needs (title, property, completed_by,
  completed_at, evidence count).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Partial-unique constraint prevents two open reviews on the
      same task
- [ ] Three verbs each emit the right event + audit row
- [ ] Permission asserts present; tests cover the unauthorised
      branch
- [ ] `request_review` called automatically from the completion
      service when the resolved setting is on
- [ ] Unit tests cover the state machine and read path

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_approvals.py -xvs
```

title: feat(api): idempotency-key middleware with persisted replay cache
description:
## Problem / goal
Every \`POST\` mutating endpoint accepts \`Idempotency-Key\`. The
middleware persists \`(token_id, key) -> (status, body_hash, body)\`
for 24h and replays the stored response on retry. Different body hash
with the same key returns 409 \`idempotency_conflict\`. Exempt
endpoints (e.g. \`POST /payslips/{id}/payout_manifest\`, §11) skip the
cache but still accept the header.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Idempotency".
- **Domain model**: a new \`idempotency_key\` table —
  \`(token_id, key, status, body_hash, body, created_at)\`, TTL 24h.
- **Mocks consuming**: every mutation in \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\`
  generates an Idempotency-Key (ULID) — the production API must
  honour them on the first network retry.

## Implementation notes
- New Alembic migration \`xxxx_idempotency_key\` (small table, indexed
  on \`(token_id, key)\` UNIQUE, partial index on \`created_at\` for
  TTL sweep).
- \`app/api/middleware/idempotency.py\`: ASGI middleware. Runs after
  auth (so \`token_id\` is known) and before the handler. Skips
  non-POST and an explicit allow-list of exempt paths.
- TTL sweep job registered with the Phase 0 scheduler — daily, deletes
  rows where \`created_at < now() - 24h\`.
- Body hash uses sha256 of the canonical JSON serialisation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] First POST with a key persists the response; second POST with
      same key + same body returns the cached response (and the
      \`Idempotency-Replay: true\` response header)
- [ ] Same key + different body returns 409 \`idempotency_conflict\`
- [ ] Exempt path bypasses cache; header is accepted but ignored
- [ ] TTL sweep removes rows older than 24h
- [ ] Works under concurrent retries (DB-level uniqueness wins races)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover all four paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/middleware/test_idempotency.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_idempotency_replay.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/manager): instructions library with scope filter (workspace / property / role)
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager curates the instruction library that the agent and workers consult — filter by scope (workspace, property, role) and hop into one to edit.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/instructions` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/instructions, GET /api/v1/properties.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- Scope filter is URL-driven (`?scope=property&scope_id=…`) so links from the agent are deep-linkable.
- Search is client-side over the loaded list; do not add a server endpoint.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/instructions`.

title: feat(authz/groups): permission group CRUD + members + system-group protection
description:
## Problem / goal
Permission groups are the deployable unit of authority. Owners + managers
must be able to define custom groups, add/remove members, and never be
able to delete or rename the system groups.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md`
  §"Permissions: surface, groups, and action catalog",
  §"Action catalog".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group",
  §"permission_group_member".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/team/PermissionGroups.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `permission_group`, `permission_group_member`.

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/identity/permission_groups.py`:
  - `list(ctx)`, `get(ctx, group_id)`, `create(ctx, slug, name,
    capabilities)`, `update(ctx, group_id, …)`, `delete(ctx, group_id)`.
  - `add_member(ctx, group_id, user_id)` /
    `remove_member(ctx, group_id, user_id)`.
- System groups (`owners`, `managers`, `workers`, `clients`) are
  flagged `system=True`. Mutations refuse: `delete` → 409
  `system_group_protected`, `update` allows only `name` (slug +
  capabilities frozen), `members` are mutable except the
  last-owner rule (defer to `p2.owners-group`).
- `capabilities_json` validated against the action catalog
  (§05); unknown capability → 422 `unknown_capability`.
- Routes mirror the methods at `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE
  /w/<slug>/api/v1/permission-groups[/{id}[/members]]`.
- Audit on every mutation
  (`audit.permission_group.{created|updated|deleted}` and
  `…member_added|member_removed`).
- Capability gate: `permission_groups.manage` (default: owners only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] System groups cannot be deleted; slug + capabilities cannot
      change
- [ ] `capabilities_json` validated against the action catalog
- [ ] Member add / remove writes audit
- [ ] Capability gate enforced; non-owner mutation → 403
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_permission_groups.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_permission_groups_routes.py -xvs
```
Manual: drive `/team/groups` in dev; attempt to delete `owners@<ws>`,
confirm 409.

title: feat(web/client): QuotesPage — quote list + accept / reject (approval-gated)
description:
## Problem / goal
Clients see quotes their agency has sent for work on their
properties. Acceptance triggers a work order; rejection closes the
quote. In production the click is approval-gated (§22); the mock
applies the change in-memory and emits the SSE event for parity.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell"
  → Quotes; §22 clients-and-vendors §"Quotes" §"Acceptance is
  approval-gated".
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`; `formatMoney`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`,
  `GET /api/v1/work_orders?client_org_id=<oid>`,
  `GET /api/v1/quotes?client_org_id=<oid>`,
  `POST /api/v1/quotes/{id}/accept` (server creates an
  `approval_request`),
  `POST /api/v1/quotes/{id}/reject`.
- **Types**: `Me`, `Property`, `Quote`, `WorkOrder`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx`.
- Accept POST returns either `{ status: 'pending_approval',
  approval_request_id }` (production) or `{ status: 'accepted',
  work_order_id }` (mock / approval-bypass dev mode). Render the
  pending state per the mock (a chip + waiting copy).
- SSE `quote.changed`, `approval_request.changed` invalidate the
  list; the chip updates when an approver acts.
- Preserve the mock's accept / reject confirmation copy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Lists quotes for binding-org clients only.
- [ ] Accept enqueues an approval request and shows pending chip.
- [ ] Reject closes the quote with an audit entry.
- [ ] Permission gate: client role.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_client_quotes.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/client-quotes.spec.ts
```

title: feat(web/client): PortfolioPage — properties scoped to client's binding orgs
description:
## Problem / goal
The client (a property owner whose home is run by an agency) needs
a read-only directory of every property they own that the agency
manages — with closures, current stay, and an "open agency
workspace" link.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell"
  (around `/w/<slug>/properties` under `ClientLayout`); §22
  clients-and-vendors §"Client portal".
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/PortfolioPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`,
  `GET /api/v1/organizations`,
  `GET /api/v1/properties`,
  `GET /api/v1/workspaces`,
  `GET /api/v1/property_workspaces`,
  `GET /api/v1/stays`.
- **Types**: `Me` (incl. `client_binding_org_ids`), `Organization`,
  `Property`, `PropertyClosure`, `PropertyWorkspace`, `Stay`,
  `Workspace`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/client/PortfolioPage.tsx`.
- Filter is `properties.where(client_org_id IN
  me.client_binding_org_ids)`. Server-side scoping is the source
  of truth; the client is a defence-in-depth filter only.
- Render closures + current-stay chip with mock copy and tones.
- Wire SSE `property.changed`, `stay.changed`, `closure.changed`
  to invalidate `qk.properties()`, `qk.stays()` resp.
- Preserve the read-only stance: no edit affordances.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Lists only properties the user is bound to via
      `client_binding_org_ids`; nothing else leaks.
- [ ] Closures + current-stay chip render identical to mock.
- [ ] SSE keeps the list live (closure added → chip updates).
- [ ] Non-client (e.g. plain employee) gets the not-authorised card.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_client_portfolio.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/client-portfolio.spec.ts
```
